     1	// ── Shared Types ──
     2	
     3	// ── Config ──
     4	
     5	export interface MexConfig {
     6	  /** Absolute path to project root (where .git lives) */
     7	  projectRoot: string;
     8	  /** Absolute path to scaffold root (.mex/ directory) */
     9	  scaffoldRoot: string;
    10	}
    11	
    12	// ── Claims (extracted from markdown) ──
    13	
    14	export type ClaimKind = "path" | "command" | "dependency" | "version";
    15	
    16	export interface Claim {
    17	  kind: ClaimKind;
    18	  value: string;
    19	  /** Source file (relative to project root) */
    20	  source: string;
    21	  /** Line number in source file */
    22	  line: number;
    23	  /** Section heading the claim was found under */
    24	  section: string | null;
    25	  /** If true, this claim is negated (e.g. "does NOT use X") */
    26	  negated: boolean;
    27	}
    28	
    29	// ── Drift ──
    30	
    31	export type Severity = "error" | "warning" | "info";
    32	
    33	export type IssueCode =
    34	  | "STALE_FILE"
    35	  | "MISSING_PATH"
    36	  | "DEAD_COMMAND"
    37	  | "DEPENDENCY_MISSING"
    38	  | "VERSION_MISMATCH"
    39	  | "CROSS_FILE_CONFLICT"
    40	  | "DEAD_EDGE"
    41	  | "INDEX_MISSING_ENTRY"
    42	  | "INDEX_ORPHAN_ENTRY"
    43	  | "UNDOCUMENTED_SCRIPT";
    44	
    45	export interface DriftIssue {
    46	  code: IssueCode;
    47	  severity: Severity;
    48	  file: string;
    49	  line: number | null;
    50	  message: string;
    51	  /** The claim that triggered this issue, if any */
    52	  claim?: Claim;
    53	}
    54	
    55	export interface DriftReport {
    56	  score: number;
    57	  issues: DriftIssue[];
    58	  filesChecked: number;
    59	  timestamp: string;
    60	}
    61	
    62	// ── Frontmatter ──
    63	
    64	export interface ScaffoldFrontmatter {
    65	  name?: string;
    66	  description?: string;
    67	  edges?: FrontmatterEdge[];
    68	  last_updated?: string;
    69	  [key: string]: unknown;
    70	}
    71	
    72	export interface FrontmatterEdge {
    73	  target: string;
    74	  condition?: string;
    75	}
    76	
    77	// ── Scanner ──
    78	
    79	export interface ManifestInfo {
    80	  type: "package.json" | "pyproject.toml" | "go.mod" | "Cargo.toml";
    81	  name: string | null;
    82	  version: string | null;
    83	  dependencies: Record<string, string>;
    84	  devDependencies: Record<string, string>;
    85	  scripts: Record<string, string>;
    86	}
    87	
    88	export interface EntryPoint {
    89	  path: string;
    90	  type: "main" | "binary" | "test" | "config";
    91	}
    92	
    93	export interface FolderCategory {
    94	  name: string;
    95	  path: string;
    96	  fileCount: number;
    97	  category: "routes" | "models" | "services" | "tests" | "config" | "utils" | "views" | "other";
    98	}
    99	
   100	export interface ToolingInfo {
   101	  testRunner: string | null;
   102	  buildTool: string | null;
   103	  linter: string | null;
   104	  formatter: string | null;
   105	  packageManager: "npm" | "yarn" | "pnpm" | "bun" | null;
   106	}
   107	
   108	export interface ScannerBrief {
   109	  manifest: ManifestInfo | null;
   110	  entryPoints: EntryPoint[];
   111	  folderTree: FolderCategory[];
   112	  tooling: ToolingInfo;
   113	  readme: string | null;
   114	  timestamp: string;
   115	}
   116	
   117	// ── Sync ──
   118	
   119	export interface SyncTarget {
   120	  file: string;
   121	  issues: DriftIssue[];
   122	  gitDiff: string | null;
   123	}
   124	
   125	export interface SyncResult {
   126	  file: string;
   127	  action: "updated" | "skipped" | "failed";
   128	  reason?: string;
   129	}
===== /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/pattern/index.ts =====
     1	import { join } from "node:path";
     2	import { existsSync, writeFileSync, appendFileSync, mkdirSync, readFileSync } from "node:fs";
     3	import chalk from "chalk";
     4	import type { MexConfig } from "../types.js";
     5	
     6	export async function runPatternAdd(config: MexConfig, name: string) {
     7	  if (!/^[a-z0-9-]+$/i.test(name)) {
     8	    throw new Error(`Invalid pattern name '${name}'. Use only letters, numbers, and hyphens.`);
     9	  }
    10	
    11	  const patternsDir = join(config.scaffoldRoot, "patterns");
    12	  const patternPath = join(patternsDir, `${name}.md`);
    13	  const indexPath = join(patternsDir, "INDEX.md");
    14	
    15	  if (existsSync(patternPath)) {
    16	    throw new Error(`Pattern '${name}' already exists at ${patternPath}`);
    17	  }
    18	
    19	  const today = new Date().toISOString().split("T")[0];
    20	
    21	  const template = `---
    22	name: ${name}
    23	description: [one line — what this pattern covers and when to use it]
    24	triggers:
    25	  - "[keyword that should trigger loading this file]"
    26	edges:
    27	  - target: "context/conventions.md"
    28	    condition: "when verifying this task"
    29	last_updated: ${today}
    30	---
    31	
    32	# ${name}
    33	
    34	## Context
    35	[What to load or know before starting this task type]
    36	
    37	## Steps
    38	[The workflow — what to do, in what order]
    39	
    40	## Gotchas
    41	[The things that go wrong. What to watch out for.]
    42	
    43	## Verify
    44	[Checklist to run after completing this task type]
    45	
    46	## Debug
    47	[What to check when this task type breaks]
    48	
    49	## Update Scaffold
    50	- [ ] Update \`ROUTER.md\` "Current Project State" if what's working/not built has changed
    51	- [ ] Update any \`context/\` files that are now out of date
    52	- [ ] If this is a new task type without a pattern, create one in \`patterns/\` and add to \`INDEX.md\`
    53	`;
    54	
    55	  mkdirSync(patternsDir, { recursive: true });
    56	  writeFileSync(patternPath, template, "utf8");
    57	
    58	  if (existsSync(indexPath)) {
    59	    const currentIndex = readFileSync(indexPath, "utf8");
    60	    const newlinePrefix = currentIndex.length === 0 || currentIndex.endsWith("\n") ? "" : "\n";
    61	    const entry = `${newlinePrefix}| [${name}.md](${name}.md) | [description] |\n`;
    62	    appendFileSync(indexPath, entry, "utf8");
    63	  }
    64	
    65	  console.log(chalk.green(`✓ Created pattern ${name}.md`));
    66	  console.log(chalk.dim(`  Added entry to patterns/INDEX.md`));
    67	  console.log(chalk.yellow(`! Remember to edit patterns/INDEX.md and replace [description] with a real use case.`));
    68	}
===== /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/sync/index.ts =====
     1	import chalk from "chalk";
     2	import { spawnSync } from "node:child_process";
     3	import { createInterface } from "node:readline";
     4	import type { MexConfig, SyncTarget, DriftIssue } from "../types.js";
     5	import { runDriftCheck } from "../drift/index.js";
     6	import { buildSyncBrief, buildCombinedBrief } from "./brief-builder.js";
     7	
     8	function askUser(question: string): Promise<string> {
     9	  const rl = createInterface({ input: process.stdin, output: process.stdout });
    10	  return new Promise((resolve) => {
    11	    rl.question(question, (answer) => {
    12	      rl.close();
    13	      resolve(answer.trim());
    14	    });
    15	  });
    16	}
    17	
    18	function runClaudeInteractive(brief: string, cwd: string): boolean {
    19	  const result = spawnSync("claude", [brief], {
    20	    cwd,
    21	    stdio: "inherit",
    22	    timeout: 300_000,
    23	  });
    24	  return result.status === 0 || result.status === null;
    25	}
    26	
    27	type SyncMode = "interactive" | "prompts";
    28	
    29	/** Run targeted sync: detect → brief → AI → verify → ask → loop */
    30	export async function runSync(
    31	  config: MexConfig,
    32	  opts: { dryRun?: boolean; includeWarnings?: boolean }
    33	): Promise<void> {
    34	  let cycle = 0;
    35	  let mode: SyncMode | null = null;
    36	
    37	  while (true) {
    38	    cycle++;
    39	
    40	    // Step 1: Run drift check
    41	    if (cycle === 1) {
    42	      console.log(chalk.bold("Running drift check..."));
    43	    } else {
    44	      console.log(chalk.bold("\nRe-checking for remaining drift..."));
    45	    }
    46	
    47	    const report = await runDriftCheck(config);
    48	
    49	    if (report.issues.length === 0) {
    50	      console.log(chalk.green("✓ No drift detected. Everything is in sync."));
    51	      return;
    52	    }
    53	
    54	    console.log(
    55	      chalk.yellow(
    56	        `Found ${report.issues.length} issues (score: ${report.score}/100)`
    57	      )
    58	    );
    59	
    60	    // Step 2: Group issues by file
    61	    const relevantIssues = opts.includeWarnings
    62	      ? report.issues
    63	      : report.issues.filter((i) => {
    64	          const fileHasError = report.issues.some(
    65	            (other) => other.file === i.file && other.severity === "error"
    66	          );
    67	          return fileHasError;
    68	        });
    69	
    70	    if (relevantIssues.length === 0) {
    71	      console.log(
    72	        chalk.green(
    73	          "No errors found. Only warnings remain (use --warnings to include them)."
    74	        )
    75	      );
    76	      return;
    77	    }
    78	
    79	    const targets = groupIntoTargets(relevantIssues);
    80	
    81	    console.log(
    82	      chalk.bold(`\n${targets.length} file(s) need attention:\n`)
    83	    );
    84	
    85	    for (const target of targets) {
    86	      const errors = target.issues.filter(
    87	        (i) => i.severity === "error"
    88	      ).length;
    89	      const warnings = target.issues.filter(
    90	        (i) => i.severity === "warning"
    91	      ).length;
    92	      console.log(
    93	        `  ${target.file} — ${errors} errors, ${warnings} warnings`
    94	      );
    95	    }
    96	
    97	    // Dry run — show combined prompt and exit
    98	    if (opts.dryRun) {
    99	      console.log(
   100	        chalk.dim("\n--dry-run: showing prompt without executing\n")
   101	      );
   102	      const brief = await buildCombinedBrief(targets, config.projectRoot);
   103	      console.log(brief);
   104	      console.log();
   105	      return;
   106	    }
   107	
   108	    // Ask user for mode (only on first cycle)
   109	    if (mode === null) {
   110	      console.log(chalk.bold("\nHow should we fix these?"));
   111	      console.log();
   112	      console.log("  1) Interactive — Claude fixes with you watching (default)");
   113	      console.log("  2) Show prompts — I'll paste manually");
   114	      console.log("  3) Exit");
   115	      console.log();
   116	
   117	      const choice = await askUser("Choice [1-3] (default: 1): ");
   118	      const picked = choice || "1";
   119	
   120	      switch (picked) {
   121	        case "1":
   122	          mode = "interactive";
   123	          break;
   124	        case "2":
   125	          mode = "prompts";
   126	          break;
   127	        case "3":
   128	          console.log(chalk.dim("Exiting. Run mex sync again anytime."));
   129	          return;
   130	        default:
   131	          console.log(chalk.dim("Exiting."));
   132	          return;
   133	      }
   134	    }
   135	
   136	    // Show prompts mode — print combined prompt and exit
   137	    if (mode === "prompts") {
   138	      const brief = await buildCombinedBrief(targets, config.projectRoot);
   139	      console.log(brief);
   140	      console.log();
   141	      return;
   142	    }
   143	
   144	    // Step 3: Fix all files in one interactive session
   145	    console.log();
   146	    console.log(chalk.bold(`\nSending all ${targets.length} file(s) to Claude in one session...\n`));
   147	
   148	    const brief = await buildCombinedBrief(targets, config.projectRoot);
   149	    const ok = runClaudeInteractive(brief, config.projectRoot);
   150	
   151	    if (!ok) {
   152	      console.log(chalk.red("  ✗ Claude session failed"));
   153	    }
   154	
   155	    // Step 4: Verify
   156	    const postReport = await runDriftCheck(config);
   157	    const scoreDelta = postReport.score - report.score;
   158	    const deltaStr =
   159	      scoreDelta > 0
   160	        ? chalk.green(`+${scoreDelta}`)
   161	        : scoreDelta === 0
   162	          ? chalk.yellow("+0")
   163	          : chalk.red(`${scoreDelta}`);
   164	
   165	    console.log(
   166	      chalk.bold(
   167	        `\nDrift score: ${report.score} → ${postReport.score}/100 (${deltaStr})`
   168	      )
   169	    );
   170	
   171	    // Step 5: Check if we should continue
   172	    const remainingErrors = postReport.issues.filter(
   173	      (i) => i.severity === "error"
   174	    ).length;
   175	    const remainingWarnings = postReport.issues.filter(
   176	      (i) => i.severity === "warning"
   177	    ).length;
   178	
   179	    if (remainingErrors === 0 && !opts.includeWarnings) {
   180	      if (remainingWarnings > 0) {
   181	        console.log(
   182	          chalk.dim(
   183	            `${remainingWarnings} warning(s) remain (use --warnings to include them).`
   184	          )
   185	        );
   186	      } else {
   187	        console.log(chalk.green("✓ All issues resolved."));
   188	      }
   189	      return;
   190	    }
   191	
   192	    if (postReport.score === 100) {
   193	      console.log(chalk.green("✓ Perfect score. All issues resolved."));
   194	      return;
   195	    }
   196	
   197	    // Ask user whether to continue
   198	    const remaining = opts.includeWarnings
   199	      ? remainingErrors + remainingWarnings
   200	      : remainingErrors;
   201	
   202	    const answer = await askUser(
   203	      `\n${remaining} issue(s) remain. Run another cycle? [Y/n] `
   204	    );
   205	
   206	    if (answer.toLowerCase() === "n") {
   207	      console.log(chalk.dim("Stopped. Run mex sync again anytime."));
   208	      return;
   209	    }
   210	  }
   211	}
   212	
   213	function groupIntoTargets(issues: DriftIssue[]): SyncTarget[] {
   214	  const byFile = new Map<string, DriftIssue[]>();
   215	  for (const issue of issues) {
   216	    if (!byFile.has(issue.file)) byFile.set(issue.file, []);
   217	    byFile.get(issue.file)!.push(issue);
   218	  }
   219	
   220	  return Array.from(byFile.entries()).map(([file, issues]) => ({
   221	    file,
   222	    issues,
   223	    gitDiff: null,
   224	  }));
   225	}
===== /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/sync/brief-builder.ts =====
     1	import { readFileSync, readdirSync, existsSync } from "node:fs";
     2	import { resolve, dirname, basename } from "node:path";
     3	import { globSync } from "glob";
     4	import { getGitDiff } from "../git.js";
     5	import type { SyncTarget } from "../types.js";
     6	
     7	/** Build a single combined prompt covering all targets */
     8	export async function buildCombinedBrief(
     9	  targets: SyncTarget[],
    10	  projectRoot: string
    11	): Promise<string> {
    12	  const sections: string[] = [];
    13	
    14	  for (const target of targets) {
    15	    sections.push(await buildFileSection(target, projectRoot));
    16	  }
    17	
    18	  return `The following scaffold files have drift issues that need fixing. Fix all of them in one pass.
    19	
    20	${sections.map((s, i) => `━━━ File ${i + 1}/${sections.length} ━━━\n\n${s}`).join("\n\n")}
    21	
    22	Update each file to fix its issues. Only change what's necessary — do not rewrite sections that are correct.
    23	When a referenced path no longer exists, find the correct current path from the filesystem context above and update the reference.`;
    24	}
    25	
    26	/** Build a targeted prompt for AI to fix a single file */
    27	export async function buildSyncBrief(
    28	  target: SyncTarget,
    29	  projectRoot: string
    30	): Promise<string> {
    31	  const section = await buildFileSection(target, projectRoot);
    32	
    33	  return `The following scaffold file has drift issues that need fixing:
    34	
    35	${section}
    36	
    37	Update the file to fix these issues. Only change what's necessary — do not rewrite sections that are correct.
    38	When a referenced path no longer exists, find the correct current path from the filesystem context above and update the reference.`;
    39	}
    40	
    41	/** Build the content section for a single target (no wrapper instructions) */
    42	async function buildFileSection(
    43	  target: SyncTarget,
    44	  projectRoot: string
    45	): Promise<string> {
    46	  const filePath = resolve(projectRoot, target.file);
    47	  let fileContent: string;
    48	  try {
    49	    fileContent = readFileSync(filePath, "utf-8");
    50	  } catch {
    51	    fileContent = "(file could not be read)";
    52	  }
    53	
    54	  const issueList = target.issues
    55	    .map((i) => `- [${i.severity}] ${i.code}: ${i.message}`)
    56	    .join("\n");
    57	
    58	  // Get git diff for paths referenced by this file's claims
    59	  const claimedPaths = target.issues
    60	    .filter((i) => i.claim?.kind === "path")
    61	    .map((i) => i.claim!.value);
    62	
    63	  const diff = claimedPaths.length
    64	    ? await getGitDiff(claimedPaths, projectRoot)
    65	    : target.gitDiff ?? "";
    66	
    67	  // For MISSING_PATH issues, find what actually exists nearby
    68	  const fileContext = buildFileContext(target, projectRoot);
    69	
    70	  let section = `**File:** ${target.file}
    71	
    72	**Issues found:**
    73	${issueList}
    74	
    75	**Current file content:**
    76	\`\`\`markdown
    77	${fileContent}
    78	\`\`\``;
    79	
    80	  if (fileContext) {
    81	    section += `
    82	
    83	**Filesystem context (what actually exists):**
    84	${fileContext}`;
    85	  }
    86	
    87	  if (diff) {
    88	    section += `
    89	
    90	**Recent git changes in referenced paths:**
    91	\`\`\`diff
    92	${diff}
    93	\`\`\``;
    94	  }
    95	
    96	  return section;
    97	}
    98	
    99	/** For missing path issues, list actual files in the relevant directories */
   100	function buildFileContext(
   101	  target: SyncTarget,
   102	  projectRoot: string
   103	): string | null {
   104	  const missingPaths = target.issues
   105	    .filter((i) => i.code === "MISSING_PATH" && i.claim?.kind === "path")
   106	    .map((i) => i.claim!.value);
   107	
   108	  if (missingPaths.length === 0) return null;
   109	
   110	  const sections: string[] = [];
   111	  const listedDirs = new Set<string>();
   112	
   113	  for (const missing of missingPaths) {
   114	    // Get the directory the missing file was expected in
   115	    const dir = missing.includes("/") ? dirname(missing) : ".";
   116	    const dirKey = dir === "." ? "root" : dir;
   117	
   118	    // List the directory contents (skip if already listed)
   119	    if (!listedDirs.has(dirKey)) {
   120	      listedDirs.add(dirKey);
   121	
   122	      const absDir = resolve(projectRoot, dir);
   123	      if (existsSync(absDir)) {
   124	        try {
   125	          const files = readdirSync(absDir)
   126	            .filter((f) => !f.startsWith("."))
   127	            .sort();
   128	          if (files.length > 0) {
   129	            sections.push(`\`${dir}/\` contains: ${files.join(", ")}`);
   130	          }
   131	        } catch {
   132	          // skip unreadable dirs
   133	        }
   134	      }
   135	    }
   136	
   137	    // Fuzzy search: find files with similar names anywhere in the project
   138	    const name = basename(missing);
   139	    const ext = name.includes(".") ? name.split(".").pop() : null;
   140	    if (ext) {
   141	      const matches = globSync(`**/*.${ext}`, {
   142	        cwd: projectRoot,
   143	        ignore: ["node_modules/**", ".mex/**", "dist/**", ".git/**"],
   144	        maxDepth: 5,
   145	      });
   146	
   147	      if (matches.length > 0 && matches.length <= 20) {
   148	        sections.push(
   149	          `All \`.${ext}\` files in project: ${matches.join(", ")}`
   150	        );
   151	        // Only list once per extension
   152	        break;
   153	      }
   154	    }
   155	  }
   156	
   157	  return sections.length > 0 ? sections.join("\n") : null;
   158	}
===== /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/index.ts =====
     1	import { readFileSync } from "node:fs";
     2	import { resolve, relative } from "node:path";
     3	import { globSync } from "glob";
     4	import type { MexConfig, DriftReport, DriftIssue, Claim } from "../types.js";
     5	import { extractClaims } from "./claims.js";
     6	import { parseFrontmatter } from "./frontmatter.js";
     7	import { computeScore } from "./scoring.js";
     8	import { checkPaths } from "./checkers/path.js";
     9	import { checkEdges } from "./checkers/edges.js";
    10	import { checkIndexSync } from "./checkers/index-sync.js";
    11	import { checkStaleness } from "./checkers/staleness.js";
    12	import { checkCommands } from "./checkers/command.js";
    13	import { checkDependencies } from "./checkers/dependency.js";
    14	import { checkCrossFile } from "./checkers/cross-file.js";
    15	import { checkScriptCoverage } from "./checkers/script-coverage.js";
    16	
    17	/** Run full drift detection across all scaffold files */
    18	export async function runDriftCheck(config: MexConfig): Promise<DriftReport> {
    19	  const { projectRoot, scaffoldRoot } = config;
    20	
    21	  // Find all markdown files in scaffold
    22	  const scaffoldFiles = findScaffoldFiles(projectRoot, scaffoldRoot);
    23	  const allClaims: Claim[] = [];
    24	  const allIssues: DriftIssue[] = [];
    25	
    26	  // Extract claims from all files
    27	  for (const filePath of scaffoldFiles) {
    28	    const source = relative(projectRoot, filePath);
    29	    const claims = extractClaims(filePath, source);
    30	    allClaims.push(...claims);
    31	  }
    32	
    33	  // Run checkers that work on individual files
    34	  for (const filePath of scaffoldFiles) {
    35	    const source = relative(projectRoot, filePath);
    36	
    37	    // Frontmatter edge check
    38	    const frontmatter = parseFrontmatter(filePath);
    39	    allIssues.push(
    40	      ...checkEdges(frontmatter, filePath, source, projectRoot, scaffoldRoot)
    41	    );
    42	
    43	    // Staleness check
    44	    const stalenessIssues = await checkStaleness(source, source, projectRoot);
    45	    allIssues.push(...stalenessIssues);
    46	  }
    47	
    48	  // Run checkers that work on claims
    49	  allIssues.push(...checkPaths(allClaims, projectRoot, scaffoldRoot));
    50	  allIssues.push(...checkCommands(allClaims, projectRoot));
    51	  allIssues.push(...checkDependencies(allClaims, projectRoot));
    52	  allIssues.push(...checkCrossFile(allClaims));
    53	
    54	  // Run structural checkers
    55	  allIssues.push(...checkIndexSync(projectRoot, scaffoldRoot));
    56	
    57	  // Run coverage checkers (reality → scaffold direction)
    58	  allIssues.push(...checkScriptCoverage(scaffoldFiles, projectRoot));
    59	
    60	  const score = computeScore(allIssues);
    61	
    62	  return {
    63	    score,
    64	    issues: allIssues,
    65	    filesChecked: scaffoldFiles.length,
    66	    timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
    67	  };
    68	}
    69	
    70	/** Find all markdown files that are part of the scaffold */
    71	function findScaffoldFiles(
    72	  projectRoot: string,
    73	  scaffoldRoot: string
    74	): string[] {
    75	  const scaffoldPatterns = [
    76	    "context/*.md",
    77	    "patterns/*.md",
    78	    "ROUTER.md",
    79	    "AGENTS.md",
    80	    "SETUP.md",
    81	    "SYNC.md",
    82	  ];
    83	
    84	  const files: string[] = [];
    85	
    86	  // Search inside scaffold root (handles both .mex/ and root layouts)
    87	  for (const pattern of scaffoldPatterns) {
    88	    const matches = globSync(pattern, {
    89	      cwd: scaffoldRoot,
    90	      absolute: true,
    91	      ignore: ["node_modules/**"],
    92	    });
    93	    files.push(...matches);
    94	  }
    95	
    96	  // Also check project root for tool config files (CLAUDE.md, etc.)
    97	  if (scaffoldRoot !== projectRoot) {
    98	    for (const name of ["CLAUDE.md", ".cursorrules", ".windsurfrules"]) {
    99	      const matches = globSync(name, {
   100	        cwd: projectRoot,
   101	        absolute: true,
   102	        ignore: ["node_modules/**"],
   103	      });
   104	      files.push(...matches);
   105	    }
   106	  }
   107	
   108	  // Deduplicate
   109	  return [...new Set(files)];
   110	}
===== /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/claims.ts =====
     1	import { readFileSync } from "node:fs";
     2	import { visit } from "unist-util-visit";
     3	import { parseMarkdown, getHeadingAtLine, isNegatedSection } from "../markdown.js";
     4	import type { Claim } from "../types.js";
     5	import type { Root, Code, InlineCode, Strong, Text } from "mdast";
     6	
     7	const KNOWN_EXTENSIONS = /\.(ts|js|tsx|jsx|py|go|rs|rb|java|json|yaml|yml|toml|md|css|scss|html|vue|svelte|sh)$/;
     8	const COMMAND_PREFIXES = /^(npm|yarn|pnpm|bun|make|cargo|python|pip|go|node|npx|tsx)\s/;
     9	const DEPENDENCY_SECTION_PATTERNS = /key\s*libraries|core\s*technologies|dependencies|stack|tech/i;
    10	/** Paths with angle brackets or square brackets are template placeholders, not real paths */
    11	const TEMPLATE_PLACEHOLDER = /[<>\[\]{}]/;
    12	
    13	/** HTTP methods that indicate an API route, not a file path */
    14	const HTTP_METHOD_PREFIX = /^(GET|POST|PUT|PATCH|DELETE|HEAD|OPTIONS)\s+\//;
    15	
    16	/** Things that look like paths but are actually code snippets, URL routes, or other non-path content */
    17	function isNotAPath(value: string): boolean {
    18	  // URL routes: /voice/incoming, /api/users — start with / but have no file extension
    19	  if (value.startsWith("/") && !KNOWN_EXTENSIONS.test(value)) return true;
    20	
    21	  // HTTP method + route: GET /api/bookmarks, POST /users/:id
    22	  if (HTTP_METHOD_PREFIX.test(value)) return true;
    23	
    24	  // Code snippets: contains =, (), ;, or other code-like characters
    25	  if (/[=();,]/.test(value)) return true;
    26	
    27	  // Quoted strings or attribute assignments: gather.action="...", foo="bar"
    28	  if (/["']/.test(value)) return true;
    29	
    30	  // Wildcard prefixes like *_streaming_client.py — patterns, not real paths
    31	  if (value.startsWith("*")) return true;
    32	
    33	  return false;
    34	}
    35	
    36	/** Extract all claims from a markdown file */
    37	export function extractClaims(filePath: string, source: string): Claim[] {
    38	  let content: string;
    39	  try {
    40	    content = readFileSync(filePath, "utf-8");
    41	  } catch {
    42	    return [];
    43	  }
    44	
    45	  const tree = parseMarkdown(content);
    46	  const claims: Claim[] = [];
    47	
    48	  // Extract from inline code
    49	  visit(tree, "inlineCode", (node: InlineCode) => {
    50	    const line = node.position?.start.line ?? 0;
    51	    const heading = getHeadingAtLine(tree, line);
    52	    const negated = isNegatedSection(heading);
    53	
    54	    // Path claims: contains / or ends in known extension
    55	    if (node.value.includes("/") || KNOWN_EXTENSIONS.test(node.value)) {
    56	      // Skip commands, template placeholders, and non-path content
    57	      if (!COMMAND_PREFIXES.test(node.value) && !TEMPLATE_PLACEHOLDER.test(node.value) && !isNotAPath(node.value)) {
    58	        claims.push({
    59	          kind: "path",
    60	          value: node.value,
    61	          source,
    62	          line,
    63	          section: heading,
    64	          negated,
    65	        });
    66	      }
    67	    }
    68	
    69	    // Command claims
    70	    if (COMMAND_PREFIXES.test(node.value)) {
    71	      claims.push({
    72	        kind: "command",
    73	        value: node.value,
    74	        source,
    75	        line,
    76	        section: heading,
    77	        negated,
    78	      });
    79	    }
    80	  });
    81	
    82	  // Extract from code blocks
    83	  visit(tree, "code", (node: Code) => {
    84	    const line = node.position?.start.line ?? 0;
    85	    const heading = getHeadingAtLine(tree, line);
    86	    const negated = isNegatedSection(heading);
    87	
    88	    // Each line of the code block could be a command
    89	    for (const codeLine of node.value.split("\n")) {
    90	      const trimmed = codeLine.trim();
    91	      if (COMMAND_PREFIXES.test(trimmed)) {
    92	        claims.push({
    93	          kind: "command",
    94	          value: trimmed,
    95	          source,
    96	          line,
    97	          section: heading,
    98	          negated,
    99	        });
   100	      }
   101	    }
   102	  });
   103	
   104	  // Extract dependencies from **BoldName** patterns in relevant sections
   105	  visit(tree, "strong", (node: Strong) => {
   106	    const line = node.position?.start.line ?? 0;
   107	    const heading = getHeadingAtLine(tree, line);
   108	    const negated = isNegatedSection(heading);
   109	
   110	    if (heading && DEPENDENCY_SECTION_PATTERNS.test(heading)) {
   111	      const text = getStrongText(node);
   112	      if (!text) return;
   113	
   114	      // Check for version pattern: "React 18" or "Node v20"
   115	      const versionMatch = text.match(/^(.+?)\s+[v^~>=<]*(\d[\d.]*\S*)$/);
   116	      if (versionMatch) {
   117	        claims.push({
   118	          kind: "dependency",
   119	          value: versionMatch[1].trim(),
   120	          source,
   121	          line,
   122	          section: heading,
   123	          negated,
   124	        });
   125	        claims.push({
   126	          kind: "version",
   127	          value: text,
   128	          source,
   129	          line,
   130	          section: heading,
   131	          negated,
   132	        });
   133	      } else {
   134	        claims.push({
   135	          kind: "dependency",
   136	          value: text,
   137	          source,
   138	          line,
   139	          section: heading,
   140	          negated,
   141	        });
   142	      }
   143	    }
   144	  });
   145	
   146	  return claims;
   147	}
   148	
   149	function getStrongText(node: Strong): string | null {
   150	  const textNode = node.children.find(
   151	    (c): c is Text => c.type === "text"
   152	  );
   153	  return textNode?.value ?? null;
   154	}
===== /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/frontmatter.ts =====
     1	import { readFileSync } from "node:fs";
     2	import { extractFrontmatter } from "../markdown.js";
     3	import type { ScaffoldFrontmatter } from "../types.js";
     4	
     5	/** Read a scaffold file and extract its YAML frontmatter */
     6	export function parseFrontmatter(
     7	  filePath: string
     8	): ScaffoldFrontmatter | null {
     9	  try {
    10	    const content = readFileSync(filePath, "utf-8");
    11	    return extractFrontmatter(content);
    12	  } catch {
    13	    return null;
    14	  }
    15	}
===== /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/markdown.ts =====
     1	import { unified } from "unified";
     2	import remarkParse from "remark-parse";
     3	import remarkFrontmatter from "remark-frontmatter";
     4	import { visit } from "unist-util-visit";
     5	import YAML from "yaml";
     6	import type { ScaffoldFrontmatter } from "./types.js";
     7	import type { Root, Content } from "mdast";
     8	
     9	const parser = unified().use(remarkParse).use(remarkFrontmatter, ["yaml"]);
    10	
    11	/** Parse markdown string into AST */
    12	export function parseMarkdown(content: string): Root {
    13	  return parser.parse(content);
    14	}
    15	
    16	/** Extract YAML frontmatter from markdown content */
    17	export function extractFrontmatter(
    18	  content: string
    19	): ScaffoldFrontmatter | null {
    20	  const tree = parseMarkdown(content);
    21	  let frontmatter: ScaffoldFrontmatter | null = null;
    22	
    23	  visit(tree, "yaml", (node: { value: string }) => {
    24	    try {
    25	      frontmatter = YAML.parse(node.value) as ScaffoldFrontmatter;
    26	    } catch {
    27	      // Invalid YAML — skip
    28	    }
    29	  });
    30	
    31	  return frontmatter;
    32	}
    33	
    34	/** Get the current heading context for a given line position */
    35	export function getHeadingAtLine(
    36	  tree: Root,
    37	  line: number
    38	): string | null {
    39	  let currentHeading: string | null = null;
    40	
    41	  for (const node of tree.children) {
    42	    if (!node.position) continue;
    43	    if (node.position.start.line > line) break;
    44	    if (node.type === "heading") {
    45	      currentHeading = getTextContent(node);
    46	    }
    47	  }
    48	
    49	  return currentHeading;
    50	}
    51	
    52	/** Extract plain text from an AST node */
    53	export function getTextContent(node: Content | Root): string {
    54	  if ("value" in node && typeof node.value === "string") {
    55	    return node.value;
    56	  }
    57	  if ("children" in node) {
    58	    return (node.children as Content[]).map(getTextContent).join("");
    59	  }
    60	  return "";
    61	}
    62	
    63	/** Check if a heading or its ancestors suggest negation */
    64	export function isNegatedSection(heading: string | null): boolean {
    65	  if (!heading) return false;
    66	  const lower = heading.toLowerCase();
    67	  return (
    68	    lower.includes("not exist") ||
    69	    lower.includes("not use") ||
    70	    lower.includes("deliberately not") ||
    71	    lower.includes("excluded") ||
    72	    lower.includes("removed") ||
    73	    lower.includes("deprecated")
    74	  );
    75	}
===== /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/watch.ts =====
     1	import { writeFileSync, readFileSync, existsSync, chmodSync, unlinkSync } from "node:fs";
     2	import { resolve } from "node:path";
     3	import chalk from "chalk";
     4	import type { MexConfig } from "./types.js";
     5	
     6	const HOOK_MARKER = "# mex-drift-check";
     7	
     8	function buildHookContent(config: MexConfig): string {
     9	  const cliPath = resolve(config.scaffoldRoot, "dist", "cli.js");
    10	  // Use local CLI if built, otherwise fall back to npx
    11	  const cmd = existsSync(cliPath)
    12	    ? `node "${cliPath}" check --quiet`
    13	    : "npx mex check --quiet";
    14	
    15	  return `#!/bin/sh
    16	${HOOK_MARKER}
    17	# Auto-installed by mex watch — runs drift check after each commit
    18	SCORE=$(${cmd} 2>&1) || true
    19	# Only show output if there are issues (not a perfect score)
    20	case "$SCORE" in
    21	  *"100/100"*) ;;
    22	  *) echo "$SCORE" ;;
    23	esac
    24	`;
    25	}
    26	
    27	export async function manageHook(
    28	  config: MexConfig,
    29	  opts: { uninstall?: boolean }
    30	): Promise<void> {
    31	  const hookPath = resolve(config.projectRoot, ".git", "hooks", "post-commit");
    32	
    33	  if (opts.uninstall) {
    34	    uninstallHook(hookPath);
    35	    return;
    36	  }
    37	
    38	  installHook(hookPath, config);
    39	}
    40	
    41	function installHook(hookPath: string, config: MexConfig): void {
    42	  const hookContent = buildHookContent(config);
    43	
    44	  if (existsSync(hookPath)) {
    45	    const existing = readFileSync(hookPath, "utf-8");
    46	    if (existing.includes(HOOK_MARKER)) {
    47	      console.log(chalk.yellow("mex post-commit hook is already installed."));
    48	      return;
    49	    }
    50	
    51	    // Append to existing hook
    52	    const updated = existing.trimEnd() + "\n\n" + hookContent;
    53	    writeFileSync(hookPath, updated);
    54	    chmodSync(hookPath, 0o755);
    55	    console.log(
    56	      chalk.green("Added mex drift check to existing post-commit hook.")
    57	    );
    58	    return;
    59	  }
    60	
    61	  writeFileSync(hookPath, hookContent);
    62	  chmodSync(hookPath, 0o755);
    63	  console.log(chalk.green("Installed mex post-commit hook."));
    64	}
    65	
    66	function uninstallHook(hookPath: string): void {
    67	  if (!existsSync(hookPath)) {
    68	    console.log(chalk.yellow("No post-commit hook found."));
    69	    return;
    70	  }
    71	
    72	  const content = readFileSync(hookPath, "utf-8");
    73	  if (!content.includes(HOOK_MARKER)) {
    74	    console.log(
    75	      chalk.yellow("post-commit hook exists but was not installed by mex.")
    76	    );
    77	    return;
    78	  }
    79	
    80	  // Remove mex section (everything between marker and next non-mex line)
    81	  const lines = content.split("\n");
    82	  const filtered: string[] = [];
    83	  let inMexBlock = false;
    84	
    85	  for (const line of lines) {
    86	    if (line.includes(HOOK_MARKER)) {
    87	      inMexBlock = true;
    88	      continue;
    89	    }
    90	    if (inMexBlock) {
    91	      // Skip lines that are part of the mex hook block
    92	      if (line.startsWith("#") || line.startsWith("SCORE=") ||
    93	          line.startsWith("case") || line.startsWith("  *") ||
    94	          line.startsWith("esac") || line.startsWith("npx mex") ||
    95	          line.startsWith("node ") || line.trim() === "") {
    96	        continue;
    97	      }
    98	      // Non-mex line found — stop skipping
    99	      inMexBlock = false;
   100	    }
   101	    filtered.push(line);
   102	  }
   103	
   104	  const remaining = filtered.join("\n").trim();
   105	  if (remaining === "#!/bin/sh" || remaining === "") {
   106	    // Only shebang left — remove the file
   107	    unlinkSync(hookPath);
   108	    console.log(chalk.green("Removed mex post-commit hook."));
   109	  } else {
   110	    writeFileSync(hookPath, remaining + "\n");
   111	    chmodSync(hookPath, 0o755);
   112	    console.log(
   113	      chalk.green("Removed mex section from post-commit hook.")
   114	    );
   115	  }
   116	}

     1	// ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     2	// MODULE: Memory Crud Utils
     3	// ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     4	
     5	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     6	   IMPORTS
     7	──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
     8	
     9	import * as mutationLedger from '../lib/storage/mutation-ledger.js';
    10	import { toErrorMessage } from '../utils/index.js';
    11	
    12	import type { DatabaseExtended } from './types.js';
    13	import type { MemoryHashSnapshot, MutationLedgerInput } from './memory-crud-types.js';
    14	
    15	// Feature catalog: Memory indexing (memory_save)
    16	// Feature catalog: Memory metadata update (memory_update)
    17	// Feature catalog: Single and folder delete (memory_delete)
    18	
    19	
    20	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    21	   HELPERS
    22	──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
    23	
    24	/** Retrieve a memory's hash snapshot from the database. Returns null if unavailable. */
    25	function getMemoryHashSnapshot(database: DatabaseExtended | null, memoryId: number): MemoryHashSnapshot | null {
    26	  if (!database || !('prepare' in database)) {
    27	    return null;
    28	  }
    29	
    30	  try {
    31	    const row = database.prepare(`
    32	      SELECT id, content_hash, spec_folder, file_path
    33	      FROM memory_index
    34	      WHERE id = ?
    35	    `).get(memoryId) as MemoryHashSnapshot | undefined;
    36	
    37	    return row ?? null;
    38	  } catch (_err: unknown) {
    39	    return null;
    40	  }
    41	}
    42	
    43	/**
    44	 * Safely append a mutation to the ledger. Returns false on failure so callers
    45	 * can surface warnings in their MCP responses (F1.10 fix).
    46	 */
    47	function appendMutationLedgerSafe(database: DatabaseExtended | null, input: MutationLedgerInput): boolean {
    48	  if (!database) {
    49	    return false;
    50	  }
    51	
    52	  try {
    53	    mutationLedger.initLedger(database as Parameters<typeof mutationLedger.initLedger>[0]);
    54	    mutationLedger.appendEntry(database as Parameters<typeof mutationLedger.appendEntry>[0], {
    55	      mutation_type: input.mutationType,
    56	      reason: input.reason,
    57	      prior_hash: input.priorHash,
    58	      new_hash: input.newHash,
    59	      linked_memory_ids: input.linkedMemoryIds,
    60	      decision_meta: input.decisionMeta,
    61	      actor: input.actor,
    62	      session_id: input.sessionId ?? null,
    63	    });
    64	    return true;
    65	  } catch (err: unknown) {
    66	    const message = toErrorMessage(err);
    67	    console.warn(`[memory-crud] mutation ledger append failed: ${message}`);
    68	    return false;
    69	  }
    70	}
    71	
    72	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    73	   EXPORTS
    74	──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
    75	
    76	export {
    77	  getMemoryHashSnapshot,
    78	  appendMutationLedgerSafe,
    79	};

     1	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     2	// MODULE: Memory Ingest Handlers
     3	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     4	
     5	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     6	   1. IMPORTS
     7	──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
     8	
     9	import { randomBytes } from 'node:crypto';
    10	import fs from 'node:fs';
    11	import path from 'node:path';
    12	
    13	import { ALLOWED_BASE_PATHS, DATABASE_PATH, checkDatabaseUpdated } from '../core/index.js';
    14	import { MAX_INGEST_PATHS } from '../schemas/tool-input-schemas.js';
    15	import { createMCPSuccessResponse, createMCPErrorResponse } from '../lib/response/envelope.js';
    16	import {
    17	  createIngestJob,
    18	  enqueueIngestJob,
    19	  getIngestJob,
    20	  cancelIngestJob,
    21	  getIngestProgressPercent,
    22	  getIngestForecast,
    23	  type IngestJob,
    24	} from '../lib/ops/job-queue.js';
    25	import * as retrievalTelemetry from '../lib/telemetry/retrieval-telemetry.js';
    26	
    27	import type { MCPResponse } from './types.js';
    28	
    29	// Feature catalog: Async ingestion job lifecycle
    30	// Feature catalog: Workspace scanning and indexing (memory_index_scan)
    31	
    32	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    33	   2. TYPES
    34	──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
    35	
    36	interface MemoryIngestStartArgs {
    37	  paths: string[];
    38	  specFolder?: string;
    39	}
    40	
    41	interface MemoryIngestStatusArgs {
    42	  jobId: string;
    43	}
    44	
    45	interface MemoryIngestCancelArgs {
    46	  jobId: string;
    47	}
    48	
    49	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    50	   3. CONSTANTS
    51	──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
    52	
    53	const MAX_PATH_LENGTH = 500;
    54	
    55	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    56	   4. HELPERS
    57	──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
    58	
    59	function hasTraversalSegment(inputPath: string): boolean {
    60	  return inputPath.split(/[\\/]+/).includes('..');
    61	}
    62	
    63	function toPublicPathLabel(filePath: string): string {
    64	  return filePath === '__job__' ? filePath : path.basename(filePath || '');
    65	}
    66	
    67	// Use a nanoid-style 12-char URL-safe identifier without UUID dependency.
    68	const NANOID_ALPHABET = '0123456789ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZabcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz';
    69	function createJobId(): string {
    70	  const bytes = randomBytes(12);
    71	  let id = '';
    72	  for (let i = 0; i < 12; i++) {
    73	    id += NANOID_ALPHABET[bytes[i] % NANOID_ALPHABET.length];
    74	  }
    75	  return `job_${id}`;
    76	}
    77	
    78	function mapJobForResponse(job: IngestJob): Record<string, unknown> {
    79	  let forecast: Record<string, unknown>;
    80	  try {
    81	    forecast = getIngestForecast(job) as unknown as Record<string, unknown>;
    82	  } catch (error: unknown) {
    83	    const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
    84	    forecast = {
    85	      etaSeconds: null,
    86	      etaConfidence: null,
    87	      failureRisk: null,
    88	      riskSignals: [],
    89	      caveat: `Forecast unavailable: ${message}`,
    90	    };
    91	  }
    92	
    93	  let telemetryPayload: Record<string, unknown> | undefined;
    94	  if (retrievalTelemetry.isExtendedTelemetryEnabled()) {
    95	    const telemetry = retrievalTelemetry.createTelemetry();
    96	    retrievalTelemetry.recordLifecycleForecastDiagnostics(telemetry, forecast, {
    97	      state: job.state,
    98	      progress: getIngestProgressPercent(job),
    99	      filesProcessed: job.filesProcessed,
   100	      filesTotal: job.filesTotal,
   101	    });
   102	    telemetryPayload = retrievalTelemetry.toJSON(telemetry);
   103	  }
   104	
   105	  return {
   106	    jobId: job.id,
   107	    state: job.state,
   108	    specFolder: job.specFolder,
   109	    paths: job.paths.map((entry) => toPublicPathLabel(entry)),
   110	    filesTotal: job.filesTotal,
   111	    filesProcessed: job.filesProcessed,
   112	    progress: getIngestProgressPercent(job),
   113	    errors: job.errors.map((entry) => ({
   114	      ...entry,
   115	      filePath: toPublicPathLabel(entry.filePath),
   116	    })),
   117	    createdAt: job.createdAt,
   118	    updatedAt: job.updatedAt,
   119	    forecast: forecast as unknown as Record<string, unknown>,
   120	    ...(telemetryPayload ? { _telemetry: telemetryPayload } : {}),
   121	  };
   122	}
   123	
   124	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   125	   5. HANDLERS
   126	──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
   127	
   128	/** Handle memory_ingest_start tool — begins batch ingestion of spec documents from disk.
   129	 * @param args - Ingest arguments (paths, scope, options)
   130	 * @returns MCP response with job ID and forecast
   131	 */
   132	async function handleMemoryIngestStart(args: MemoryIngestStartArgs): Promise<MCPResponse> {
   133	  await checkDatabaseUpdated();
   134	
   135	  let paths = Array.isArray(args.paths)
   136	    ? args.paths.filter((entry): entry is string => typeof entry === 'string' && entry.trim().length > 0)
   137	    : [];
   138	
   139	  if (paths.length === 0) {
   140	    throw new Error('paths must be a non-empty array of file paths');
   141	  }
   142	
   143	  if (paths.length > MAX_INGEST_PATHS) {
   144	    throw new Error(`paths exceeds maximum of ${MAX_INGEST_PATHS}`);
   145	  }
   146	
   147	  const overlengthPaths = paths
   148	    .filter((entry) => entry.length > MAX_PATH_LENGTH)
   149	    .map((entry) => ({
   150	      filePath: toPublicPathLabel(entry),
   151	      reason: `path exceeds ${MAX_PATH_LENGTH} characters`,
   152	    }));
   153	  const withinLength = paths.filter((entry) => entry.length <= MAX_PATH_LENGTH);
   154	  if (withinLength.length !== paths.length) {
   155	    console.warn(
   156	      `[memory-ingest] Dropped ${paths.length - withinLength.length} path(s) longer than ${MAX_PATH_LENGTH} characters`,
   157	    );
   158	  }
   159	  paths = withinLength;
   160	
   161	  if (paths.length === 0) {
   162	    throw new Error('paths must be a non-empty array of file paths');
   163	  }
   164	
   165	  const configuredMemoryRoot = process.env.MEMORY_BASE_PATH;
   166	  const derivedAllowedBasePath = configuredMemoryRoot && configuredMemoryRoot.trim().length > 0
   167	    ? path.resolve(process.cwd(), configuredMemoryRoot)
   168	    : path.resolve(path.dirname(DATABASE_PATH));
   169	  const allowedBasePaths = Array.from(
   170	    new Set([derivedAllowedBasePath, ...ALLOWED_BASE_PATHS].map((basePath) => path.resolve(basePath))),
   171	  );
   172	
   173	  const normalizedPaths: string[] = [];
   174	  const invalidPaths: Array<{ input: string; reason: string }> = [];
   175	
   176	  for (const inputPath of paths) {
   177	    const trimmedPath = inputPath.trim();
   178	
   179	    if (hasTraversalSegment(trimmedPath)) {
   180	      invalidPaths.push({ input: inputPath, reason: 'contains path traversal segments (..)' });
   181	      continue;
   182	    }
   183	
   184	    const resolvedPath = path.resolve(trimmedPath);
   185	
   186	    const hasTraversalAfterResolve = resolvedPath.split(path.sep).includes('..');
   187	    if (hasTraversalAfterResolve) {
   188	      invalidPaths.push({ input: inputPath, reason: 'contains path traversal segments (..)' });
   189	      continue;
   190	    }
   191	
   192	    // Resolve symlinks so traversal checks apply to canonical targets.
   193	    let realPath: string;
   194	    try {
   195	      realPath = fs.realpathSync(resolvedPath);
   196	    } catch {
   197	      // Fall back to resolvedPath during pre-validation when target does not yet exist.
   198	      realPath = resolvedPath;
   199	    }
   200	
   201	    const isWithinAllowedBase = allowedBasePaths.some((basePath) => (
   202	      realPath === basePath || realPath.startsWith(`${basePath}${path.sep}`)
   203	    ));
   204	    if (!isWithinAllowedBase) {
   205	      invalidPaths.push({ input: inputPath, reason: 'is outside allowed memory roots' });
   206	      continue;
   207	    }
   208	
   209	    normalizedPaths.push(realPath);
   210	  }
   211	
   212	  if (invalidPaths.length > 0) {
   213	    return createMCPErrorResponse({
   214	      tool: 'memory_ingest_start',
   215	      error: `Invalid path(s) rejected: ${invalidPaths.map((entry) => `"${entry.input}" (${entry.reason})`).join(', ')}`,
   216	      code: 'E_VALIDATION',
   217	      details: {
   218	        allowedBasePathCount: allowedBasePaths.length,
   219	        allowedPathPolicy: 'configured-memory-roots',
   220	        rejectedCount: invalidPaths.length,
   221	      },
   222	      recovery: {
   223	        hint: 'Provide file paths under an allowed memory base directory and remove traversal segments.',
   224	        actions: ['Use absolute paths rooted in the configured memory base path'],
   225	        severity: 'warning',
   226	      },
   227	    });
   228	  }
   229	
   230	  const deduplicatedPaths: string[] = [];
   231	  const seenPaths = new Set<string>();
   232	  let duplicatePathCount = 0;
   233	
   234	  for (const normalizedPath of normalizedPaths) {
   235	    if (seenPaths.has(normalizedPath)) {
   236	      duplicatePathCount++;
   237	      continue;
   238	    }
   239	
   240	    seenPaths.add(normalizedPath);
   241	    deduplicatedPaths.push(normalizedPath);
   242	  }
   243	
   244	  paths = deduplicatedPaths;
   245	
   246	  const jobId = createJobId();
   247	  const job = await createIngestJob({
   248	    id: jobId,
   249	    paths,
   250	    specFolder: args.specFolder,
   251	  });
   252	
   253	  enqueueIngestJob(job.id);
   254	
   255	  return createMCPSuccessResponse({
   256	    tool: 'memory_ingest_start',
   257	    summary: `Queued ingest job ${job.id} (${job.filesTotal} file(s))`,
   258	    data: {
   259	      jobId: job.id,
   260	      state: job.state,
===== /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/schemas/tool-input-schemas.ts =====
     1	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     2	// MODULE: Tool Input Schemas
     3	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     4	// Centralized strict Zod validation schemas for MCP tool inputs.
     5	// Strict mode is controlled by SPECKIT_STRICT_SCHEMAS (default: true).
     6	
     7	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     8	   1. IMPORTS
     9	──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
    10	
    11	import { z, ZodError, type ZodType } from 'zod';
    12	
    13	// Feature catalog: Strict Zod schema validation
    14	
    15	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    16	   2. TYPES
    17	──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
    18	
    19	type ToolInput = Record<string, unknown>;
    20	type ToolInputSchema = ZodType<ToolInput>;
    21	
    22	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    23	   3. HELPERS
    24	──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
    25	
    26	export const getSchema = <T extends z.ZodRawShape>(shape: T): z.ZodObject<T> => {
    27	  const strict = process.env.SPECKIT_STRICT_SCHEMAS !== 'false';
    28	  const base = z.object(shape);
    29	  return strict ? base.strict() : base.passthrough();
    30	};
    31	
    32	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    33	   4. CONSTANTS
    34	──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
    35	
    36	// Guard against safeNumericPreprocess.pipe(z.number()) coercing "", null, and false to 0.
    37	// Use a safe preprocessor that only accepts actual numbers or numeric strings.
    38	const safeNumericPreprocess = z.preprocess((val) => {
    39	  if (typeof val === 'number') return val;
    40	  if (typeof val === 'string' && val.trim().length > 0) {
    41	    const parsed = Number(val);
    42	    return Number.isFinite(parsed) ? parsed : val;
    43	  }
    44	  return val;
    45	}, z.number());
    46	
    47	const positiveInt = safeNumericPreprocess.pipe(z.number().int().positive());
    48	const positiveIntMax = (max: number) => safeNumericPreprocess.pipe(z.number().int().min(1).max(max));
    49	const boundedNumber = (min: number, max: number) => safeNumericPreprocess.pipe(z.number().min(min).max(max));
    50	const optionalStringArray = z.array(z.string()).optional();
    51	
    52	const PATH_TRAVERSAL_MESSAGE = 'Path must not contain traversal sequences';
    53	const isSafePath = (value: string): boolean => !value.includes('..') && !value.includes('\0');
    54	const pathString = (minLength = 0) => {
    55	  let schema = z.string();
    56	  if (minLength > 0) {
    57	    schema = schema.min(minLength);
    58	  }
    59	  return schema.refine(isSafePath, { message: PATH_TRAVERSAL_MESSAGE });
    60	};
    61	const optionalPathString = (minLength = 0) => pathString(minLength).optional();
    62	
    63	/** Shared max paths constant — used by both schema and handler. */
    64	export const MAX_INGEST_PATHS = 50;
    65	export const MEMORY_BULK_DELETE_MIN_OLDER_THAN_DAYS = 1;
    66	
    67	const intentEnum = z.enum([
    68	  'add_feature',
    69	  'fix_bug',
    70	  'refactor',
    71	  'security_audit',
    72	  'understand',
    73	  'find_spec',
    74	  'find_decision',
    75	]);
    76	
    77	const importanceTierEnum = z.enum([
    78	  'constitutional',
    79	  'critical',
    80	  'important',
    81	  'normal',
    82	  'temporary',

codex
I’ve confirmed Mex’s core model is file-scaffold drift management, not a first-class mutable memory database. I’m now reading the exact Spec Kit handlers for delete, update, and dedup/no-op behavior so the comparison lands on real lifecycle semantics rather than just feature names.

     2	// MODULE: Memory Crud Delete

     4	
     5	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

     8	
     9	import { checkDatabaseUpdated } from '../core/index.js';
    10	import * as vectorIndex from '../lib/search/vector-index.js';
    11	import * as checkpoints from '../lib/storage/checkpoints.js';
    12	import * as mutationLedger from '../lib/storage/mutation-ledger.js';
    13	import * as causalEdges from '../lib/storage/causal-edges.js';
    14	import { createMCPSuccessResponse, createMCPErrorResponse } from '../lib/response/envelope.js';
    15	import { toErrorMessage } from '../utils/index.js';
    16	
    17	import { recordHistory } from '../lib/storage/history.js';
    18	import { appendMutationLedgerSafe, getMemoryHashSnapshot } from './memory-crud-utils.js';
    19	import { runPostMutationHooks } from './mutation-hooks.js';
    20	import { buildMutationHookFeedback } from '../hooks/mutation-feedback.js';
    21	import { clearDegreeCacheForDb } from '../lib/search/graph-search-fn.js';
    22	
    23	import type { MCPResponse } from './types.js';
    24	import type { DeleteArgs, MemoryHashSnapshot } from './memory-crud-types.js';
    25	
    26	// Feature catalog: Single and folder delete (memory_delete)
    27	// Feature catalog: Validation feedback (memory_validate)
    28	// Feature catalog: Transaction wrappers on mutation handlers
    29	// Feature catalog: Per-memory history log
    30	
    31	
    32	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    33	   CORE LOGIC
    34	──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
    35	
    36	function parseMemoryId(rawId: number | string): number {
    37	  const numericId = typeof rawId === 'string'
    38	    ? Number.parseInt(rawId.trim(), 10)
    39	    : rawId;
    40	
    41	  if (
    42	    typeof numericId !== 'number' ||
    43	    !Number.isSafeInteger(numericId) ||
    44	    numericId <= 0 ||
    45	    (typeof rawId === 'string' && !/^\d+$/.test(rawId.trim()))
    46	  ) {
    47	    throw new Error('Invalid memory ID: must be a positive integer');
    48	  }
    49	
    50	  return numericId;
    51	}
    52	
    53	function createDatabaseUnavailableDeleteResponse(): MCPResponse {
    54	  return createMCPErrorResponse({
    55	    tool: 'memory_delete',
    56	    error: 'Delete aborted: database unavailable',
    57	    code: 'E_DB_UNAVAILABLE',
    58	    details: { deleted: 0 },
    59	    recovery: {
    60	      hint: 'Restart the MCP server or run memory_index_scan() to reinitialize the database',
    61	      actions: ['Restart the MCP server', 'Call memory_index_scan()'],
    62	      severity: 'error',
    63	    },
    64	  });
    65	}
    66	
    67	/** Handle memory_delete tool -- deletes a single memory by ID or bulk-deletes by spec folder. */
    68	async function handleMemoryDelete(args: DeleteArgs): Promise<MCPResponse> {
    69	  await checkDatabaseUpdated();
    70	
    71	  const { id, specFolder, confirm } = args;
    72	  if (!id && !specFolder) throw new Error('Either id or specFolder is required');
    73	  if (specFolder !== undefined && typeof specFolder !== 'string') throw new Error('specFolder must be a string');
    74	  if (specFolder && !id && !confirm) throw new Error('Bulk delete requires confirm: true');
    75	
    76	  let numericId: number | null = null;
    77	  if (id !== undefined && id !== null) {
    78	    numericId = parseMemoryId(id);
    79	  }
    80	
    81	  let deletedCount = 0;
    82	  let checkpointName: string | null = null;
    83	  let mutationLedgerWarning: string | null = null;
    84	  const database = vectorIndex.getDb();
    85	
    86	  if (!database) {
    87	    // Unified DB-unavailable contract for single and bulk delete paths.
    88	    return createDatabaseUnavailableDeleteResponse();
    89	  }
    90	
    91	  if (numericId !== null) {
    92	    const singleSnapshot = getMemoryHashSnapshot(database, numericId);
    93	
    94	    // T2-5 transaction wrapper — wraps single-delete path (memory delete, causal edge
    95	    // Cleanup, ledger append) in a transaction for atomicity on error.
    96	    database.transaction(() => {
    97	      deletedCount = vectorIndex.deleteMemory(numericId) ? 1 : 0;
    98	
    99	      if (deletedCount > 0) {
   100	        // Record DELETE history after confirmed delete (no FK, history rows survive).
   101	        // Placed after deleteMemory to avoid false audit rows for non-existent IDs.
   102	        try {
   103	          recordHistory(
   104	            numericId,
   105	            'DELETE',
   106	            singleSnapshot?.file_path ?? null,
   107	            null,
   108	            'mcp:memory_delete',
   109	            singleSnapshot?.spec_folder ?? null,
   110	          );
   111	        } catch (_histErr: unknown) {
   112	          // History recording is best-effort
   113	        }
   114	
   115	        causalEdges.init(database);
   116	        causalEdges.deleteEdgesForMemory(String(numericId));
   117	        // H1 FIX: Use db-specific invalidation instead of the no-op global version
   118	        clearDegreeCacheForDb(database);
   119	
   120	        const ledgerRecorded = appendMutationLedgerSafe(database, {
   121	          mutationType: 'delete',
   122	          reason: 'memory_delete: single memory delete',
   123	          priorHash: singleSnapshot?.content_hash ?? null,
   124	          newHash: mutationLedger.computeHash(`delete:${numericId}:${Date.now()}`),
   125	          linkedMemoryIds: [numericId],
   126	          decisionMeta: {
   127	            tool: 'memory_delete',
   128	            bulk: false,
   129	            memoryId: numericId,
   130	            specFolder: singleSnapshot?.spec_folder ?? null,
   131	            filePath: singleSnapshot?.file_path ?? null,
   132	          },
   133	          actor: 'mcp:memory_delete',
   134	        });
   135	        if (!ledgerRecorded) {
   136	          mutationLedgerWarning = 'Mutation ledger append failed; audit trail may be incomplete.';
   137	        }
   138	      }
   139	    })();
   140	  } else {
   141	    const memories: { id: number }[] = vectorIndex.getMemoriesByFolder(specFolder as string);
   142	    const deletedIds: number[] = [];
   143	    const hashById = new Map<number, MemoryHashSnapshot>();
   144	
   145	    if (database && 'prepare' in database) {
   146	      try {
   147	        const rows = database.prepare(`
   148	          SELECT id, content_hash, spec_folder, file_path
   149	          FROM memory_index
   150	          WHERE spec_folder = ?
   151	        `).all(specFolder as string) as MemoryHashSnapshot[];
   152	
   153	        for (const row of rows) {
   154	          hashById.set(row.id, row);
   155	        }
   156	      } catch (_err: unknown) {
   157	        // Non-fatal — bulk delete proceeds without per-memory hash snapshots; ledger entries will lack prior hashes.
   158	      }
   159	    }
   160	
   161	    if (memories.length > 0) {
   162	      const timestamp = new Date().toISOString().replace(/[:.]/g, '-').slice(0, 19);
   163	      const proposedCheckpointName = `pre-cleanup-${timestamp}`;
   164	      try {
   165	        const checkpoint = checkpoints.createCheckpoint({
   166	          name: proposedCheckpointName,
   167	          specFolder,
   168	          metadata: {
   169	            reason: 'auto-checkpoint before bulk delete',
   170	            memoryCount: memories.length,
   171	          },
   172	        });
   173	
   174	        if (checkpoint) {
   175	          checkpointName = checkpoint.name;
   176	          console.error(`[memory-delete] Created checkpoint: ${checkpointName}`);
   177	        } else {
   178	          console.warn('[memory-delete] Checkpoint creation returned no checkpoint; proceeding without rollback.');
   179	          checkpointName = null;
   180	        }
   181	      } catch (cpErr: unknown) {
   182	        const message = toErrorMessage(cpErr);
   183	        console.error(`[memory-delete] Failed to create checkpoint: ${message}`);
   184	        // Confirm is always true here (validated at function entry) — safe to proceed without checkpoint.
   185	        console.warn('[memory-delete] Proceeding without backup (user confirmed)');
   186	        checkpointName = null;
   187	      }
   188	    }
   189	
   190	    // Snapshot-then-delete is safe under single-process better-sqlite3; re-evaluate if multi-process support is added
   191	    causalEdges.init(database);
   192	    const bulkDeleteTx = database.transaction(() => {
   193	      for (const memory of memories) {
   194	        if (vectorIndex.deleteMemory(memory.id)) {
   195	          // Record DELETE history after confirmed delete (no FK, history rows survive).
   196	          try {
   197	            const snapshot = hashById.get(memory.id);
   198	            recordHistory(
   199	              memory.id,
   200	              'DELETE',
   201	              snapshot?.file_path ?? null,
   202	              null,
   203	              'mcp:memory_delete',
   204	              snapshot?.spec_folder ?? null,
   205	            );
   206	          } catch (_histErr: unknown) {
   207	            // History recording is best-effort inside bulk delete
   208	          }
   209	          deletedCount++;
   210	          deletedIds.push(memory.id);
   211	          causalEdges.deleteEdgesForMemory(String(memory.id));
   212	        }
   213	      }
   214	
   215	      // Mutation ledger entries written inside bulk transaction for atomicity with deletes.
   216	      for (const deletedId of deletedIds) {
   217	        const snapshot = hashById.get(deletedId) ?? null;
   218	        const ledgerRecorded = appendMutationLedgerSafe(database, {
   219	          mutationType: 'delete',
   220	          reason: 'memory_delete: bulk delete by spec folder',
   221	          priorHash: snapshot?.content_hash ?? null,
   222	          newHash: mutationLedger.computeHash(`bulk-delete:${deletedId}:${Date.now()}`),
   223	          linkedMemoryIds: [deletedId],
   224	          decisionMeta: {
   225	            tool: 'memory_delete',
   226	            bulk: true,
   227	            specFolder,
   228	            checkpoint: checkpointName,
   229	            memoryId: deletedId,
   230	            filePath: snapshot?.file_path ?? null,
   231	          },
   232	          actor: 'mcp:memory_delete',
   233	        });
   234	        if (!ledgerRecorded) {
   235	          mutationLedgerWarning = 'Mutation ledger append failed; audit trail may be incomplete.';
   236	        }
   237	      }
   238	    });
   239	    bulkDeleteTx();
   240	  }
   241	
   242	  let postMutationFeedback: ReturnType<typeof buildMutationHookFeedback> | null = null;
   243	  if (deletedCount > 0) {
   244	    let postMutationHooks: import('./mutation-hooks.js').MutationHookResult;
   245	    try {
   246	      postMutationHooks = runPostMutationHooks('delete', { specFolder, deletedCount });
   247	    } catch (hookError: unknown) {
   248	      const msg = hookError instanceof Error ? hookError.message : String(hookError);
   249	      postMutationHooks = {
   250	        latencyMs: 0, triggerCacheCleared: false,
   251	        constitutionalCacheCleared: false, toolCacheInvalidated: 0,
   252	        graphSignalsCacheCleared: false, coactivationCacheCleared: false,
   253	        errors: [msg],
   254	      };
   255	    }
   256	    postMutationFeedback = buildMutationHookFeedback('delete', postMutationHooks);
   257	  }
   258	
   259	  const summary = deletedCount > 0
   260	    ? `Deleted ${deletedCount} memory(s)`
   261	    : 'No memories found to delete';
   262	
   263	  const hints: string[] = [];
   264	  if (checkpointName) {
   265	    hints.push(`Restore with: checkpoint_restore({ name: "${checkpointName}" })`);
   266	  }
   267	  if (deletedCount === 0) {
   268	    hints.push('Use memory_list() to find existing memories');
   269	  }
   270	  if (postMutationFeedback) {
   271	    hints.push(...postMutationFeedback.hints);
   272	  }
   273	  if (mutationLedgerWarning) {
   274	    hints.push(mutationLedgerWarning);
   275	  }
   276	
   277	  const data: Record<string, unknown> = { deleted: deletedCount };
   278	  if (checkpointName) {
   279	    data.checkpoint = checkpointName;
   280	    data.restoreCommand = `checkpoint_restore({ name: "${checkpointName}" })`;
   281	  }
   282	  if (postMutationFeedback) {
   283	    data.postMutationHooks = postMutationFeedback.data;
   284	  }
   285	  if (mutationLedgerWarning) {
   286	    data.warning = mutationLedgerWarning;
   287	  }
   288	
   289	  return createMCPSuccessResponse({
   290	    tool: 'memory_delete',
   291	    summary,
   292	    data,
   293	    hints,
   294	  });
   295	}
   296	
   297	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   298	   EXPORTS
   299	──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
   300	
   301	export { handleMemoryDelete };
