     1	// ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     2	// MODULE: Memory Crud Update Handler
     3	// ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     4	
     5	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     6	   IMPORTS
     7	──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
     8	
     9	import { randomUUID } from 'node:crypto';
    10	import { checkDatabaseUpdated } from '../core/index.js';
    11	import * as vectorIndex from '../lib/search/vector-index.js';
    12	import type { UpdateMemoryParams } from '../lib/search/vector-index.js';
    13	import * as embeddings from '../lib/providers/embeddings.js';
    14	import * as bm25Index from '../lib/search/bm25-index.js';
    15	import { VALID_TIERS, isValidTier } from '../lib/scoring/importance-tiers.js';
    16	import { MemoryError, ErrorCodes } from '../lib/errors.js';
    17	import * as mutationLedger from '../lib/storage/mutation-ledger.js';
    18	import { runInTransaction } from '../lib/storage/transaction-manager.js';
    19	import { createMCPSuccessResponse, createMCPErrorResponse } from '../lib/response/envelope.js';
    20	import { toErrorMessage } from '../utils/index.js';
    21	
    22	import { recordHistory } from '../lib/storage/history.js';
    23	import { appendMutationLedgerSafe, getMemoryHashSnapshot } from './memory-crud-utils.js';
    24	import { runPostMutationHooks } from './mutation-hooks.js';
    25	import { buildMutationHookFeedback } from '../hooks/mutation-feedback.js';
    26	
    27	import type { MCPResponse } from './types.js';
    28	import type { UpdateArgs } from './memory-crud-types.js';
    29	
    30	// Feature catalog: Memory metadata update (memory_update)
    31	// Feature catalog: Validation feedback (memory_validate)
    32	// Feature catalog: Transaction wrappers on mutation handlers
    33	// Feature catalog: Per-memory history log
    34	
    35	
    36	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    37	   CORE LOGIC
    38	──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
    39	
    40	/** Handle memory_update tool -- updates metadata fields and optionally regenerates embeddings. */
    41	async function handleMemoryUpdate(args: UpdateArgs): Promise<MCPResponse> {
    42	  // A7-P2-1: Generate requestId for incident correlation in error responses
    43	  const requestId = randomUUID();
    44	  await checkDatabaseUpdated();
    45	
    46	  const {
    47	    id,
    48	    title,
    49	    triggerPhrases,
    50	    importanceWeight,
    51	    importanceTier,
    52	    allowPartialUpdate = false,
    53	  } = args;
    54	
    55	  if (typeof id !== 'number') {
    56	    throw new MemoryError(ErrorCodes.MISSING_REQUIRED_PARAM, 'id is required', { param: 'id' });
    57	  }
    58	
    59	  if (importanceWeight !== undefined && (typeof importanceWeight !== 'number' || importanceWeight < 0 || importanceWeight > 1)) {
    60	    throw new MemoryError(
    61	      ErrorCodes.INVALID_PARAMETER,
    62	      'importanceWeight must be a number between 0 and 1',
    63	      { param: 'importanceWeight', value: importanceWeight }
    64	    );
    65	  }
    66	
    67	  if (importanceTier !== undefined && !isValidTier(importanceTier)) {
    68	    throw new MemoryError(
    69	      ErrorCodes.INVALID_PARAMETER,
    70	      `Invalid importance tier: ${importanceTier}. Valid tiers: ${VALID_TIERS.join(', ')}`,
    71	      { param: 'importanceTier', value: importanceTier }
    72	    );
    73	  }
    74	
    75	  const existing = vectorIndex.getMemory(id);
    76	  if (!existing) {
    77	    throw new MemoryError(ErrorCodes.FILE_NOT_FOUND, `Memory not found: ${id}`, { id });
    78	  }
    79	
    80	  const database = vectorIndex.getDb();
    81	  const priorSnapshot = getMemoryHashSnapshot(database, id);
    82	
    83	  const updateParams: UpdateMemoryParams = { id };
    84	  if (title !== undefined) updateParams.title = title;
    85	  if (triggerPhrases !== undefined) updateParams.triggerPhrases = triggerPhrases;
    86	  if (importanceWeight !== undefined) updateParams.importanceWeight = importanceWeight;
    87	  if (importanceTier !== undefined) updateParams.importanceTier = importanceTier;
    88	
    89	  let embeddingRegenerated = false;
    90	  let embeddingMarkedForReindex = false;
    91	  let embeddingStatusNeedsPendingWrite = false;
    92	  let mutationLedgerWarning: string | null = null;
    93	
    94	  if (title !== undefined && title !== existing.title) {
    95	    console.error(`[memory-update] Title changed, regenerating embedding for memory ${id} [requestId=${requestId}]`);
    96	    let newEmbedding: Float32Array | null = null;
    97	
    98	    try {
    99	      // Embed title + content_text, not title alone.
   100	      // This produces better semantic embeddings that capture the full memory context.
   101	      const contentText = existing.content_text || '';
   102	      const embeddingInput = contentText ? `${title}\n\n${contentText}` : title;
   103	      newEmbedding = await embeddings.generateDocumentEmbedding(embeddingInput);
   104	    } catch (err: unknown) {
   105	      const message = toErrorMessage(err);
   106	      if (allowPartialUpdate) {
   107	        console.warn(`[memory-update] Embedding regeneration failed, marking for re-index [requestId=${requestId}]: ${message}`);
   108	        embeddingStatusNeedsPendingWrite = true;
   109	        embeddingMarkedForReindex = true;
   110	      } else {
   111	        console.error(`[memory-update] Embedding regeneration failed, rolling back update [requestId=${requestId}]: ${message}`);
   112	        throw new MemoryError(
   113	          ErrorCodes.EMBEDDING_FAILED,
   114	          'Embedding regeneration failed, update rolled back. No changes were made.',
   115	          { originalError: message, memoryId: id }
   116	        );
   117	      }
   118	    }
   119	
   120	    if (newEmbedding) {
   121	      updateParams.embedding = newEmbedding;
   122	      embeddingRegenerated = true;
   123	    } else if (!embeddingMarkedForReindex) {
   124	      if (allowPartialUpdate) {
   125	        console.warn('[memory-update] Embedding returned null, marking for re-index');
   126	        embeddingStatusNeedsPendingWrite = true;
   127	        embeddingMarkedForReindex = true;
   128	      } else {
   129	        throw new MemoryError(
   130	          ErrorCodes.EMBEDDING_FAILED,
   131	          'Failed to regenerate embedding (null result), update rolled back. No changes were made.',
   132	          { memoryId: id }
   133	        );
   134	      }
   135	    }
   136	  }
   137	
   138	  // T2-5 transaction wrapper — wraps all synchronous mutation steps (DB update,
   139	  // Cache invalidation, BM25 re-index, ledger append) in a single transaction for atomicity.
   140	  // Embedding generation (async) runs before this block; its result feeds into updateParams.
   141	  const fields = Object.keys(updateParams).filter((key) => key !== 'id' && key !== 'embedding');
   142	
   143	  if (database) {
   144	    runInTransaction(database, () => {
   145	      if (embeddingStatusNeedsPendingWrite) {
   146	        vectorIndex.updateEmbeddingStatus(id, 'pending');
   147	      }
   148	
   149	      vectorIndex.updateMemory(updateParams);
   150	
   151	      // T2-6 — BM25 index stores title + trigger phrases; must re-index when either changes
   152	      // So keyword search reflects the updated content.
   153	      // T-05: BM25 re-index failure now rolls back the transaction when the index is operational.
   154	      // Infrastructure failures (BM25 not available, DB missing prepare) are non-fatal warnings.
   155	      if ((updateParams.title !== undefined || updateParams.triggerPhrases !== undefined) && bm25Index.isBm25Enabled()) {
   156	        try {
   157	          const bm25Idx = bm25Index.getIndex();
   158	          const row = database.prepare(
   159	            'SELECT title, content_text, trigger_phrases, file_path FROM memory_index WHERE id = ?'
   160	          ).get(id) as { title: string | null; content_text: string | null; trigger_phrases: string | null; file_path: string | null } | undefined;
   161	          if (row) {
   162	            const text = bm25Index.buildBm25DocumentText(row);
   163	            if (text.trim()) {
   164	              bm25Idx.addDocument(String(id), text);
   165	            }
   166	          }
   167	        } catch (e: unknown) {
   168	          const bm25ErrMsg = e instanceof Error ? e.message : String(e);
   169	          // T-05 + P1-02 fix: Distinguish infrastructure failures from data failures.
   170	          // Infrastructure failures mean the BM25 subsystem is unavailable or torn down —
   171	          // These are non-fatal warnings. Data failures mean the index IS operational but rejected
   172	          // The input — those must re-throw to roll back the transaction.
   173	          const isBm25InfraFailure = (msg: string): boolean =>
   174	            msg.includes('not a function') ||
   175	            msg.includes('not initialized') ||
   176	            msg.includes('Cannot read properties') ||
   177	            msg.includes('is not defined') ||
   178	            msg.includes('database is closed') ||
   179	            msg.includes('no such table');
   180	          if (isBm25InfraFailure(bm25ErrMsg)) {
   181	            console.warn(`[memory-crud-update] BM25 infrastructure unavailable, skipping re-index [requestId=${requestId}]: ${bm25ErrMsg}`);
   182	          } else {
   183	            console.error(`[memory-crud-update] BM25 re-index failed, rolling back update [requestId=${requestId}]: ${bm25ErrMsg}`);
   184	            throw new Error(`BM25 re-index failed: ${bm25ErrMsg}`);
   185	          }
   186	        }
   187	      }
   188	
   189	      // T-05: Record UPDATE history after successful mutation
   190	      try {
   191	        recordHistory(
   192	          id, 'UPDATE',
   193	          existing.title ?? null,
   194	          updateParams.title ?? existing.title ?? null,
   195	          'mcp:memory_update'
   196	        );
   197	      } catch (_histErr: unknown) {
   198	        // History recording is best-effort
   199	      }
   200	
   201	      const ledgerRecorded = appendMutationLedgerSafe(database, {
   202	        mutationType: 'update',
   203	        reason: 'memory_update: metadata update',
   204	        priorHash: priorSnapshot?.content_hash ?? (existing.content_hash as string | null) ?? null,
   205	        newHash: mutationLedger.computeHash(JSON.stringify({
   206	          id,
   207	          title: updateParams.title ?? existing.title ?? null,
   208	          triggerPhrases: updateParams.triggerPhrases ?? null,
   209	          importanceWeight: updateParams.importanceWeight ?? null,
   210	          importanceTier: updateParams.importanceTier ?? null,
   211	        })),
   212	        linkedMemoryIds: [id],
   213	        decisionMeta: {
   214	          tool: 'memory_update',
   215	          fields,
   216	          embeddingRegenerated,
   217	          embeddingMarkedForReindex,
   218	          allowPartialUpdate,
   219	        },
   220	        actor: 'mcp:memory_update',
   221	      });
   222	      if (!ledgerRecorded) {
   223	        mutationLedgerWarning = 'Mutation ledger append failed; audit trail may be incomplete.';
   224	      }
   225	    });
   226	  } else {
   227	    // P1-021 — No database handle means we cannot guarantee transactional
   228	    // Consistency. Abort early rather than risk partial state.
   229	    console.warn('[memory-crud-update] No database handle, aborting update to prevent partial state');
   230	    return createMCPErrorResponse({
   231	      tool: 'memory_update',
   232	      error: `Memory ${id} update aborted: database unavailable`,
   233	      code: 'E_DB_UNAVAILABLE',
   234	      details: { updated: null, fields },
   235	      recovery: {
   236	        hint: 'Restart the MCP server or run memory_index_scan() to reinitialize the database',
   237	        actions: ['Restart the MCP server', 'Call memory_index_scan()'],
   238	        severity: 'error',
   239	      },
   240	    });
   241	  }
   242	
   243	  let postMutationHooks: import('./mutation-hooks.js').MutationHookResult;
   244	  try {
   245	    postMutationHooks = runPostMutationHooks('update', { memoryId: id });
   246	  } catch (hookError: unknown) {
   247	    const msg = hookError instanceof Error ? hookError.message : String(hookError);
   248	    postMutationHooks = {
   249	      latencyMs: 0, triggerCacheCleared: false,
   250	      constitutionalCacheCleared: false, toolCacheInvalidated: 0,
   251	      graphSignalsCacheCleared: false, coactivationCacheCleared: false,
   252	      errors: [msg],
   253	    };
   254	  }
   255	  const postMutationFeedback = buildMutationHookFeedback('update', postMutationHooks);
   256	
   257	  const summary = embeddingMarkedForReindex
   258	    ? `Memory ${id} updated (embedding pending re-index)`
   259	    : `Memory ${id} updated successfully`;
   260	
   261	  const hints: string[] = [];
   262	  if (embeddingMarkedForReindex) {
   263	    hints.push('Run memory_index_scan() to regenerate embeddings');
   264	  }
   265	  if (embeddingRegenerated) {
   266	    hints.push('Embedding regenerated - search results may differ');
   267	  }
   268	  if (mutationLedgerWarning) {
   269	    hints.push(mutationLedgerWarning);
   270	  }
   271	  hints.push(...postMutationFeedback.hints);
   272	
   273	  const data: Record<string, unknown> = {
   274	    updated: id,
   275	    fields,
   276	    embeddingRegenerated,
   277	    postMutationHooks: postMutationFeedback.data,
   278	  };
   279	
   280	  if (embeddingMarkedForReindex) {
   281	    data.warning = 'Embedding regeneration failed, memory marked for re-indexing';
   282	    data.embeddingStatus = 'pending';
   283	  }
   284	  if (mutationLedgerWarning) {
   285	    data.mutationLedgerWarning = mutationLedgerWarning;
   286	  }
   287	
   288	  return createMCPSuccessResponse({
   289	    tool: 'memory_update',
   290	    summary,
   291	    data,
   292	    hints,
   293	  });
   294	}
   295	
   296	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   297	   EXPORTS
   298	──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
   299	
   300	export { handleMemoryUpdate };

===== /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/save/index.ts =====
     1	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     2	// MODULE: Index
     3	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     4	// Re-export barrel consumed by test suites. Production code imports
     5	// from concrete sub-modules (./dedup, ./embedding-pipeline, etc.).
     6	
     7	// Feature catalog: Memory indexing (memory_save)
     8	// Feature catalog: Atomic-save parity and partial-indexing hints
     9	
    10	export type {
    11	  ValidationResult,
    12	  SimilarMemory,
    13	  PeDecision,

===== /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/save/db-helpers.ts =====
     1	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     2	// MODULE: Db Helpers
     3	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     4	import type Database from 'better-sqlite3';
     5	
     6	// Feature catalog: Memory indexing (memory_save)
     7	// Feature catalog: Reconsolidation-on-save
     8	
     9	export {
    10	  ALLOWED_POST_INSERT_COLUMNS,
    11	  applyPostInsertMetadata,
    12	} from '../../lib/storage/post-insert-metadata.js';
    13	
    14	/**
    15	 * TM-06 safety gate: verify a pre-reconsolidation checkpoint exists.
    16	 * Accepts either exact name `pre-reconsolidation` or prefixed variants.
    17	 */
    18	export function hasReconsolidationCheckpoint(database: Database.Database, specFolder: string): boolean {
    19	  try {
    20	    const tableExists = database.prepare(
    21	      "SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='checkpoints'"
    22	    ).get();
    23	
    24	    if (!tableExists) {
    25	      return false;
    26	    }
    27	
    28	    const row = database.prepare(`
    29	      SELECT COUNT(*) AS count
    30	      FROM checkpoints
    31	      WHERE (name = 'pre-reconsolidation' OR name LIKE 'pre-reconsolidation-%')
    32	        AND (spec_folder = ? OR spec_folder IS NULL OR spec_folder = '')
    33	    `).get(specFolder) as { count?: number } | undefined;
    34	
    35	    return (row?.count ?? 0) > 0;
    36	  } catch (error: unknown) {
    37	    console.warn('[db-helpers] hasReconsolidationCheckpoint lookup failed:', error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error));
    38	    return false;
    39	  }
    40	}

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:507:**Phase 13 chunked-save finalization hardening** -- chunked saves now track the created parent and child IDs so finalization can stay transactional. Prediction-error supersede finalization records cross-path `supersedes` edges and marks predecessors superseded inside one transaction. Safe-swap updates now null old-child `parent_id` values before bulk delete inside that same finalization step, and any finalize failure triggers compensating cleanup that removes the staged replacement chunk tree. Parent BM25 mutation is delayed until at least one chunk succeeds and, for safe-swap updates, until finalization completes, which preserves the old parent BM25 state when all chunks fail.

   109	// Create local path validator
   110	const validateFilePathLocal = createFilePathValidator(ALLOWED_BASE_PATHS, validateFilePath);
   111	const MANUAL_FALLBACK_SOURCE_CLASSIFICATION = 'manual-fallback' as const;
   112	
   113	interface PreparedParsedMemory {
   114	  parsed: ReturnType<typeof memoryParser.parseMemoryFile>;
   115	  validation: ReturnType<typeof memoryParser.validateParsedMemory>;
   116	  qualityLoopResult: QualityLoopResult;
   117	  sufficiencyResult: MemorySufficiencyResult;
   118	  templateContract: MemoryTemplateContractResult;
   119	  specDocHealth: SpecDocHealthResult | null;
   120	  finalizedFileContent: string | null;
   121	  sourceClassification: 'template-generated' | typeof MANUAL_FALLBACK_SOURCE_CLASSIFICATION;
   122	}
   123	
   124	type ParsedMemoryWithIndexHints = ReturnType<typeof memoryParser.parseMemoryFile> & {
   125	  _skipIndex?: boolean;
   126	  _vRuleIndexBlockIds?: string[];
   127	};
   128	
   129	const STANDARD_MEMORY_TEMPLATE_MARKERS = [
   130	  '## continue session',
   131	  '## recovery hints',
   132	  '<!-- memory metadata -->',
   133	];
   134	
   135	class VRuleUnavailableError extends Error {
   136	  constructor(message: string) {
   137	    super(message);
   138	    this.name = 'VRuleUnavailableError';
   139	  }
   140	}
   141	
   142	function isVRuleUnavailableResult(value: unknown): value is {
   143	  passed: false;
   144	  status: 'error' | 'warning';
   145	  message: string;
   146	  _unavailable: true;
   147	} {
   148	  return typeof value === 'object'
   149	    && value !== null
   150	    && 'passed' in value
   151	    && (value as { passed?: unknown }).passed === false
   152	    && 'status' in value
   153	    && typeof (value as { status?: unknown }).status === 'string'
   154	    && 'message' in value
   155	    && typeof (value as { message?: unknown }).message === 'string';
   156	}
   157	
   158	function classifyMemorySaveSource(
   159	  content: string,
   160	): 'template-generated' | typeof MANUAL_FALLBACK_SOURCE_CLASSIFICATION {
   161	  const normalizedContent = content.toLowerCase();
   162	  const hasAnyStandardMarker = STANDARD_MEMORY_TEMPLATE_MARKERS.some((marker) => normalizedContent.includes(marker));
   163	  return hasAnyStandardMarker ? 'template-generated' : MANUAL_FALLBACK_SOURCE_CLASSIFICATION;
   164	}
   165	
   166	function shouldBypassTemplateContract(
   167	  sourceClassification: PreparedParsedMemory['sourceClassification'],
   168	  sufficiencyResult: MemorySufficiencyResult,
   169	  templateContract: MemoryTemplateContractResult,
   170	): boolean {
   171	  return sourceClassification === MANUAL_FALLBACK_SOURCE_CLASSIFICATION
   172	    && sufficiencyResult.pass
   173	    && sufficiencyResult.evidenceCounts.primary === 0
   174	    && sufficiencyResult.evidenceCounts.support >= 3
   175	    && sufficiencyResult.evidenceCounts.anchors >= 1
   176	    && !templateContract.valid;
   177	}
   178	
   179	function buildQualityLoopMetadata(
   180	  parsed: ReturnType<typeof memoryParser.parseMemoryFile>,
   181	  database: ReturnType<typeof requireDb>,
   182	): Record<string, unknown> {
   183	  return {
   184	    title: parsed.title ?? '',
   185	    triggerPhrases: parsed.triggerPhrases,
   186	    specFolder: parsed.specFolder,
   187	    contextType: parsed.contextType,
   188	    importanceTier: parsed.importanceTier,
   189	    causalLinks: parsed.causalLinks,
   190	    filePath: parsed.filePath,
   191	    lastModified: parsed.lastModified,
   192	    resolveReference: (reference: string) => resolveMemoryReference(database, reference) !== null,
   193	  };
   194	}
   195	
   196	function prepareParsedMemoryForIndexing(
   197	  parsed: ReturnType<typeof memoryParser.parseMemoryFile>,
   198	  database: ReturnType<typeof requireDb>,
   199	  options: {
   200	    emitEvalMetrics?: boolean;
   201	  } = {},
   202	): PreparedParsedMemory {
   203	  const validation = memoryParser.validateParsedMemory(parsed);
   204	  if (validation.warnings && validation.warnings.length > 0) {
   205	    console.warn(`[memory] Warning for ${path.basename(parsed.filePath)}:`);
   206	    validation.warnings.forEach((warning: string) => console.warn(`[memory]   - ${warning}`));
   207	  }
   208	
   209	  // O2-5/O2-12: Run V-rule validation (previously only in workflow path)
   210	  const vRuleResult = validateMemoryQualityContent(parsed.content, { filePath: parsed.filePath });
   211	  if (isVRuleUnavailableResult(vRuleResult) && vRuleResult.status === 'error') {
   212	    throw new VRuleUnavailableError(vRuleResult.message);
   213	  }
   214	  if (vRuleResult && '_unavailable' in vRuleResult) {
   215	    validation.warnings.push('V-rule validator module unavailable — quality gate bypassed. Save proceeds without V-rule enforcement.');
   216	  }
   217	  if (vRuleResult && !isVRuleUnavailableResult(vRuleResult) && !vRuleResult.valid) {
   218	    const vRuleDisposition = determineValidationDisposition(
   219	      vRuleResult.failedRules,
   220	      parsed.memoryTypeSource || null,
   221	    );
   222	    if (vRuleDisposition && vRuleDisposition.disposition === 'abort_write') {
   223	      const failedRuleIds = vRuleDisposition.blockingRuleIds;
   224	      console.error(`[memory-save] V-rule hard block for ${path.basename(parsed.filePath)}: ${failedRuleIds.join(', ')}`);
   225	      // Return early with a rejected quality loop result so callers see the block
   226	      const rejectScore = { total: 0, breakdown: { triggers: 0, anchors: 0, budget: 0, coherence: 0 }, issues: [`V-rule hard block: ${failedRuleIds.join(', ')}`] };
   227	      return {
   228	        parsed,
   229	        validation,
   230	        qualityLoopResult: {
   231	          passed: false,
   232	          score: rejectScore,
   233	          attempts: 0,
   234	          fixes: [],
   235	          rejected: true,
   236	          rejectionReason: `V-rule hard block: ${failedRuleIds.join(', ')}`,
   237	        },
   238	        sufficiencyResult: {
   239	          pass: false,
   240	          rejectionCode: MEMORY_SUFFICIENCY_REJECTION_CODE,
   241	          score: 0,
   242	          reasons: [`V-rule hard block: ${failedRuleIds.join(', ')}`],
   243	          evidenceCounts: { primary: 0, support: 0, total: 0, semanticChars: 0, uniqueWords: 0, anchors: 0, triggerPhrases: 0 },
   244	        },
   245	        templateContract: { valid: false, violations: [], missingAnchors: [], unexpectedTemplateArtifacts: [] } as MemoryTemplateContractResult,
   246	        specDocHealth: null,
   247	        finalizedFileContent: null,
   248	        sourceClassification: 'template-generated',
   249	      };
   250	    }
   251	    if (vRuleDisposition && vRuleDisposition.disposition === 'write_skip_index') {
   252	      console.warn(`[memory-save] V-rule index block for ${path.basename(parsed.filePath)}: ${vRuleDisposition.indexBlockingRuleIds.join(', ')}`);
   253	      validation.warnings.push(`V-rule index block: ${vRuleDisposition.indexBlockingRuleIds.join(', ')}`);
   254	      // F07-002: Flag to skip indexing for write_skip_index disposition
   255	      const parsedWithIndexHints = parsed as ParsedMemoryWithIndexHints;
   256	      parsedWithIndexHints._skipIndex = true;
   257	      parsedWithIndexHints._vRuleIndexBlockIds = vRuleDisposition.indexBlockingRuleIds;
   258	    }
   259	  }
   260	
   261	  const qualityLoopResult = runQualityLoop(parsed.content, buildQualityLoopMetadata(parsed, database), {
   262	    emitEvalMetrics: options.emitEvalMetrics,
   263	  });
   264	  parsed.qualityScore = qualityLoopResult.score.total;
   265	  parsed.qualityFlags = qualityLoopResult.score.issues;
   266	  if (qualityLoopResult.fixedTriggerPhrases) {
   267	    parsed.triggerPhrases = qualityLoopResult.fixedTriggerPhrases;
   268	  }
   269	  const finalizedFileContent = qualityLoopResult.fixedContent
   270	    && qualityLoopResult.passed
   271	    ? qualityLoopResult.fixedContent
   272	    : null;
   273	  if (finalizedFileContent) {
   274	    parsed.content = finalizedFileContent;
   275	    parsed.contentHash = memoryParser.computeContentHash(parsed.content);
   276	  }
   277	
   278	  const sourceClassification = classifyMemorySaveSource(parsed.content);
   279	  if (sourceClassification === MANUAL_FALLBACK_SOURCE_CLASSIFICATION) {
   280	    const warning = 'Manual fallback save mode detected; standard generate-context template markers are missing.';
   281	    console.warn(`[memory-save] ${warning} ${path.basename(parsed.filePath)}`);
   282	    validation.warnings.push(warning);
   283	  }
   284	
   285	  const sufficiencyResult = evaluateMemorySufficiency(
   286	    {
   287	      ...buildParsedMemoryEvidenceSnapshot(parsed),
   288	      sourceClassification,
   289	    },
   290	  );
   291	  applyInsufficiencyMetadata(parsed, sufficiencyResult);
   292	  const templateContract = validateMemoryTemplateContract(parsed.content);
   293	
   294	  // Non-blocking spec doc health annotation
   295	  // parsed.specFolder is a relative key (e.g., "system-spec-kit/100-feature"),
   296	  // so resolve the absolute spec folder by walking up from the memory file path.
   297	  let specDocHealth: SpecDocHealthResult | null = null;
   298	  if (parsed.specFolder && parsed.filePath) {
   299	    try {
   300	      const absFilePath = path.resolve(parsed.filePath);
   301	      // Memory files live at .../NNN-name/memory/*.md — walk up to the spec folder
   302	      const memoryDir = path.dirname(absFilePath);
   303	      const parentDir = path.dirname(memoryDir);
   304	      // Verify this is actually a spec folder (has spec.md)
   305	      const specMdPath = path.join(parentDir, 'spec.md');
   306	      if (fs.existsSync(specMdPath)) {
   307	        specDocHealth = evaluateSpecDocHealth(parentDir);
   308	      }
   309	    } catch (error: unknown) {
   310	      const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
   311	      console.warn(
   312	        `[memory-save] spec-doc-health annotation skipped for ${path.basename(parsed.filePath)}: ${message}`
   313	      );
   314	    }
   315	  }
   316	
   317	  return {
   318	    parsed,
   319	    validation,
   320	    qualityLoopResult,
   321	    sufficiencyResult,
   322	    templateContract,
   323	    specDocHealth,
   324	    finalizedFileContent,
   325	    sourceClassification,
   326	  };
   327	}
   328	
   329	async function finalizeMemoryFileContent(
   330	  filePath: string,
   331	  content: string,
   332	): Promise<void> {
   333	  const backupPath = `${filePath}.${randomUUID().slice(0, 8)}.bak`;
   334	  const tempPath = `${filePath}.${randomUUID().slice(0, 8)}.tmp`;
   335	  let backupCreated = false;
   336	  let tempCreated = false;
   337	
   338	  try {
   339	    try {
   340	      await fs.promises.copyFile(filePath, backupPath);
===== /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/save/dedup.ts =====
     1	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     2	// MODULE: Dedup
     3	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     4	import fs from 'fs';
     5	import type Database from 'better-sqlite3';
     6	
     7	import type { ParsedMemory } from '../../lib/parsing/memory-parser.js';
     8	import type { IndexResult } from './types.js';
     9	
    10	// Feature catalog: SHA-256 content-hash deduplication
    11	// Feature catalog: Canonical ID dedup hardening
    12	// Feature catalog: Generation-time duplicate and empty content prevention
    13	
    14	
    15	const UNCHANGED_EMBEDDING_STATUSES = new Set(['success', 'pending', 'partial']);
    16	const DEDUP_ELIGIBLE_EMBEDDING_STATUSES = ['success', 'partial'] as const;
    17	const QUALITY_SCORE_EPSILON = 1e-9;
    18	const SCOPE_COLUMNS = [
    19	  ['tenant_id', 'tenantId'],
    20	  ['user_id', 'userId'],
    21	  ['agent_id', 'agentId'],
    22	  ['session_id', 'sessionId'],
    23	  ['shared_space_id', 'sharedSpaceId'],
    24	] as const;
    25	
    26	interface SamePathDedupExclusion {
    27	  canonicalFilePath: string;
    28	  filePath: string;
    29	}
    30	
    31	import type { MemoryScopeMatch } from './types.js';
    32	import { normalizeScopeMatchValue } from './types.js';
    33	
    34	type ScopeColumnName = typeof SCOPE_COLUMNS[number][0];
    35	
    36	interface LatestMemoryLookupRow {
    37	  id: number;
    38	  content_hash: string;
    39	  embedding_status: string | null;
    40	  trigger_phrases: string | null;
    41	  quality_score: number | null;
    42	  quality_flags: string | null;
    43	}
    44	
    45	interface DuplicateLookupRow {
    46	  id: number;
    47	  file_path: string;
    48	  title: string | null;
    49	  content_text?: string | null;
    50	}
    51	
    52	function buildScopedWhereClauses(scope: MemoryScopeMatch): {
    53	  clauses: string[];
    54	  params: Array<string>;
    55	} {
    56	  const normalizedScope: MemoryScopeMatch = {
    57	    tenantId: normalizeScopeMatchValue(scope.tenantId),
    58	    userId: normalizeScopeMatchValue(scope.userId),
    59	    agentId: normalizeScopeMatchValue(scope.agentId),
    60	    sessionId: normalizeScopeMatchValue(scope.sessionId),
    61	    sharedSpaceId: normalizeScopeMatchValue(scope.sharedSpaceId),
    62	  };
    63	  const clauses: string[] = [];
    64	  const params: string[] = [];
    65	
    66	  for (const [column, key] of SCOPE_COLUMNS) {
    67	    const value = normalizedScope[key];
    68	    if (value === null || value === undefined) {
    69	      clauses.push(`${column} IS NULL`);
    70	      continue;
    71	    }
    72	    clauses.push(`${column} = ?`);
    73	    params.push(value);
    74	  }
    75	
    76	  return { clauses, params };
    77	}
    78	
    79	function selectLatestExistingRow(
    80	  database: Database.Database,
    81	  parsed: ParsedMemory,
    82	  pathColumn: ScopeColumnName | 'canonical_file_path' | 'file_path',
    83	  pathValue: string,
    84	  scopeClauses: string[],
    85	  scopeParams: string[],
    86	): LatestMemoryLookupRow | undefined {
    87	  const whereClauses = [
    88	    'spec_folder = ?',
    89	    'parent_id IS NULL',
    90	    `${pathColumn} = ?`,
    91	    ...scopeClauses,
    92	  ];
    93	
    94	  return database.prepare(`
    95	    SELECT id, content_hash, embedding_status, trigger_phrases, quality_score, quality_flags
    96	    FROM memory_index
    97	    WHERE ${whereClauses.join('\n      AND ')}
    98	    ORDER BY id DESC
    99	    LIMIT 1
   100	  `).get(
   101	    parsed.specFolder,
   102	    pathValue,
   103	    ...scopeParams,
   104	  ) as LatestMemoryLookupRow | undefined;
   105	}
   106	
   107	function parseJsonStringArray(raw: string | null): string[] {
   108	  if (!raw) {
   109	    return [];
   110	  }
   111	
   112	  try {
   113	    const parsed = JSON.parse(raw) as unknown;
   114	    return Array.isArray(parsed)
   115	      ? parsed.filter((value): value is string => typeof value === 'string')
   116	      : [];
   117	  } catch {
   118	    return [];
   119	  }
   120	}
   121	
   122	function normalizeStringArray(values: string[]): string[] {
   123	  return [...new Set(values.map(value => value.trim()).filter(Boolean))].sort();
   124	}
   125	
   126	function areEquivalentStringArrays(left: string[], right: string[]): boolean {
   127	  const normalizedLeft = normalizeStringArray(left);
   128	  const normalizedRight = normalizeStringArray(right);
   129	
   130	  if (normalizedLeft.length !== normalizedRight.length) {
   131	    return false;
   132	  }
   133	
   134	  return normalizedLeft.every((value, index) => value === normalizedRight[index]);
   135	}
   136	
   137	function isSamePathMetadataEquivalent(
   138	  existing: {
   139	    trigger_phrases: string | null;
   140	    quality_score: number | null;
   141	    quality_flags: string | null;
   142	  },
   143	  parsed: ParsedMemory,
   144	): boolean {
   145	  const persistedTriggerPhrases = parseJsonStringArray(existing.trigger_phrases);
   146	  if (!areEquivalentStringArrays(persistedTriggerPhrases, parsed.triggerPhrases)) {
   147	    return false;
   148	  }
   149	
   150	  const persistedQualityFlags = parseJsonStringArray(existing.quality_flags);
   151	  if (!areEquivalentStringArrays(persistedQualityFlags, parsed.qualityFlags ?? [])) {
   152	    return false;
   153	  }
   154	
   155	  const persistedQualityScore = existing.quality_score ?? 0;
   156	  const parsedQualityScore = parsed.qualityScore ?? 0;
   157	  return Math.abs(persistedQualityScore - parsedQualityScore) <= QUALITY_SCORE_EPSILON;
   158	}
   159	
   160	function verifyStoredContentMatch(
   161	  storedContent: string | null | undefined,
   162	  storedPath: string | null | undefined,
   163	  incomingContent: string,
   164	): boolean | null {
   165	  if (typeof storedContent === 'string') {
   166	    return storedContent === incomingContent;
   167	  }
   168	
   169	  if (typeof storedPath === 'string' && storedPath.length > 0) {
   170	    try {
   171	      if (fs.existsSync(storedPath)) {
   172	        return fs.readFileSync(storedPath, 'utf-8') === incomingContent;
   173	      }
   174	    } catch {
   175	      return null;
   176	    }
   177	  }
   178	
   179	  return null;
   180	}
   181	
   182	export function checkExistingRow(
   183	  database: Database.Database,
   184	  parsed: ParsedMemory,
   185	  canonicalFilePath: string,
   186	  filePath: string,
   187	  force: boolean,
   188	  warnings: string[] | undefined,
   189	  scope: MemoryScopeMatch = {},
   190	): IndexResult | null {
   191	  const { clauses: scopeClauses, params: scopeParams } = buildScopedWhereClauses(scope);
   192	  const candidates = [
   193	    selectLatestExistingRow(
   194	      database,
   195	      parsed,
   196	      'canonical_file_path',
   197	      canonicalFilePath,
   198	      scopeClauses,
   199	      scopeParams,
   200	    ),
   201	  ];
   202	
   203	  if (filePath !== canonicalFilePath) {
   204	    candidates.push(
   205	      selectLatestExistingRow(
   206	        database,
   207	        parsed,
   208	        'file_path',
   209	        filePath,
   210	        scopeClauses,
   211	        scopeParams,
   212	      ),
   213	    );
   214	  }
   215	
   216	  const existing = candidates
   217	    .filter((candidate): candidate is LatestMemoryLookupRow => candidate !== undefined)
   218	    .sort((left, right) => right.id - left.id)[0];
   219	
   220	  const existingStatus = existing?.embedding_status ?? null;
   221	  const isUnchangedEligible = existingStatus !== null && UNCHANGED_EMBEDDING_STATUSES.has(existingStatus);
   222	  const isMetadataEquivalent = existing
   223	    ? isSamePathMetadataEquivalent(existing, parsed)
   224	    : false;
   225	
   226	  // P1-4 FIX: Check content hash even during force reindex to prevent duplicate
   227	  // row accumulation. If content AND metadata are identical, the embedding would
   228	  // not change either, so re-indexing provides no value and creates duplicates.
   229	  if (existing && existing.content_hash === parsed.contentHash && isUnchangedEligible && isMetadataEquivalent) {
   230	    return {
   231	      status: 'unchanged',
   232	      id: existing.id,
   233	      specFolder: parsed.specFolder,
   234	      title: parsed.title ?? '',
   235	      triggerPhrases: parsed.triggerPhrases,
   236	      contextType: parsed.contextType,
   237	      importanceTier: parsed.importanceTier,
   238	      warnings,
   239	    };
   240	  }
   241	
   242	  return null;
   243	}
   244	
   245	export function checkContentHashDedup(
   246	  database: Database.Database,
   247	  parsed: ParsedMemory,
   248	  force: boolean,
   249	  warnings: string[] | undefined,
   250	  samePathExclusion?: SamePathDedupExclusion,
   251	  scope: MemoryScopeMatch = {},
   252	): IndexResult | null {
   253	  if (!force) {
   254	    const { clauses: scopeClauses, params: scopeParams } = buildScopedWhereClauses(scope);
   255	    const whereClauses = [
   256	      'spec_folder = ?',
   257	      'content_hash = ?',
   258	      'parent_id IS NULL',
   259	      'embedding_status IN (?, ?)',
   260	      ...scopeClauses,
   261	    ];
   262	    const duplicateParams: Array<string> = [
   263	      parsed.specFolder,
   264	      parsed.contentHash,
   265	      ...DEDUP_ELIGIBLE_EMBEDDING_STATUSES,
   266	      ...scopeParams,
   267	    ];
   268	
   269	    if (samePathExclusion) {
   270	      whereClauses.push('file_path != ?');
   271	      duplicateParams.push(samePathExclusion.filePath);
   272	      whereClauses.push('(canonical_file_path IS NULL OR canonical_file_path != ?)');
   273	      duplicateParams.push(samePathExclusion.canonicalFilePath);
   274	    }
   275	
   276	    const duplicateByHash = database.prepare(`
   277	      SELECT id, file_path, title, content_text
   278	      FROM memory_index
   279	      WHERE ${whereClauses.join('\n        AND ')}
   280	      ORDER BY id DESC
   281	      LIMIT 1
   282	    `).get(...duplicateParams) as DuplicateLookupRow | undefined;
   283	
   284	    if (duplicateByHash) {
   285	      const verifiedMatch = verifyStoredContentMatch(
   286	        duplicateByHash.content_text,
   287	        duplicateByHash.file_path,
   288	        parsed.content,
   289	      );
   290	      if (verifiedMatch === false) {
   291	        console.warn(`[memory-save] Hash match for memory #${duplicateByHash.id} failed secondary content verification; continuing save`);
   292	        return null;
   293	      }
   294	      console.error(`[memory-save] T054: Duplicate content detected (hash match id=${duplicateByHash.id}), skipping embedding`);
   295	      return {
   296	        status: 'duplicate',
   297	        id: duplicateByHash.id,
   298	        specFolder: parsed.specFolder,
   299	        title: parsed.title ?? duplicateByHash.title ?? '',
   300	        triggerPhrases: parsed.triggerPhrases,
   301	        contextType: parsed.contextType,
   302	        importanceTier: parsed.importanceTier,
   303	        warnings,
   304	        message: `Duplicate content detected: identical to existing memory #${duplicateByHash.id} (${duplicateByHash.file_path}). Skipping embedding generation.`,
   305	      };
   306	    }
   307	  }
   308	
   309	  return null;
   310	}
===== /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/save/create-record.ts =====
     1	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     2	// MODULE: Create Record
     3	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     4	import path from 'path';
     5	import type BetterSqlite3 from 'better-sqlite3';
     6	
     7	import * as vectorIndex from '../../lib/search/vector-index.js';
     8	import * as bm25Index from '../../lib/search/bm25-index.js';
     9	import * as predictionErrorGate from '../../lib/cognitive/prediction-error-gate.js';
    10	import * as fsrsScheduler from '../../lib/cognitive/fsrs-scheduler.js';
    11	import * as incrementalIndex from '../../lib/storage/incremental-index.js';
    12	import * as causalEdges from '../../lib/storage/causal-edges.js';
    13	import type * as memoryParser from '../../lib/parsing/memory-parser.js';
    14	import { sanitizeEmbeddingFailureMessage } from '../../lib/providers/retry-manager.js';
    15	import { getCanonicalPathKey } from '../../lib/utils/canonical-path.js';
    16	import { recordLineageTransition } from '../../lib/storage/lineage-state.js';
    17	import { toErrorMessage } from '../../utils/index.js';
    18	
    19	import { recordHistory } from '../../lib/storage/history.js';
    20	import { calculateDocumentWeight, isSpecDocumentType } from '../pe-gating.js';
    21	import { detectSpecLevelFromParsed } from '../handler-utils.js';
    22	import { classifyEncodingIntent } from '../../lib/search/encoding-intent.js';
    23	import { isEncodingIntentEnabled } from '../../lib/search/search-flags.js';
    24	import { applyPostInsertMetadata } from './db-helpers.js';
    25	
    26	// Feature catalog: Memory indexing (memory_save)
    27	// Feature catalog: Per-memory history log
    28	// Feature catalog: Prediction-error save arbitration
    29	
    30	
    31	import type { PeDecision, MemoryScopeMatch } from './types.js';
    32	import { normalizeScopeMatchValue } from './types.js';
    33	export type { MemoryScopeMatch };
    34	
    35	interface LineageRoutingDecision {
    36	  predecessorMemoryId: number | null;
    37	  transitionEvent: 'CREATE' | 'UPDATE' | 'SUPERSEDE';
    38	  causalSupersedesMemoryId: number | null;
    39	}
    40	
    41	export function resolveCreateRecordLineage(
    42	  peDecision: PeDecision,
    43	  samePathExistingId: number | null,
    44	): LineageRoutingDecision {
    45	  if (peDecision.action === predictionErrorGate.ACTION.SUPERSEDE && peDecision.existingMemoryId != null) {
    46	    return {
    47	      predecessorMemoryId: samePathExistingId,
    48	      transitionEvent: samePathExistingId != null ? 'SUPERSEDE' : 'CREATE',
    49	      causalSupersedesMemoryId: samePathExistingId === peDecision.existingMemoryId
    50	        ? null
    51	        : peDecision.existingMemoryId,
    52	    };
    53	  }
    54	
    55	  if (samePathExistingId != null) {
    56	    return {
    57	      predecessorMemoryId: samePathExistingId,
    58	      transitionEvent: 'UPDATE',
    59	      causalSupersedesMemoryId: null,
    60	    };
    61	  }
    62	
    63	  return {
    64	    predecessorMemoryId: null,
    65	    transitionEvent: 'CREATE',
    66	    causalSupersedesMemoryId: null,
    67	  };
    68	}
    69	
    70	function recordCrossPathSupersedesEdge(
    71	  database: BetterSqlite3.Database,
    72	  memoryId: number,
    73	  supersededMemoryId: number | null,
    74	  reason: string | null | undefined,
    75	): void {
    76	  if (supersededMemoryId == null) {
    77	    return;
    78	  }
    79	
    80	  causalEdges.init(database);
    81	  const evidence = reason && reason.trim().length > 0
    82	    ? reason.trim()
    83	    : 'Prediction-error contradiction across different file paths';
    84	  causalEdges.insertEdge(
    85	    String(memoryId),
    86	    String(supersededMemoryId),
    87	    causalEdges.RELATION_TYPES.SUPERSEDES,
    88	    1.0,
    89	    evidence,
    90	    true,
    91	    'auto',
    92	  );
    93	}
    94	
    95	export function findSamePathExistingMemory(
    96	  database: BetterSqlite3.Database,
    97	  specFolder: string,
    98	  canonicalFilePath: string,
    99	  filePath: string,
   100	  scope: MemoryScopeMatch = {},
   101	): { id: number; title: string | null; content_hash?: string | null } | undefined {
   102	  const tenantId = normalizeScopeMatchValue(scope.tenantId);
   103	  const userId = normalizeScopeMatchValue(scope.userId);
   104	  const agentId = normalizeScopeMatchValue(scope.agentId);
   105	  const sessionId = normalizeScopeMatchValue(scope.sessionId);
   106	  const sharedSpaceId = normalizeScopeMatchValue(scope.sharedSpaceId);
   107	
   108	  return database.prepare(`
   109	    SELECT id, title, content_hash
   110	    FROM memory_index
   111	    WHERE spec_folder = ?
   112	      AND parent_id IS NULL
   113	      AND (canonical_file_path = ? OR file_path = ?)
   114	      AND ((? IS NULL AND tenant_id IS NULL) OR tenant_id = ?)
   115	      AND ((? IS NULL AND user_id IS NULL) OR user_id = ?)
   116	      AND ((? IS NULL AND agent_id IS NULL) OR agent_id = ?)
   117	      AND ((? IS NULL AND session_id IS NULL) OR session_id = ?)
   118	      AND ((? IS NULL AND shared_space_id IS NULL) OR shared_space_id = ?)
   119	    ORDER BY id DESC
   120	    LIMIT 1
   121	  `).get(
   122	    specFolder,
   123	    canonicalFilePath,
   124	    filePath,
   125	    tenantId,
   126	    tenantId,
   127	    userId,
   128	    userId,
   129	    agentId,
   130	    agentId,
   131	    sessionId,
   132	    sessionId,
   133	    sharedSpaceId,
   134	    sharedSpaceId,
   135	  ) as { id: number; title: string | null; content_hash?: string | null } | undefined;
   136	}
   137	
   138	/**
   139	 * Creates a memory row with metadata, optional BM25 entry, and save history.
   140	 * Returns the persisted memory id for downstream save handlers.
   141	 */
   142	export function createMemoryRecord(
   143	  database: BetterSqlite3.Database,
   144	  parsed: ReturnType<typeof memoryParser.parseMemoryFile>,
   145	  filePath: string,
   146	  embedding: Float32Array | null,
   147	  embeddingFailureReason: string | null,
   148	  peDecision: PeDecision,
   149	  scope: MemoryScopeMatch = {},
   150	): number {
   151	  if (!embedding) {
   152	    console.error(`[memory-save] Using deferred indexing for ${path.basename(filePath)}`);
   153	  }
   154	
   155	  // Detect spec level for spec documents.
   156	  const specLevel = isSpecDocumentType(parsed.documentType)
   157	    ? detectSpecLevelFromParsed(filePath)
   158	    : null;
   159	  const encodingIntent = isEncodingIntentEnabled()
   160	    ? classifyEncodingIntent(parsed.content)
   161	    : undefined;
   162	  const canonicalFilePath = getCanonicalPathKey(filePath);
   163	  // Security: raw provider errors sanitized before persistence/response
   164	  const persistedEmbeddingFailureReason = sanitizeEmbeddingFailureMessage(embeddingFailureReason);
   165	
   166	  const indexWithMetadata = database.transaction(() => {
   167	    // Determine importance weight based on document type.
   168	    const importanceWeight = calculateDocumentWeight(filePath, parsed.documentType);
   169	    const samePathExisting = findSamePathExistingMemory(
   170	      database,
   171	      parsed.specFolder,
   172	      canonicalFilePath,
   173	      filePath,
   174	      scope,
   175	    );
   176	    const lineageRouting = resolveCreateRecordLineage(peDecision, samePathExisting?.id ?? null);
   177	    const predecessorMemoryId = lineageRouting.predecessorMemoryId;
   178	    const transitionEvent = lineageRouting.transitionEvent;
   179	
   180	    const memory_id: number = embedding
   181	      ? vectorIndex.indexMemory({
   182	          specFolder: parsed.specFolder,
   183	          filePath,
   184	          title: parsed.title,
   185	          triggerPhrases: parsed.triggerPhrases,
   186	          importanceWeight,
   187	          embedding,
   188	          encodingIntent,
   189	          documentType: parsed.documentType || 'memory',
   190	          specLevel,
   191	          contentText: parsed.content,
   192	          qualityScore: parsed.qualityScore,
   193	          qualityFlags: parsed.qualityFlags,
   194	          appendOnly: predecessorMemoryId != null,
   195	        })
   196	      : vectorIndex.indexMemoryDeferred({
   197	          specFolder: parsed.specFolder,
   198	          filePath,
   199	          title: parsed.title,
   200	          triggerPhrases: parsed.triggerPhrases,
   201	          importanceWeight,
   202	          failureReason: persistedEmbeddingFailureReason,
   203	          encodingIntent,
   204	          documentType: parsed.documentType || 'memory',
   205	          specLevel,
   206	          contentText: parsed.content,
   207	          qualityScore: parsed.qualityScore,
   208	          qualityFlags: parsed.qualityFlags,
   209	          appendOnly: predecessorMemoryId != null,
   210	        });
   211	
   212	    const fileMetadata = incrementalIndex.getFileMetadata(filePath);
   213	    const fileMtimeMs = fileMetadata ? fileMetadata.mtime : null;
   214	
   215	    applyPostInsertMetadata(database, memory_id, {
   216	      content_hash: parsed.contentHash,
   217	      context_type: parsed.contextType,
   218	      importance_tier: parsed.importanceTier,
   219	      memory_type: parsed.memoryType,
   220	      type_inference_source: parsed.memoryTypeSource,
   221	      stability: fsrsScheduler.DEFAULT_INITIAL_STABILITY,
   222	      difficulty: fsrsScheduler.DEFAULT_INITIAL_DIFFICULTY,
   223	      file_mtime_ms: fileMtimeMs,
   224	      encoding_intent: encodingIntent,
   225	      document_type: parsed.documentType || 'memory',
   226	      spec_level: specLevel,
   227	      quality_score: parsed.qualityScore ?? 0,
   228	      quality_flags: JSON.stringify(parsed.qualityFlags ?? []),
   229	    });
   230	
   231	    if (embedding && peDecision.action === predictionErrorGate.ACTION.CREATE_LINKED && peDecision.existingMemoryId != null) {
   232	      try {
   233	        database.prepare(`
   234	          UPDATE memory_index
   235	          SET related_memories = ?
   236	          WHERE id = ?
   237	        `).run(JSON.stringify([peDecision.existingMemoryId]), memory_id);
   238	      } catch (err: unknown) {
   239	        const message = toErrorMessage(err);
   240	        console.error('[PE-Gate] Could not store related memories:', message);
   241	      }
   242	    }
   243	
   244	    if (predecessorMemoryId != null) {
   245	      database.prepare(`
   246	        UPDATE memory_index
   247	        SET importance_tier = 'deprecated',
   248	            updated_at = ?
   249	        WHERE id = ?
   250	      `).run(new Date().toISOString(), predecessorMemoryId);
   251	    }
   252	
   253	    recordLineageTransition(database, memory_id, {
   254	      actor: 'mcp:memory_save',
   255	      predecessorMemoryId,
   256	      transitionEvent,
   257	    });
   258	    recordCrossPathSupersedesEdge(
   259	      database,
   260	      memory_id,
   261	      lineageRouting.causalSupersedesMemoryId,
   262	      peDecision.reason,
   263	    );
   264	
   265	    if (bm25Index.isBm25Enabled()) {
   266	      try {
   267	        const bm25 = bm25Index.getIndex();
   268	        bm25.addDocument(String(memory_id), bm25Index.buildBm25DocumentText({
   269	          title: parsed.title,
   270	          content_text: parsed.content,
   271	          trigger_phrases: parsed.triggerPhrases,
   272	          file_path: filePath,
   273	        }));
   274	      } catch (bm25_err: unknown) {
   275	        const message = toErrorMessage(bm25_err);
   276	        console.warn(embedding
   277	          ? `[memory-save] BM25 indexing failed: ${message}`
   278	          : `[memory-save] BM25 indexing failed (deferred path): ${message}`);
   279	      }
   280	    }
   281	
   282	    // Append-first writes add a new row for every new current version.
   283	    try {
   284	      recordHistory(memory_id, 'ADD', null, parsed.title ?? filePath, 'mcp:memory_save');
   285	      if (predecessorMemoryId != null) {
   286	        recordHistory(
   287	          predecessorMemoryId,
   288	          'UPDATE',
   289	          samePathExisting?.title ?? null,
   290	          parsed.title ?? filePath,
   291	          'mcp:memory_save',
   292	        );
   293	      }
   294	    } catch (_histErr: unknown) {
   295	      // History recording is best-effort during save
   296	    }
   297	
   298	    return memory_id;
   299	  });
   300	
   301	  return indexWithMetadata();
   302	}
===== /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/save/reconsolidation-bridge.ts =====
     1	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     2	// MODULE: Reconsolidation Bridge
     3	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     4	import path from 'path';
     5	import type BetterSqlite3 from 'better-sqlite3';
     6	
     7	import * as vectorIndex from '../../lib/search/vector-index.js';
     8	import * as embeddings from '../../lib/providers/embeddings.js';
     9	import * as bm25Index from '../../lib/search/bm25-index.js';
    10	import * as fsrsScheduler from '../../lib/cognitive/fsrs-scheduler.js';
    11	import * as incrementalIndex from '../../lib/storage/incremental-index.js';
    12	import { reconsolidate } from '../../lib/storage/reconsolidation.js';
    13	import type { ReconsolidationResult } from '../../lib/storage/reconsolidation.js';
    14	import { classifyEncodingIntent } from '../../lib/search/encoding-intent.js';
    15	import {
    16	  isEncodingIntentEnabled,
    17	  isReconsolidationEnabled as isReconsolidationFlagEnabled,
    18	  isAssistiveReconsolidationEnabled as _isAssistiveReconsolidationEnabled,
    19	} from '../../lib/search/search-flags.js';
    20	import type * as memoryParser from '../../lib/parsing/memory-parser.js';
    21	import { toErrorMessage } from '../../utils/index.js';
    22	
    23	import { recordHistory } from '../../lib/storage/history.js';
    24	import { appendMutationLedgerSafe } from '../memory-crud-utils.js';
    25	import { calculateDocumentWeight, isSpecDocumentType } from '../pe-gating.js';
    26	import { detectSpecLevelFromParsed } from '../handler-utils.js';
    27	import { applyPostInsertMetadata, hasReconsolidationCheckpoint } from './db-helpers.js';
    28	import type {
    29	  AssistiveRecommendation,
    30	  IndexResult,
    31	  ReconWarningList,
    32	} from './types.js';
    33	export type { AssistiveClassification, AssistiveRecommendation } from './types.js';
    34	
    35	// Feature catalog: Reconsolidation-on-save
    36	// Feature catalog: Memory indexing (memory_save)
    37	// Feature catalog: Assistive reconsolidation (REQ-D4-005)
    38	
    39	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    40	// ASSISTIVE RECONSOLIDATION CONSTANTS (REQ-D4-005)
    41	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    42	
    43	/**
    44	 * Similarity threshold above which two memories are considered near-duplicates
    45	 * and auto-merged.  Requires SPECKIT_ASSISTIVE_RECONSOLIDATION=true.
    46	 */
    47	export const ASSISTIVE_AUTO_MERGE_THRESHOLD = 0.96;
    48	
    49	/**
    50	 * Similarity threshold above which two memories are considered borderline
    51	 * (possible supersede/complement).  A recommendation is logged but no
    52	 * destructive action is taken.
    53	 */
    54	export const ASSISTIVE_REVIEW_THRESHOLD = 0.88;
    55	
    56	/**
    57	 * Check whether the assistive reconsolidation feature is enabled.
    58	 * Default: ON (graduated). Set SPECKIT_ASSISTIVE_RECONSOLIDATION=false to disable.
    59	 */
    60	export function isAssistiveReconsolidationEnabled(): boolean {
    61	  return _isAssistiveReconsolidationEnabled();
    62	}
    63	
    64	/**
    65	 * Determine the assistive reconsolidation classification for a pair of memories
    66	 * based on their similarity score.
    67	 *
    68	 * Tiers:
    69	 *   similarity >= 0.96  → auto-merge (near-duplicate)
    70	 *   0.88 <= sim < 0.96  → review (supersede or complement recommendation)
    71	 *   sim < 0.88          → keep separate (complement)
    72	 *
    73	 * @param similarity - Cosine similarity in [0, 1]
    74	 * @returns Classification string
    75	 */
    76	export function classifyAssistiveSimilarity(
    77	  similarity: number
    78	): 'auto_merge' | 'review' | 'keep_separate' {
    79	  if (similarity >= ASSISTIVE_AUTO_MERGE_THRESHOLD) return 'auto_merge';
    80	  if (similarity >= ASSISTIVE_REVIEW_THRESHOLD)     return 'review';
    81	  return 'keep_separate';
    82	}
    83	
    84	/**
    85	 * Classify whether a borderline (review-tier) memory pair should be
    86	 * superseded or complemented.
    87	 *
    88	 * Heuristic: if the newer memory's content is longer than the older one,
    89	 * it likely adds information (complement); otherwise it likely replaces it
    90	 * (supersede).  Callers may override with domain-specific logic.
    91	 *
    92	 * @param olderContent - Content text of the older memory
    93	 * @param newerContent - Content text of the newer memory
    94	 * @returns 'supersede' or 'complement'
    95	 */
    96	export function classifySupersededOrComplement(
    97	  olderContent: string,
    98	  newerContent: string
    99	): 'supersede' | 'complement' {
   100	  const olderLen = (olderContent ?? '').length;
   101	  const newerLen = (newerContent ?? '').length;
   102	  // Newer is substantially longer → it complements rather than replaces
   103	  return newerLen > olderLen * 1.2 ? 'complement' : 'supersede';
   104	}
   105	
   106	/**
   107	 * Log a borderline recommendation to the console (shadow-only).
   108	 * No database writes are performed — this is purely observational.
   109	 *
   110	 * @param recommendation - The recommendation payload
   111	 */
   112	export function logAssistiveRecommendation(
   113	  recommendation: AssistiveRecommendation
   114	): void {
   115	  console.warn(
   116	    `[reconsolidation-bridge] assistive recommendation: ` +
   117	    `${recommendation.classification} — ` +
   118	    `older=${recommendation.olderMemoryId} newer=${recommendation.newerMemoryId ?? 'pending'} ` +
   119	    `similarity=${recommendation.similarity.toFixed(3)}`
   120	  );
   121	}
   122	
   123	/**
   124	 * Result payload from reconsolidation pre-checks during memory_save.
   125	 */
   126	export interface ReconsolidationBridgeResult {
   127	  earlyReturn: IndexResult | null;
   128	  warnings: ReconWarningList;
   129	  /** Populated when SPECKIT_ASSISTIVE_RECONSOLIDATION is enabled and a
   130	   *  borderline pair is detected (review tier). */
   131	  assistiveRecommendation?: AssistiveRecommendation | null;
   132	}
   133	
   134	function repairBm25Document(args: {
   135	  memoryId: number;
   136	  title: string | null;
   137	  contentText: string;
   138	  triggerPhrases: string[];
   139	  filePath: string;
   140	  contextLabel: string;
   141	}): string | null {
   142	  try {
   143	    const bm25 = bm25Index.getIndex();
   144	    const documentText = bm25Index.buildBm25DocumentText({
   145	      title: args.title,
   146	      content_text: args.contentText,
   147	      trigger_phrases: args.triggerPhrases,
   148	      file_path: args.filePath,
   149	    });
   150	
   151	    bm25.removeDocument(String(args.memoryId));
   152	    bm25.addDocument(String(args.memoryId), documentText);
   153	    return null;
   154	  } catch (repairErr: unknown) {
   155	    const repairMessage = toErrorMessage(repairErr);
   156	    console.warn(`[memory-save] Immediate BM25 repair failed (${args.contextLabel}): ${repairMessage}`);
   157	    return `BM25 repair failed after ${args.contextLabel} for memory ${args.memoryId}: ${repairMessage}`;
   158	  }
   159	}
   160	
   161	/**
   162	 * Runs reconsolidation when enabled and returns either an early tool response
   163	 * or a signal to continue the standard create-record path.
   164	 */
   165	export async function runReconsolidationIfEnabled(
   166	  database: BetterSqlite3.Database,
   167	  parsed: ReturnType<typeof memoryParser.parseMemoryFile>,
   168	  filePath: string,
   169	  force: boolean,
   170	  embedding: Float32Array | null,
   171	  scope?: { tenantId?: string | null; userId?: string | null; agentId?: string | null; sessionId?: string | null; sharedSpaceId?: string | null },
   172	): Promise<ReconsolidationBridgeResult> {
   173	  // BUG-2 fix: Track reconsolidation warnings for structured MCP response (not just console.warn)
   174	  const reconWarnings = [] as ReconWarningList;
   175	
   176	  // T-04: search-flags.ts is the canonical caller-visible opt-in gate.
   177	  // Reconsolidation.ts keeps an internal guard as a defensive fallback for
   178	  // Direct callers and future entry points.
   179	  if (!force && isReconsolidationFlagEnabled() && embedding) {
   180	    try {
   181	      const hasCheckpoint = hasReconsolidationCheckpoint(database, parsed.specFolder);
   182	      if (!hasCheckpoint) {
   183	        const reconMsg = 'TM-06: reconsolidation skipped — create checkpoint "pre-reconsolidation" first';
   184	        console.warn(`[memory-save] ${reconMsg}`);
   185	        reconWarnings.push(reconMsg);
   186	        // Continue normal create path without reconsolidation.
   187	      } else {
   188	        const reconResult: ReconsolidationResult | null = await reconsolidate(
   189	          {
   190	            title: parsed.title,
   191	            content: parsed.content,
   192	            specFolder: parsed.specFolder,
   193	            filePath,
   194	            embedding,
   195	            triggerPhrases: parsed.triggerPhrases,
   196	            importanceTier: parsed.importanceTier,
   197	          },
   198	          database,
   199	          {
   200	            findSimilar: (emb, opts) => {
   201	              const searchEmb = emb instanceof Float32Array ? emb : new Float32Array(emb as number[]);
   202	              const results = vectorIndex.vectorSearch(searchEmb, {
   203	                limit: (opts.limit ?? 3) * 3, // Over-fetch for post-scope-filter
   204	                specFolder: opts.specFolder,
   205	                minSimilarity: 50,
   206	                includeConstitutional: false,
   207	              });
   208	              // Post-filter by governance scope to prevent cross-tenant reconsolidation
   209	              const scopeFiltered = results.filter((r: Record<string, unknown>) => {
   210	                if (scope?.tenantId && r.tenant_id && r.tenant_id !== scope.tenantId) return false;
   211	                if (scope?.userId && r.user_id && r.user_id !== scope.userId) return false;
   212	                if (scope?.agentId && r.agent_id && r.agent_id !== scope.agentId) return false;
   213	                if (scope?.sharedSpaceId && r.shared_space_id && r.shared_space_id !== scope.sharedSpaceId) return false;
   214	                return true;
   215	              }).slice(0, opts.limit ?? 3);
   216	              return scopeFiltered.map((r: Record<string, unknown>) => ({
   217	                id: typeof r.id === 'number' ? r.id : 0,
   218	                file_path: typeof r.file_path === 'string' ? r.file_path : '',
   219	                title: typeof r.title === 'string' ? r.title : null,
   220	                content_text: typeof r.content_text === 'string' ? r.content_text : (typeof r.content === 'string' ? r.content : null),
   221	                similarity: (typeof r.similarity === 'number' ? r.similarity : 0) / 100,
   222	                spec_folder: parsed.specFolder,
   223	                importance_weight: typeof r.importance_weight === 'number'
   224	                  ? r.importance_weight
   225	                  : 0.5,
   226	              }));
   227	            },
   228	            storeMemory: (memory) => {
   229	              const importanceWeight = calculateDocumentWeight(filePath, parsed.documentType);
   230	              const callbackSpecLevel = isSpecDocumentType(parsed.documentType)
   231	                ? detectSpecLevelFromParsed(memory.filePath)
   232	                : null;
   233	              const memoryEncodingIntent = isEncodingIntentEnabled()
   234	                ? classifyEncodingIntent(memory.content)
   235	                : undefined;
   236	
   237	              // P1-01 fix — wrap all DB writes (index, metadata, BM25, history) in a
   238	              // Single transaction for atomicity. better-sqlite3 supports nested transactions
   239	              // Via savepoints, so this is safe even if indexMemory uses its own transaction.
   240	              const fileMetadata = incrementalIndex.getFileMetadata(memory.filePath);
   241	              const fileMtimeMs = fileMetadata ? fileMetadata.mtime : null;
   242	
   243	              const memoryId = database.transaction(() => {
   244	                const id = vectorIndex.indexMemory({
   245	                  specFolder: memory.specFolder,
   246	                  filePath: memory.filePath,
   247	                  title: memory.title,
   248	                  triggerPhrases: memory.triggerPhrases ?? [],
   249	                  importanceWeight,
   250	                  embedding: memory.embedding as Float32Array,
   251	                  encodingIntent: memoryEncodingIntent,
   252	                  documentType: parsed.documentType || 'memory',
   253	                  specLevel: callbackSpecLevel,
   254	                  contentText: memory.content,
   255	                  qualityScore: parsed.qualityScore,
   256	                  qualityFlags: parsed.qualityFlags,
   257	                });
   258	
   259	                applyPostInsertMetadata(database, id, {
   260	                  content_hash: parsed.contentHash,
   261	                  context_type: parsed.contextType,
   262	                  importance_tier: parsed.importanceTier,
   263	                  memory_type: parsed.memoryType,
   264	                  type_inference_source: parsed.memoryTypeSource,
   265	                  stability: fsrsScheduler.DEFAULT_INITIAL_STABILITY,
   266	                  difficulty: fsrsScheduler.DEFAULT_INITIAL_DIFFICULTY,
   267	                  file_mtime_ms: fileMtimeMs,
   268	                  encoding_intent: memoryEncodingIntent,
   269	                  document_type: parsed.documentType || 'memory',
   270	                  spec_level: callbackSpecLevel,
   271	                  quality_score: parsed.qualityScore ?? 0,
   272	                  quality_flags: JSON.stringify(parsed.qualityFlags ?? []),
   273	                });
   274	
   275	                if (bm25Index.isBm25Enabled()) {
   276	                  try {
   277	                    const bm25 = bm25Index.getIndex();
   278	                    bm25.addDocument(String(id), bm25Index.buildBm25DocumentText({
   279	                      title: memory.title,
   280	                      content_text: memory.content,
   281	                      trigger_phrases: memory.triggerPhrases ?? [],
   282	                      file_path: memory.filePath,
   283	                    }));
   284	                  } catch (bm25Err: unknown) {
   285	                    const message = toErrorMessage(bm25Err);
   286	                    console.warn(`[memory-save] BM25 indexing failed (recon conflict store): ${message}`);
   287	                    const bm25Warning = repairBm25Document({
   288	                      memoryId: id,
   289	                      title: memory.title,
   290	                      contentText: memory.content,
   291	                      triggerPhrases: memory.triggerPhrases ?? [],
   292	                      filePath: memory.filePath,
   293	                      contextLabel: 'recon conflict store',
   294	                    });
   295	                    if (bm25Warning) {
   296	                      reconWarnings.push(bm25Warning);
   297	                    }
   298	                  }
   299	                }
   300	
   301	                recordHistory(id, 'ADD', null, memory.title ?? memory.filePath ?? null, 'mcp:memory_save');
   302	
   303	                return id;
   304	              })();
   305	
   306	              return memoryId;
   307	            },
   308	            generateEmbedding: async (content: string) => {
   309	              return embeddings.generateDocumentEmbedding(content);
   310	            },
   311	          }
   312	        );
   313	
   314	        if (reconResult && reconResult.action !== 'complement') {
   315	          // Reconsolidation handled the memory (merge or conflict) — skip normal CREATE path
   316	          console.error(`[memory-save] TM-06: Reconsolidation ${reconResult.action} for ${path.basename(filePath)}`);
   317	
   318	          const reconId = reconResult.newMemoryId;
   319	
   320	          const ledgerRecorded = appendMutationLedgerSafe(database, {
   321	            mutationType: 'update',
   322	            reason: `memory_save: reconsolidation ${reconResult.action}`,
   323	            priorHash: null,
   324	            newHash: parsed.contentHash,
   325	            linkedMemoryIds: reconResult.action === 'conflict'
   326	              ? [reconResult.newMemoryId, reconResult.existingMemoryId]
   327	              : [reconId],
   328	            decisionMeta: {
   329	              tool: 'memory_save',
   330	              action: `reconsolidation_${reconResult.action}`,
   331	              similarity: reconResult.similarity,
   332	              specFolder: parsed.specFolder,
   333	              filePath,
   334	            },
   335	            actor: 'mcp:memory_save',
   336	          });
   337	          const earlyReturnWarnings = [
   338	            ...reconWarnings,
   339	            ...(reconResult.warnings ?? []),
   340	            ...(!ledgerRecorded ? ['Mutation ledger append failed; audit trail may be incomplete.'] : []),
===== /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/storage/reconsolidation.ts =====
     1	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     2	// MODULE: Reconsolidation
     3	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     4	// TM-06: Reconsolidation-on-Save
     5	//
     6	// After embedding generation, check top-3 most similar memories
     7	// In the spec folder:
     8	// - similarity in [0.88, 1.0]: MERGE (duplicate — merge content, boost importance_weight)
     9	// - similarity in [0.75, 0.88): CONFLICT (supersede prior memory via causal 'supersedes' edge)
    10	// - similarity in [0, 0.75): COMPLEMENT (store new memory unchanged)
    11	// Note: cosine similarity, normalized [0,1]. Thresholds defined by MERGE_THRESHOLD and CONFLICT_THRESHOLD constants.
    12	//
    13	// Behind SPECKIT_RECONSOLIDATION opt-in flag (default OFF)
    14	// REQUIRES: checkpoint created before first enable
    15	import { createHash } from 'crypto';
    16	import type Database from 'better-sqlite3';
    17	import { recordHistory } from './history.js';
    18	import * as causalEdges from './causal-edges.js';
    19	import * as bm25Index from '../search/bm25-index.js';
    20	import { clear_search_cache } from '../search/vector-index-aliases.js';
    21	import { refresh_interference_scores_for_folder } from '../search/vector-index-store.js';
    22	import { getCanonicalPathKey } from '../utils/canonical-path.js';
    23	import { delete_memory_from_database } from '../search/vector-index-mutations.js';
    24	import { recordLineageTransition } from './lineage-state.js';
    25	import {
    26	  applyPostInsertMetadata,
    27	  type PostInsertMetadataFields,
    28	} from './post-insert-metadata.js';
    29	
    30	// Feature catalog: Reconsolidation-on-save
    31	
    32	
    33	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    34	// 1. TYPES
    35	
    36	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    37	/** Action determined by similarity threshold comparison */
    38	export type ReconsolidationAction = 'merge' | 'conflict' | 'complement';
    39	
    40	/** An existing memory found during similarity search */
    41	export interface SimilarMemory {
    42	  id: number;
    43	  file_path: string;
    44	  title: string | null;
    45	  content_text: string | null;
    46	  similarity: number;
    47	  spec_folder: string;
    48	  importance_weight?: number;
    49	  [key: string]: unknown;
    50	}
    51	
    52	/** A new memory to be reconsolidated */
    53	export interface NewMemoryData {
    54	  id?: number;
    55	  title: string | null;
    56	  content: string;
    57	  specFolder: string;
    58	  filePath: string;
    59	  embedding: Float32Array | number[];
    60	  triggerPhrases?: string[];
    61	  importanceTier?: string;
    62	  importanceWeight?: number;
    63	}
    64	
    65	/** Result of a merge operation */
    66	export interface MergeResult {
    67	  action: 'merge';
    68	  existingMemoryId: number;
    69	  newMemoryId: number;
    70	  importanceWeight: number;
    71	  mergedContentLength: number;
    72	  similarity: number;
    73	  warnings?: string[];
    74	}
    75	
    76	export type MergeAbortStatus = 'predecessor_changed' | 'predecessor_gone';
    77	
    78	/** Result when a merge candidate becomes stale before commit */
    79	export interface MergeAbortedResult {
    80	  action: 'complement';
    81	  status: MergeAbortStatus;
    82	  existingMemoryId: number;
    83	  newMemoryId: number;
    84	  similarity: number;
    85	  warnings?: string[];
    86	}
    87	
    88	/** Result of a conflict (supersede) operation */
    89	export interface ConflictResult {
    90	  action: 'conflict';
    91	  existingMemoryId: number;
    92	  newMemoryId: number;
    93	  causalEdgeId: number | null;
    94	  similarity: number;
    95	  warnings?: string[];
    96	}
    97	
    98	/** Result of a complement (new) operation */
    99	export interface ComplementResult {
   100	  action: 'complement';
   101	  /**
   102	   * Memory ID when already known by caller; 0 means "not persisted yet" and
   103	   * caller should execute normal create flow.
   104	   */
   105	  newMemoryId: number;
   106	  similarity: number | null;
   107	  warnings?: string[];
   108	}
   109	
   110	/** Combined reconsolidation result */
   111	export type ReconsolidationResult = MergeResult | MergeAbortedResult | ConflictResult | ComplementResult;
   112	
   113	/** Callback for finding similar memories by embedding */
   114	type FindSimilarFn = (
   115	  embedding: Float32Array | number[],
   116	  options: { limit: number; specFolder?: string }
   117	) => SimilarMemory[];
   118	
   119	/** Callback for storing a new memory (complement path) */
   120	type StoreMemoryFn = (memory: NewMemoryData) => number;
   121	
   122	/** Callback for generating an embedding from content */
   123	type GenerateEmbeddingFn = (content: string) => Promise<Float32Array | number[] | null>;
   124	
   125	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   126	// 2. CONFIGURATION
   127	
   128	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   129	/** Threshold above which memories are merged (near-duplicates) */
   130	const MERGE_THRESHOLD = 0.88;
   131	
   132	/** Threshold above which memories are in conflict (supersede) */
   133	const CONFLICT_THRESHOLD = 0.75;
   134	
   135	/** Maximum number of similar memories to check */
   136	const SIMILAR_MEMORY_LIMIT = 3;
   137	
   138	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   139	// 3. FEATURE FLAG
   140	
   141	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   142	// Reconsolidation gate — canonical implementation in search-flags.ts.
   143	// Default: ON (graduated). Set SPECKIT_RECONSOLIDATION=false to disable.
   144	import { isReconsolidationEnabled } from '../search/search-flags.js';
   145	export { isReconsolidationEnabled };
   146	
   147	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   148	// 4. SIMILARITY SEARCH
   149	
   150	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   151	/**
   152	 * Find the top-N most similar memories in a spec folder.
   153	 *
   154	 * @param embedding - The embedding vector to compare against
   155	 * @param specFolder - The spec folder to search within
   156	 * @param findSimilar - Callback to find similar memories by embedding
   157	 * @param limit - Maximum number of results (default: 3)
   158	 * @returns Array of similar memories sorted by similarity DESC
   159	 */
   160	export function findSimilarMemories(
   161	  embedding: Float32Array | number[],
   162	  specFolder: string,
   163	  findSimilar: FindSimilarFn,
   164	  limit: number = SIMILAR_MEMORY_LIMIT
   165	): SimilarMemory[] {
   166	  try {
   167	    return findSimilar(embedding, { limit, specFolder });
   168	  } catch (err: unknown) {
   169	    const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);
   170	    console.warn('[reconsolidation] findSimilarMemories error:', message);
   171	    return [];
   172	  }
   173	}
   174	
   175	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   176	// 5. ACTION DETERMINATION
   177	
   178	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   179	/**
   180	 * Determine the reconsolidation action based on similarity score.
   181	 *
   182	 * @param similarity - Cosine similarity between new and existing memory
   183	 * @returns The action to take: 'merge', 'conflict', or 'complement'
   184	 */
   185	export function determineAction(similarity: number): ReconsolidationAction {
   186	  if (similarity >= MERGE_THRESHOLD) {
   187	    return 'merge';
   188	  }
   189	  if (similarity >= CONFLICT_THRESHOLD) {
   190	    return 'conflict';
   191	  }
   192	  return 'complement';
   193	}
   194	
   195	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   196	// 6. MERGE OPERATION
   197	
   198	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   199	/**
   200	 * Merge a new memory into an existing one (similarity >= 0.88).
   201	 *
   202	 * Combines content by appending new unique sections to the existing
   203	 * memory, boosts the importance_weight, and updates the embedding
   204	 * to reflect the merged content.
   205	 *
   206	 * @param existingMemory - The existing memory to merge into
   207	 * @param newMemory - The new memory being saved
   208	 * @param db - The database instance
   209	 * @param generateEmbedding - Optional callback to regenerate embedding for merged content
   210	 * @returns MergeResult when merged, or a complement-style abort result when the predecessor changed
   211	 */
   212	export async function executeMerge(
   213	  existingMemory: SimilarMemory,
   214	  newMemory: NewMemoryData,
   215	  db: Database.Database,
