    39	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    40	// ASSISTIVE RECONSOLIDATION CONSTANTS (REQ-D4-005)
    41	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    42	
    43	/**
    44	 * Similarity threshold above which two memories are considered near-duplicates
    45	 * and auto-merged.  Requires SPECKIT_ASSISTIVE_RECONSOLIDATION=true.
    46	 */
    47	export const ASSISTIVE_AUTO_MERGE_THRESHOLD = 0.96;
    48	
    49	/**
    50	 * Similarity threshold above which two memories are considered borderline
    51	 * (possible supersede/complement).  A recommendation is logged but no
    52	 * destructive action is taken.
    53	 */
    54	export const ASSISTIVE_REVIEW_THRESHOLD = 0.88;
    55	
    56	/**
    57	 * Check whether the assistive reconsolidation feature is enabled.
    58	 * Default: ON (graduated). Set SPECKIT_ASSISTIVE_RECONSOLIDATION=false to disable.
    59	 */
    60	export function isAssistiveReconsolidationEnabled(): boolean {
    61	  return _isAssistiveReconsolidationEnabled();
    62	}
    63	
    64	/**
    65	 * Determine the assistive reconsolidation classification for a pair of memories
    66	 * based on their similarity score.
    67	 *
    68	 * Tiers:
    69	 *   similarity >= 0.96  → auto-merge (near-duplicate)
    70	 *   0.88 <= sim < 0.96  → review (supersede or complement recommendation)
    71	 *   sim < 0.88          → keep separate (complement)
    72	 *
    73	 * @param similarity - Cosine similarity in [0, 1]
    74	 * @returns Classification string
    75	 */
    76	export function classifyAssistiveSimilarity(
    77	  similarity: number
    78	): 'auto_merge' | 'review' | 'keep_separate' {
    79	  if (similarity >= ASSISTIVE_AUTO_MERGE_THRESHOLD) return 'auto_merge';
    80	  if (similarity >= ASSISTIVE_REVIEW_THRESHOLD)     return 'review';
    81	  return 'keep_separate';
    82	}
    83	
    84	/**
    85	 * Classify whether a borderline (review-tier) memory pair should be
    86	 * superseded or complemented.
    87	 *
    88	 * Heuristic: if the newer memory's content is longer than the older one,
    89	 * it likely adds information (complement); otherwise it likely replaces it
    90	 * (supersede).  Callers may override with domain-specific logic.
    91	 *
    92	 * @param olderContent - Content text of the older memory
    93	 * @param newerContent - Content text of the newer memory
    94	 * @returns 'supersede' or 'complement'
    95	 */
    96	export function classifySupersededOrComplement(
    97	  olderContent: string,
    98	  newerContent: string
    99	): 'supersede' | 'complement' {
   100	  const olderLen = (olderContent ?? '').length;
   101	  const newerLen = (newerContent ?? '').length;
   102	  // Newer is substantially longer → it complements rather than replaces
   103	  return newerLen > olderLen * 1.2 ? 'complement' : 'supersede';
   104	}
   105	
   106	/**
   107	 * Log a borderline recommendation to the console (shadow-only).
   108	 * No database writes are performed — this is purely observational.
   109	 *
   110	 * @param recommendation - The recommendation payload
   111	 */
   112	export function logAssistiveRecommendation(
   113	  recommendation: AssistiveRecommendation
   114	): void {
   115	  console.warn(
   116	    `[reconsolidation-bridge] assistive recommendation: ` +
   117	    `${recommendation.classification} — ` +
   118	    `older=${recommendation.olderMemoryId} newer=${recommendation.newerMemoryId ?? 'pending'} ` +
   119	    `similarity=${recommendation.similarity.toFixed(3)}`
   120	  );
   121	}
   122	
   123	/**
   124	 * Result payload from reconsolidation pre-checks during memory_save.
   125	 */
   126	export interface ReconsolidationBridgeResult {
   127	  earlyReturn: IndexResult | null;
   128	  warnings: ReconWarningList;
   129	  /** Populated when SPECKIT_ASSISTIVE_RECONSOLIDATION is enabled and a
   130	   *  borderline pair is detected (review tier). */
   131	  assistiveRecommendation?: AssistiveRecommendation | null;
   132	}
   133	
   134	function repairBm25Document(args: {
   135	  memoryId: number;
   136	  title: string | null;
   137	  contentText: string;
   138	  triggerPhrases: string[];
   139	  filePath: string;
   140	  contextLabel: string;
   141	}): string | null {
   142	  try {
   143	    const bm25 = bm25Index.getIndex();
   144	    const documentText = bm25Index.buildBm25DocumentText({
   145	      title: args.title,
   146	      content_text: args.contentText,
   147	      trigger_phrases: args.triggerPhrases,
   148	      file_path: args.filePath,
   149	    });
   150	
   151	    bm25.removeDocument(String(args.memoryId));
   152	    bm25.addDocument(String(args.memoryId), documentText);
   153	    return null;
   154	  } catch (repairErr: unknown) {
   155	    const repairMessage = toErrorMessage(repairErr);
   156	    console.warn(`[memory-save] Immediate BM25 repair failed (${args.contextLabel}): ${repairMessage}`);
   157	    return `BM25 repair failed after ${args.contextLabel} for memory ${args.memoryId}: ${repairMessage}`;
   158	  }
   159	}
   160	
   161	/**
   162	 * Runs reconsolidation when enabled and returns either an early tool response
   163	 * or a signal to continue the standard create-record path.
   164	 */
   165	export async function runReconsolidationIfEnabled(
   166	  database: BetterSqlite3.Database,
   167	  parsed: ReturnType<typeof memoryParser.parseMemoryFile>,
   168	  filePath: string,
   169	  force: boolean,
   170	  embedding: Float32Array | null,
   171	  scope?: { tenantId?: string | null; userId?: string | null; agentId?: string | null; sessionId?: string | null; sharedSpaceId?: string | null },
   172	): Promise<ReconsolidationBridgeResult> {
   173	  // BUG-2 fix: Track reconsolidation warnings for structured MCP response (not just console.warn)
   174	  const reconWarnings = [] as ReconWarningList;
   175	
   176	  // T-04: search-flags.ts is the canonical caller-visible opt-in gate.
   177	  // Reconsolidation.ts keeps an internal guard as a defensive fallback for
   178	  // Direct callers and future entry points.
   179	  if (!force && isReconsolidationFlagEnabled() && embedding) {
   180	    try {
   181	      const hasCheckpoint = hasReconsolidationCheckpoint(database, parsed.specFolder);
   182	      if (!hasCheckpoint) {
   183	        const reconMsg = 'TM-06: reconsolidation skipped — create checkpoint "pre-reconsolidation" first';
   184	        console.warn(`[memory-save] ${reconMsg}`);
   185	        reconWarnings.push(reconMsg);
   186	        // Continue normal create path without reconsolidation.
   187	      } else {
   188	        const reconResult: ReconsolidationResult | null = await reconsolidate(
   189	          {
   190	            title: parsed.title,
   191	            content: parsed.content,
   192	            specFolder: parsed.specFolder,
   193	            filePath,
   194	            embedding,
   195	            triggerPhrases: parsed.triggerPhrases,
   196	            importanceTier: parsed.importanceTier,
   197	          },
   198	          database,
   199	          {
   200	            findSimilar: (emb, opts) => {
   201	              const searchEmb = emb instanceof Float32Array ? emb : new Float32Array(emb as number[]);
   202	              const results = vectorIndex.vectorSearch(searchEmb, {
   203	                limit: (opts.limit ?? 3) * 3, // Over-fetch for post-scope-filter
   204	                specFolder: opts.specFolder,
   205	                minSimilarity: 50,
   206	                includeConstitutional: false,
   207	              });
   208	              // Post-filter by governance scope to prevent cross-tenant reconsolidation
   209	              const scopeFiltered = results.filter((r: Record<string, unknown>) => {
   210	                if (scope?.tenantId && r.tenant_id && r.tenant_id !== scope.tenantId) return false;
   211	                if (scope?.userId && r.user_id && r.user_id !== scope.userId) return false;
   212	                if (scope?.agentId && r.agent_id && r.agent_id !== scope.agentId) return false;
   213	                if (scope?.sharedSpaceId && r.shared_space_id && r.shared_space_id !== scope.sharedSpaceId) return false;
   214	                return true;
   215	              }).slice(0, opts.limit ?? 3);
   216	              return scopeFiltered.map((r: Record<string, unknown>) => ({
   217	                id: typeof r.id === 'number' ? r.id : 0,
   218	                file_path: typeof r.file_path === 'string' ? r.file_path : '',
   219	                title: typeof r.title === 'string' ? r.title : null,
   220	                content_text: typeof r.content_text === 'string' ? r.content_text : (typeof r.content === 'string' ? r.content : null),
===== /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/save/reconsolidation-bridge.ts : slice B =====
   221	                similarity: (typeof r.similarity === 'number' ? r.similarity : 0) / 100,
   222	                spec_folder: parsed.specFolder,
   223	                importance_weight: typeof r.importance_weight === 'number'
   224	                  ? r.importance_weight
   225	                  : 0.5,
   226	              }));
   227	            },
   228	            storeMemory: (memory) => {
   229	              const importanceWeight = calculateDocumentWeight(filePath, parsed.documentType);
   230	              const callbackSpecLevel = isSpecDocumentType(parsed.documentType)
   231	                ? detectSpecLevelFromParsed(memory.filePath)
   232	                : null;
   233	              const memoryEncodingIntent = isEncodingIntentEnabled()
   234	                ? classifyEncodingIntent(memory.content)
   235	                : undefined;
   236	
   237	              // P1-01 fix — wrap all DB writes (index, metadata, BM25, history) in a
   238	              // Single transaction for atomicity. better-sqlite3 supports nested transactions
   239	              // Via savepoints, so this is safe even if indexMemory uses its own transaction.
   240	              const fileMetadata = incrementalIndex.getFileMetadata(memory.filePath);
   241	              const fileMtimeMs = fileMetadata ? fileMetadata.mtime : null;
   242	
   243	              const memoryId = database.transaction(() => {
   244	                const id = vectorIndex.indexMemory({
   245	                  specFolder: memory.specFolder,
   246	                  filePath: memory.filePath,
   247	                  title: memory.title,
   248	                  triggerPhrases: memory.triggerPhrases ?? [],
   249	                  importanceWeight,
   250	                  embedding: memory.embedding as Float32Array,
   251	                  encodingIntent: memoryEncodingIntent,
   252	                  documentType: parsed.documentType || 'memory',
   253	                  specLevel: callbackSpecLevel,
   254	                  contentText: memory.content,
   255	                  qualityScore: parsed.qualityScore,
   256	                  qualityFlags: parsed.qualityFlags,
   257	                });
   258	
   259	                applyPostInsertMetadata(database, id, {
   260	                  content_hash: parsed.contentHash,
   261	                  context_type: parsed.contextType,
   262	                  importance_tier: parsed.importanceTier,
   263	                  memory_type: parsed.memoryType,
   264	                  type_inference_source: parsed.memoryTypeSource,
   265	                  stability: fsrsScheduler.DEFAULT_INITIAL_STABILITY,
   266	                  difficulty: fsrsScheduler.DEFAULT_INITIAL_DIFFICULTY,
   267	                  file_mtime_ms: fileMtimeMs,
   268	                  encoding_intent: memoryEncodingIntent,
   269	                  document_type: parsed.documentType || 'memory',
   270	                  spec_level: callbackSpecLevel,
   271	                  quality_score: parsed.qualityScore ?? 0,
   272	                  quality_flags: JSON.stringify(parsed.qualityFlags ?? []),
   273	                });
   274	
   275	                if (bm25Index.isBm25Enabled()) {
   276	                  try {
   277	                    const bm25 = bm25Index.getIndex();
   278	                    bm25.addDocument(String(id), bm25Index.buildBm25DocumentText({
   279	                      title: memory.title,
   280	                      content_text: memory.content,
   281	                      trigger_phrases: memory.triggerPhrases ?? [],
   282	                      file_path: memory.filePath,
   283	                    }));
   284	                  } catch (bm25Err: unknown) {
   285	                    const message = toErrorMessage(bm25Err);
   286	                    console.warn(`[memory-save] BM25 indexing failed (recon conflict store): ${message}`);
   287	                    const bm25Warning = repairBm25Document({
   288	                      memoryId: id,
   289	                      title: memory.title,
   290	                      contentText: memory.content,
   291	                      triggerPhrases: memory.triggerPhrases ?? [],
   292	                      filePath: memory.filePath,
   293	                      contextLabel: 'recon conflict store',
   294	                    });
   295	                    if (bm25Warning) {
   296	                      reconWarnings.push(bm25Warning);
   297	                    }
   298	                  }
   299	                }
   300	
   301	                recordHistory(id, 'ADD', null, memory.title ?? memory.filePath ?? null, 'mcp:memory_save');
   302	
   303	                return id;
   304	              })();
   305	
   306	              return memoryId;
   307	            },
   308	            generateEmbedding: async (content: string) => {
   309	              return embeddings.generateDocumentEmbedding(content);
   310	            },
   311	          }
   312	        );
   313	
   314	        if (reconResult && reconResult.action !== 'complement') {
   315	          // Reconsolidation handled the memory (merge or conflict) — skip normal CREATE path
   316	          console.error(`[memory-save] TM-06: Reconsolidation ${reconResult.action} for ${path.basename(filePath)}`);
   317	
   318	          const reconId = reconResult.newMemoryId;
   319	
   320	          const ledgerRecorded = appendMutationLedgerSafe(database, {
   321	            mutationType: 'update',
   322	            reason: `memory_save: reconsolidation ${reconResult.action}`,
   323	            priorHash: null,
   324	            newHash: parsed.contentHash,
   325	            linkedMemoryIds: reconResult.action === 'conflict'
   326	              ? [reconResult.newMemoryId, reconResult.existingMemoryId]
   327	              : [reconId],
   328	            decisionMeta: {
   329	              tool: 'memory_save',
   330	              action: `reconsolidation_${reconResult.action}`,
   331	              similarity: reconResult.similarity,
   332	              specFolder: parsed.specFolder,
   333	              filePath,
   334	            },
   335	            actor: 'mcp:memory_save',
   336	          });
   337	          const earlyReturnWarnings = [
   338	            ...reconWarnings,
   339	            ...(reconResult.warnings ?? []),
   340	            ...(!ledgerRecorded ? ['Mutation ledger append failed; audit trail may be incomplete.'] : []),
   341	          ];
   342	
   343	          return {
   344	            earlyReturn: {
   345	              status: reconResult.action === 'merge' ? 'merged' : 'superseded',
   346	              id: reconId,
   347	              specFolder: parsed.specFolder,
   348	              title: parsed.title ?? '',
   349	              reconsolidation: reconResult,
   350	              message: `Reconsolidation: ${reconResult.action} (similarity: ${reconResult.similarity?.toFixed(3) ?? 'N/A'})`,
   351	              warnings: earlyReturnWarnings.length > 0 ? earlyReturnWarnings : undefined,
   352	            },
   353	            warnings: reconWarnings,
   354	          };
   355	        }
   356	        // ReconResult is null or complement — fall through to normal CREATE path
   357	      }
   358	    } catch (reconErr: unknown) {
   359	      const message = toErrorMessage(reconErr);
   360	      console.warn(`[memory-save] TM-06: Reconsolidation error (proceeding with normal save): ${message}`);
   361	      // Reconsolidation errors must not block saves
   362	    }
   363	  }
   364	
   365	  // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   366	  // ASSISTIVE RECONSOLIDATION (REQ-D4-005)
   367	  // Runs independently of the TM-06 flag.  Requires embedding.
   368	  // Does NOT block normal save — all actions are advisory or
   369	  // shadow-only (auto-merge at >= 0.96 only archives old record).
   370	  // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   371	  let assistiveRecommendation: AssistiveRecommendation | null = null;
   372	
   373	  if (!force && isAssistiveReconsolidationEnabled() && embedding) {
   374	    try {
   375	      // Find the top similar memory using the existing vector search
   376	      const searchEmb = embedding instanceof Float32Array ? embedding : new Float32Array(embedding as number[]);
   377	      const candidateResults = vectorIndex.vectorSearch(searchEmb, {
   378	        limit: 3,
   379	        specFolder: parsed.specFolder,
   380	        minSimilarity: Math.round(ASSISTIVE_REVIEW_THRESHOLD * 100), // convert to 0-100 scale
   381	        includeConstitutional: false,
   382	      });
   383	
   384	      if (candidateResults.length > 0) {
   385	        const top = candidateResults[0] as Record<string, unknown>;
   386	        // vectorSearch returns similarity in [0, 100], normalise to [0, 1]
   387	        const rawSimilarity = typeof top.similarity === 'number' ? top.similarity : 0;
   388	        const similarity = rawSimilarity > 1 ? rawSimilarity / 100 : rawSimilarity;
   389	        const topId = typeof top.id === 'number' ? top.id : 0;
   390	        const topContent = typeof top.content_text === 'string' ? top.content_text :
   391	                          (typeof top.content === 'string' ? top.content : '');
   392	
   393	        const tier = classifyAssistiveSimilarity(similarity);
   394	
   395	        if (tier === 'auto_merge') {
   396	          // Auto-merge: archive the older memory record (shadow operation —
   397	          // we mark is_archived so it is excluded from future search results
   398	          // but NOT physically deleted).
   399	          try {
   400	            database.prepare(`
   401	              UPDATE memory_index
   402	              SET is_archived = 1,
   403	                  updated_at = datetime('now')
   404	              WHERE id = ?
   405	            `).run(topId);
   406	            if (bm25Index.isBm25Enabled()) {
   407	              bm25Index.getIndex().removeDocument(String(topId));
   408	            }
   409	            console.warn(
   410	              `[reconsolidation-bridge] assistive auto-merge: archived older=${topId} ` +
   411	              `(similarity=${similarity.toFixed(3)}) — newer memory continues normal save`
   412	            );
   413	          } catch (mergeErr: unknown) {
   414	            const msg = mergeErr instanceof Error ? mergeErr.message : String(mergeErr);
   415	            console.warn(`[reconsolidation-bridge] assistive auto-merge archive failed: ${msg}`);
   416	          }
   417	        } else if (tier === 'review') {
   418	          // Review tier: classify and surface as recommendation (no mutations)
   419	          const classification = classifySupersededOrComplement(topContent, parsed.content);
   420	          assistiveRecommendation = {

   421	            action: 'review',
   422	            candidateMemoryIds: [topId],
   423	            description:
   424	              `Review borderline similarity ${similarity.toFixed(3)} between the pending save ` +
   425	              `and existing memory #${topId}; heuristic suggests ${classification}.`,
   426	            olderMemoryId: topId,
   427	            newerMemoryId: null,

   440	      const message = toErrorMessage(assistiveErr);

   449	    earlyReturn: null,
   450	    warnings: reconWarnings,
   451	    assistiveRecommendation,
   452	  };

===== /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/storage/reconsolidation.ts : slice A =====
     1	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     2	// MODULE: Reconsolidation
     3	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     4	// TM-06: Reconsolidation-on-Save
     5	//
     6	// After embedding generation, check top-3 most similar memories
     7	// In the spec folder:
     8	// - similarity in [0.88, 1.0]: MERGE (duplicate — merge content, boost importance_weight)
     9	// - similarity in [0.75, 0.88): CONFLICT (supersede prior memory via causal 'supersedes' edge)
    10	// - similarity in [0, 0.75): COMPLEMENT (store new memory unchanged)
    11	// Note: cosine similarity, normalized [0,1]. Thresholds defined by MERGE_THRESHOLD and CONFLICT_THRESHOLD constants.
    12	//
    13	// Behind SPECKIT_RECONSOLIDATION opt-in flag (default OFF)
    14	// REQUIRES: checkpoint created before first enable
    15	import { createHash } from 'crypto';
    16	import type Database from 'better-sqlite3';
    17	import { recordHistory } from './history.js';
    18	import * as causalEdges from './causal-edges.js';
    19	import * as bm25Index from '../search/bm25-index.js';
    20	import { clear_search_cache } from '../search/vector-index-aliases.js';
    21	import { refresh_interference_scores_for_folder } from '../search/vector-index-store.js';
    22	import { getCanonicalPathKey } from '../utils/canonical-path.js';
    23	import { delete_memory_from_database } from '../search/vector-index-mutations.js';
    24	import { recordLineageTransition } from './lineage-state.js';
    25	import {
    26	  applyPostInsertMetadata,
    27	  type PostInsertMetadataFields,
    28	} from './post-insert-metadata.js';
    29	
    30	// Feature catalog: Reconsolidation-on-save
    31	
    32	
    33	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    34	// 1. TYPES
    35	
    36	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    37	/** Action determined by similarity threshold comparison */
    38	export type ReconsolidationAction = 'merge' | 'conflict' | 'complement';
    39	
    40	/** An existing memory found during similarity search */
    41	export interface SimilarMemory {
    42	  id: number;
    43	  file_path: string;
    44	  title: string | null;
    45	  content_text: string | null;
    46	  similarity: number;
    47	  spec_folder: string;
    48	  importance_weight?: number;
    49	  [key: string]: unknown;
    50	}
    51	
    52	/** A new memory to be reconsolidated */
    53	export interface NewMemoryData {
    54	  id?: number;
    55	  title: string | null;
    56	  content: string;
    57	  specFolder: string;
    58	  filePath: string;
    59	  embedding: Float32Array | number[];
    60	  triggerPhrases?: string[];
    61	  importanceTier?: string;
    62	  importanceWeight?: number;
    63	}
    64	
    65	/** Result of a merge operation */
    66	export interface MergeResult {
    67	  action: 'merge';
    68	  existingMemoryId: number;
    69	  newMemoryId: number;
    70	  importanceWeight: number;
    71	  mergedContentLength: number;
    72	  similarity: number;
    73	  warnings?: string[];
    74	}
    75	
    76	export type MergeAbortStatus = 'predecessor_changed' | 'predecessor_gone';
    77	
    78	/** Result when a merge candidate becomes stale before commit */
    79	export interface MergeAbortedResult {
    80	  action: 'complement';
    81	  status: MergeAbortStatus;
    82	  existingMemoryId: number;
    83	  newMemoryId: number;
    84	  similarity: number;
    85	  warnings?: string[];
    86	}
    87	
    88	/** Result of a conflict (supersede) operation */
    89	export interface ConflictResult {
    90	  action: 'conflict';
    91	  existingMemoryId: number;
    92	  newMemoryId: number;
    93	  causalEdgeId: number | null;
    94	  similarity: number;
    95	  warnings?: string[];
    96	}
    97	
    98	/** Result of a complement (new) operation */
    99	export interface ComplementResult {
   100	  action: 'complement';
   101	  /**
   102	   * Memory ID when already known by caller; 0 means "not persisted yet" and
   103	   * caller should execute normal create flow.
   104	   */
   105	  newMemoryId: number;
   106	  similarity: number | null;
   107	  warnings?: string[];
   108	}
   109	
   110	/** Combined reconsolidation result */
   111	export type ReconsolidationResult = MergeResult | MergeAbortedResult | ConflictResult | ComplementResult;
   112	
   113	/** Callback for finding similar memories by embedding */
   114	type FindSimilarFn = (
   115	  embedding: Float32Array | number[],
   116	  options: { limit: number; specFolder?: string }
   117	) => SimilarMemory[];
   118	
   119	/** Callback for storing a new memory (complement path) */
   120	type StoreMemoryFn = (memory: NewMemoryData) => number;
   121	
   122	/** Callback for generating an embedding from content */
   123	type GenerateEmbeddingFn = (content: string) => Promise<Float32Array | number[] | null>;
   124	
   125	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   126	// 2. CONFIGURATION
   127	
   128	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   129	/** Threshold above which memories are merged (near-duplicates) */
   130	const MERGE_THRESHOLD = 0.88;
   131	
   132	/** Threshold above which memories are in conflict (supersede) */
   133	const CONFLICT_THRESHOLD = 0.75;
   134	
   135	/** Maximum number of similar memories to check */
   136	const SIMILAR_MEMORY_LIMIT = 3;
   137	
   138	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   139	// 3. FEATURE FLAG
   140	
   141	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   142	// Reconsolidation gate — canonical implementation in search-flags.ts.
   143	// Default: ON (graduated). Set SPECKIT_RECONSOLIDATION=false to disable.
   144	import { isReconsolidationEnabled } from '../search/search-flags.js';
   145	export { isReconsolidationEnabled };
   146	
   147	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   148	// 4. SIMILARITY SEARCH
   149	
   150	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   151	/**
   152	 * Find the top-N most similar memories in a spec folder.
   153	 *
   154	 * @param embedding - The embedding vector to compare against
   155	 * @param specFolder - The spec folder to search within
   156	 * @param findSimilar - Callback to find similar memories by embedding
   157	 * @param limit - Maximum number of results (default: 3)
   158	 * @returns Array of similar memories sorted by similarity DESC
   159	 */
   160	export function findSimilarMemories(
   161	  embedding: Float32Array | number[],
   162	  specFolder: string,
   163	  findSimilar: FindSimilarFn,
   164	  limit: number = SIMILAR_MEMORY_LIMIT
   165	): SimilarMemory[] {
   166	  try {
   167	    return findSimilar(embedding, { limit, specFolder });
   168	  } catch (err: unknown) {
   169	    const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);
   170	    console.warn('[reconsolidation] findSimilarMemories error:', message);
   171	    return [];
   172	  }
   173	}
   174	
   175	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   176	// 5. ACTION DETERMINATION
   177	
   178	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   179	/**
   180	 * Determine the reconsolidation action based on similarity score.
   181	 *
   182	 * @param similarity - Cosine similarity between new and existing memory
   183	 * @returns The action to take: 'merge', 'conflict', or 'complement'
   184	 */
   185	export function determineAction(similarity: number): ReconsolidationAction {
   186	  if (similarity >= MERGE_THRESHOLD) {
   187	    return 'merge';
   188	  }
   189	  if (similarity >= CONFLICT_THRESHOLD) {
   190	    return 'conflict';
   191	  }
   192	  return 'complement';
   193	}
   194	
   195	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   196	// 6. MERGE OPERATION
   197	
   198	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   199	/**
   200	 * Merge a new memory into an existing one (similarity >= 0.88).
   201	 *
   202	 * Combines content by appending new unique sections to the existing
   203	 * memory, boosts the importance_weight, and updates the embedding
   204	 * to reflect the merged content.
   205	 *
   206	 * @param existingMemory - The existing memory to merge into
   207	 * @param newMemory - The new memory being saved
   208	 * @param db - The database instance
   209	 * @param generateEmbedding - Optional callback to regenerate embedding for merged content
   210	 * @returns MergeResult when merged, or a complement-style abort result when the predecessor changed
   211	 */
   212	export async function executeMerge(
   213	  existingMemory: SimilarMemory,
   214	  newMemory: NewMemoryData,
   215	  db: Database.Database,
   216	  generateEmbedding?: GenerateEmbeddingFn | null
   217	): Promise<MergeResult | MergeAbortedResult> {
   218	  const newContent = newMemory.content;
   219	  let newId = 0;
   220	  let bm25RepairWarning: string | null = null;
===== /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/storage/reconsolidation.ts : slice B =====
   221	
   222	  try {
   223	    const memoryIndexColumns = getTableColumns(db, 'memory_index');
   224	    ensureBm25RepairFlagColumn(db, memoryIndexColumns);
   225	    const existingRow = db.prepare('SELECT * FROM memory_index WHERE id = ?').get(existingMemory.id) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;
   226	
   227	    if (!existingRow || isArchivedRow(existingRow)) {
   228	      return buildMergeAbortResult(existingMemory, newMemory, 'predecessor_gone');
   229	    }
   230	
   231	    const predecessorVersion = capturePredecessorVersion(existingRow);
   232	    const existingContent = getOptionalString(existingRow, 'content_text') ?? '';
   233	    const mergedContent = mergeContent(existingContent, newContent);
   234	    const mergedHash = createHash('sha256').update(mergedContent, 'utf-8').digest('hex');
   235	    const currentWeight = getOptionalNumber(existingRow, 'importance_weight') ?? existingMemory.importance_weight ?? 0.5;
   236	    const boostedWeight = Math.min(1.0, currentWeight + 0.1);
   237	    const now = new Date().toISOString();
   238	
   239	    // Generate embedding BEFORE transaction (async I/O cannot run inside
   240	    // Better-sqlite3's synchronous transaction callback).
   241	    let newEmbedding: Float32Array | number[] | null = null;
   242	    if (generateEmbedding) {
   243	      try {
   244	        newEmbedding = await generateEmbedding(mergedContent);
   245	      } catch (embErr: unknown) {
   246	        const msg = embErr instanceof Error ? embErr.message : String(embErr);
   247	        console.warn('[reconsolidation] Failed to regenerate embedding for merge:', msg);
   248	        // Non-fatal: merged content is stored even without updated embedding
   249	      }
   250	    }
   251	
   252	    let bm25RepairNeeded = false;
   253	    let mergedBm25DocumentText = '';
   254	
   255	    // F04-001: Append-only merge — mark old as superseded, create new record
   256	    const txResult = db.transaction((): { status: 'merged' } | { status: MergeAbortStatus } => {
   257	      const currentRow = db.prepare('SELECT * FROM memory_index WHERE id = ?').get(existingMemory.id) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;
   258	
   259	      if (!currentRow || isArchivedRow(currentRow)) {
   260	        return { status: 'predecessor_gone' };
   261	      }
   262	
   263	      if (hasPredecessorChanged(predecessorVersion, currentRow)) {
   264	        return { status: 'predecessor_changed' };
   265	      }
   266	
   267	      const currentFilePath = getOptionalString(currentRow, 'file_path') ?? existingMemory.file_path;
   268	      const reusedEmbeddingRow = db.prepare(`
   269	        SELECT embedding
   270	        FROM vec_memories
   271	        WHERE rowid = ?
   272	      `).get(existingMemory.id) as { embedding?: Buffer } | undefined;
   273	      const mergedEmbeddingBuffer = newEmbedding
   274	        ? embeddingToBuffer(newEmbedding)
   275	        : (reusedEmbeddingRow?.embedding ?? null);
   276	      const mergedEmbeddingStatus = mergedEmbeddingBuffer ? 'success' : getOptionalString(currentRow, 'embedding_status') ?? 'pending';
   277	      const mergedCanonicalPath = getOptionalString(currentRow, 'canonical_file_path') ?? getCanonicalPathKey(currentFilePath);
   278	      const mergedTriggerPhrases = buildMergedTriggerPhrases(currentRow, newMemory.triggerPhrases);
   279	      const mergedTitle = newMemory.title ?? getOptionalString(currentRow, 'title') ?? existingMemory.title ?? '';
   280	      const mergedImportanceTier = newMemory.importanceTier ?? getOptionalString(currentRow, 'importance_tier');
   281	
   282	      mergedBm25DocumentText = bm25Index.buildBm25DocumentText({
   283	        title: mergedTitle,
   284	        content_text: mergedContent,
   285	        trigger_phrases: mergedTriggerPhrases,
   286	        file_path: currentFilePath,
   287	      });
   288	
   289	      // Mark existing memory as archived (superseded)
   290	      db.prepare(`
   291	        UPDATE memory_index
   292	        SET is_archived = 1,
   293	            updated_at = datetime('now')
   294	        WHERE id = ?
   295	      `).run(existingMemory.id);
   296	
   297	      const insertValues = buildMergedMemoryInsertValues(currentRow, {
   298	        spec_folder: existingMemory.spec_folder,
   299	        file_path: currentFilePath,
   300	        canonical_file_path: mergedCanonicalPath,
   301	        anchor_id: getOptionalString(currentRow, 'anchor_id'),
   302	        title: mergedTitle,
   303	        trigger_phrases: mergedTriggerPhrases,
   304	        importance_weight: boostedWeight,
   305	        importance_tier: mergedImportanceTier,
   306	        content_text: mergedContent,
   307	        content_hash: mergedHash,
   308	        embedding_status: mergedEmbeddingStatus,
   309	        embedding_model: getOptionalString(currentRow, 'embedding_model'),
   310	        embedding_generated_at: mergedEmbeddingBuffer ? now : getOptionalString(currentRow, 'embedding_generated_at'),
   311	        encoding_intent: getOptionalString(currentRow, 'encoding_intent'),
   312	        document_type: getOptionalString(currentRow, 'document_type'),
   313	        spec_level: getOptionalNumber(currentRow, 'spec_level'),
   314	        bm25_repair_needed: 0,
   315	        created_at: now,
   316	        updated_at: now,
   317	      }, memoryIndexColumns);
   318	      const insertColumns = Object.keys(insertValues);
   319	      const insertSql = `
   320	        INSERT INTO memory_index (${insertColumns.join(', ')})
   321	        VALUES (${insertColumns.map(() => '?').join(', ')})
   322	      `;
   323	      const insertResult = db.prepare(insertSql).run(...insertColumns.map((column) => insertValues[column]));
   324	
   325	      newId = Number(insertResult.lastInsertRowid);
   326	
   327	      if (mergedEmbeddingBuffer) {
   328	        db.prepare(`
   329	          INSERT INTO vec_memories (rowid, embedding)
   330	          VALUES (?, ?)
   331	        `).run(newId, mergedEmbeddingBuffer);
   332	      }
   333	
   334	      const postInsertMetadata = buildMergePostInsertMetadata(currentRow, {
   335	        content_hash: mergedHash,
   336	        importance_tier: mergedImportanceTier,
   337	        embedding_status: mergedEmbeddingStatus,
   338	        document_type: getOptionalString(currentRow, 'document_type'),
   339	        spec_level: getOptionalNumber(currentRow, 'spec_level'),
   340	      }, memoryIndexColumns);
   341	
   342	      if (Object.keys(postInsertMetadata).length > 0) {
   343	        applyPostInsertMetadata(db, newId, postInsertMetadata);
   344	      }
   345	
   346	      // Create supersedes causal edge
   347	      db.prepare(`
   348	        INSERT OR IGNORE INTO causal_edges (source_id, target_id, relation, strength, extracted_at)
   349	        VALUES (?, ?, 'supersedes', 1.0, datetime('now'))
   350	      `).run(newId, existingMemory.id);
   351	
   352	      recordLineageTransition(db, newId, {
   353	        actor: 'mcp:reconsolidation',
   354	        predecessorMemoryId: existingMemory.id,
   355	        transitionEvent: 'SUPERSEDE',
   356	      });
   357	
   358	      if (bm25Index.isBm25Enabled()) {
   359	        try {
   360	          const bm25 = bm25Index.getIndex();
   361	          bm25.removeDocument(String(existingMemory.id));
   362	          bm25.addDocument(String(newId), mergedBm25DocumentText);
   363	        } catch (bm25Err: unknown) {
   364	          const message = bm25Err instanceof Error ? bm25Err.message : String(bm25Err);
   365	          console.warn('[reconsolidation] Failed to update BM25 index for merge:', message);
   366	          bm25RepairNeeded = true;
   367	        }
   368	      }
   369	
   370	      refresh_interference_scores_for_folder(db, existingMemory.spec_folder);
   371	      clear_search_cache();
   372	
   373	      try {
   374	        recordHistory(newId, 'ADD', null, mergedTitle || existingMemory.file_path, 'mcp:reconsolidation');
   375	        recordHistory(
   376	          existingMemory.id,
   377	          'UPDATE',
   378	          existingMemory.title ?? existingMemory.file_path,
   379	          mergedTitle || existingMemory.file_path,
   380	          'mcp:reconsolidation',
   381	        );
   382	      } catch (_historyErr: unknown) {
   383	        // Best-effort history tracking during reconsolidation merge
   384	      }
   385	      return { status: 'merged' };
   386	    })();
   387	
   388	    if (txResult.status !== 'merged') {
   389	      return buildMergeAbortResult(existingMemory, newMemory, txResult.status);
   390	    }
   391	
   392	    if (bm25RepairNeeded) {
   393	      const repairResult = repairBm25Document({
   394	        previousMemoryId: existingMemory.id,
   395	        memoryId: newId,
   396	        documentText: mergedBm25DocumentText,
   397	      });
   398	      if (!repairResult.success) {
   399	        setBm25RepairNeededFlag(db, memoryIndexColumns, newId, true);
   400	        bm25RepairWarning =
   401	          `BM25 repair failed after reconsolidation merge for memory ${newId}: ${repairResult.error}`;
   402	      } else {
   403	        setBm25RepairNeededFlag(db, memoryIndexColumns, newId, false);
   404	      }
   405	    }
   406	
   407	    return {
   408	      action: 'merge',
   409	      existingMemoryId: existingMemory.id,
   410	      newMemoryId: newId,
   411	      importanceWeight: boostedWeight,
   412	      mergedContentLength: mergedContent.length,
   413	      similarity: existingMemory.similarity,
   414	      warnings: bm25RepairWarning ? [bm25RepairWarning] : undefined,
   415	    };
   416	  } catch (err: unknown) {
   417	    const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);
   418	    throw new Error(`Merge operation failed: ${message}`);
   419	  }
   420	}
===== /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/storage/reconsolidation.ts : slice C =====
   421	
   422	/**
   423	 * Merge two content strings by appending unique new lines.
   424	 *
   425	 * Splits both contents into lines, then appends lines from the new
   426	 * content that are not present in the existing content.
   427	 *
   428	 * @param existing - The existing memory content
   429	 * @param incoming - The new memory content
   430	 * @returns The merged content string
   431	 */
   432	export function mergeContent(existing: string, incoming: string): string {
   433	  if (!existing || existing.trim().length === 0) {
   434	    return incoming;
   435	  }
   436	  if (!incoming || incoming.trim().length === 0) {
   437	    return existing;
   438	  }
   439	
   440	  const existingLines = new Set(
   441	    existing.split('\n').map(line => line.trim()).filter(line => line.length > 0)
   442	  );
   443	
   444	  const newLines = incoming
   445	    .split('\n')
   446	    .map(line => line.trim())
   447	    .filter(line => line.length > 0 && !existingLines.has(line));
   448	
   449	  if (newLines.length === 0) {
   450	    return existing;
   451	  }
   452	
   453	  return existing + '\n\n<!-- Merged content -->\n' + newLines.join('\n');
   454	}
   455	
   456	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   457	// 7. CONFLICT OPERATION
   458	
   459	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   460	/**
   461	 * Resolve a conflict between highly similar memories (similarity 0.75-0.88).
   462	 *
   463	 * Preferred path (when caller provides a distinct new memory ID):
   464	 * - Mark existing memory as deprecated (superseded)
   465	 * - Create a 'supersedes' causal edge from new -> existing
   466	 *
   467	 * Legacy fallback (when no new ID is available):
   468	 * - Update existing memory content/title in-place
   469	 * - Skip edge creation (avoids self-referential edges)
   470	 *
   471	 * @param existingMemory - The existing memory being superseded
   472	 * @param newMemory - The new memory replacing it
   473	 * @param db - The database instance
   474	 * @returns ConflictResult with supersede details
   475	 */
   476	export function executeConflict(
   477	  existingMemory: SimilarMemory,
   478	  newMemory: NewMemoryData,
   479	  db: Database.Database
   480	): ConflictResult {
   481	  try {
   482	    // Add causal 'supersedes' edge only when caller provides a distinct new ID.
   483	    // Prevent self-referential supersedes edges (source == target).
   484	    let edgeId: number | null = null;
   485	    const hasDistinctNewId =
   486	      typeof newMemory.id === 'number' &&
   487	      Number.isFinite(newMemory.id) &&
   488	      newMemory.id !== existingMemory.id;
   489	
   490	    if (hasDistinctNewId) {
   491	      // Atomic transaction: deprecate + edge must succeed or fail together.
   492	      // Without this, a failed insertEdge leaves an orphaned deprecation.
   493	      db.transaction(() => {
   494	        const updateResult = db.prepare(`
   495	          UPDATE memory_index
   496	          SET importance_tier = 'deprecated',
   497	              updated_at = datetime('now')
   498	          WHERE id = ?
   499	        `).run(existingMemory.id) as { changes: number };
   500	
   501	        if (updateResult.changes === 0) {
   502	          console.warn('[reconsolidation] Deprecate target not found, skipping edge insert');
   503	          return;
   504	        }
   505	
   506	        const sourceId = String(newMemory.id);
   507	        const targetId = String(existingMemory.id);
   508	        edgeId = causalEdges.insertEdge(
   509	          sourceId,
   510	          targetId,
   511	          'supersedes',
   512	          1.0,
   513	          `TM-06 reconsolidation conflict: similarity ${(existingMemory.similarity * 100).toFixed(1)}%`
   514	        );
   515	        if (edgeId == null) {
   516	          throw new Error(
   517	            `Failed to insert supersedes edge (${sourceId} -> ${targetId}) — aborting reconsolidation`
   518	          );
   519	        }
   520	      })();
   521	    } else {
   522	      // Atomic transaction: content + embedding + hash update together.
   523	      const updatedHash = createHash('sha256').update(newMemory.content, 'utf-8').digest('hex');
   524	      db.transaction(() => {
   525	        db.prepare(`
   526	          UPDATE memory_index
   527	          SET content_text = ?,
   528	              title = ?,
   529	              content_hash = ?,
   530	              updated_at = datetime('now')
   531	          WHERE id = ?
   532	        `).run(newMemory.content, newMemory.title, updatedHash, existingMemory.id);
   533	
   534	        if (newMemory.embedding) {
   535	          const buffer = embeddingToBuffer(newMemory.embedding);
   536	          db.prepare(
   537	            'UPDATE vec_memories SET embedding = ? WHERE rowid = ?'
   538	          ).run(buffer, existingMemory.id);
   539	        }
   540	      })();
   541	    }
   542	
   543	    return {
   544	      action: 'conflict',
   545	      existingMemoryId: existingMemory.id,
   546	      newMemoryId: newMemory.id ?? existingMemory.id,
   547	      causalEdgeId: edgeId,
   548	      similarity: existingMemory.similarity,
   549	    };
   550	  } catch (err: unknown) {
   551	    const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);
   552	    throw new Error(`Conflict operation failed: ${message}`);
   553	  }
   554	}
   555	
   556	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   557	// 8. COMPLEMENT OPERATION
   558	
   559	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   560	/**
   561	 * Store a new memory unchanged (similarity < 0.75).
   562	 *
   563	 * The new memory is distinct enough from existing memories to be
   564	 * stored as a separate entry.
   565	 *
   566	 * @param newMemory - The new memory to store
   567	 * @param storeMemory - Callback to store the memory
   568	 * @param topSimilarity - The highest similarity score found (null if no candidates)
   569	 * @returns ComplementResult with storage details
   570	 */
   571	export function executeComplement(
   572	  newMemory: NewMemoryData,
   573	  storeMemory: StoreMemoryFn,
   574	  topSimilarity: number | null
   575	): ComplementResult {
   576	  const newMemoryId = storeMemory(newMemory);
   577	
   578	  return {
   579	    action: 'complement',
   580	    newMemoryId,
   581	    similarity: topSimilarity,
   582	  };
   583	}
   584	
   585	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   586	// 9. RECONSOLIDATION ORCHESTRATOR
   587	
   588	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   589	/** Options for the reconsolidation orchestrator */
   590	export interface ReconsolidateOptions {
   591	  findSimilar: FindSimilarFn;
   592	  storeMemory: StoreMemoryFn;
   593	  generateEmbedding?: GenerateEmbeddingFn | null;
   594	}
   595	
   596	/**
   597	 * Orchestrate the full reconsolidation flow for a new memory.
   598	 *
   599	 * 1. Find top-3 most similar memories in the spec folder
   600	 * 2. Determine action based on highest similarity score
   601	 * 3. Execute the appropriate operation (merge/conflict/complement)
   602	 *
   603	 * When the feature flag is OFF, returns null (caller should use
   604	 * normal store path).
   605	 *
   606	 * @param newMemory - The new memory to reconsolidate
   607	 * @param db - The database instance
   608	 * @param options - Callbacks for similarity search and storage
   609	 * @returns ReconsolidationResult or null if feature is disabled
   610	 */
   611	export async function reconsolidate(
   612	  newMemory: NewMemoryData,
   613	  db: Database.Database,
   614	  options: ReconsolidateOptions
   615	): Promise<ReconsolidationResult | null> {
   616	  if (!isReconsolidationEnabled()) {
   617	    return null;
   618	  }
   619	
   620	  const { findSimilar, storeMemory, generateEmbedding } = options;
   621	
   622	  // Step 1: Find similar memories
   623	  const similarMemories = findSimilarMemories(
   624	    newMemory.embedding,
   625	    newMemory.specFolder,
   626	    findSimilar
   627	  );
   628	
   629	  // No existing memories: complement (new)
   630	  if (similarMemories.length === 0) {
   631	    // Do not persist in orchestrator complement path.
   632	    // Caller owns canonical create flow (prevents duplicate writes).
   633	    return {
   634	      action: 'complement',
   635	      newMemoryId: newMemory.id ?? 0,
   636	      similarity: null,
   637	    };
   638	  }
   639	
   640	  // Step 2: Get the most similar memory and determine action
   641	  const topMatch = similarMemories[0];
   642	  const action = determineAction(topMatch.similarity);
   643	
   644	  // Step 3: Execute action
   645	  switch (action) {
   646	    case 'merge':
   647	      return executeMerge(topMatch, newMemory, db, generateEmbedding);
   648	
   649	    case 'conflict':
   650	      {
   651	        let conflictMemory = newMemory;
   652	
   653	        // F04-002: Wrap store + conflict in single transaction for atomicity
   654	        // TM-06 live-save path: materialize memory + supersede edge together
   655	        try {
   656	          const conflictTx = db.transaction(() => {
   657	            if (conflictMemory.id === undefined) {
   658	              const storedId = storeMemory(newMemory);
   659	              if (
   660	                typeof storedId === 'number' &&
   661	                Number.isFinite(storedId) &&
   662	                storedId > 0 &&
   663	                storedId !== topMatch.id
   664	              ) {
   665	                conflictMemory = { ...newMemory, id: storedId };
   666	              }
   667	            }
   668	            return executeConflict(topMatch, conflictMemory, db);
   669	          });
   670	          return conflictTx();
   671	        } catch (conflictErr: unknown) {
   672	          // If storeMemory succeeded but executeConflict failed, clean up the orphan
   673	          // Memory so we don't leave dangling rows with no supersedes edge.
   674	          if (conflictMemory.id !== undefined && conflictMemory.id !== newMemory.id) {
   675	            // Graph cleanup: Use delete_memory_from_database (includes deleteAncillaryMemoryRows)
   676	            // instead of raw DELETE to clean lineage, projections, and graph residue.
   677	            try {
   678	              const deleted = delete_memory_from_database(db, conflictMemory.id);
   679	              if (deleted) {
   680	                try {
   681	                  recordHistory(
   682	                    conflictMemory.id!,
   683	                    'DELETE',
   684	                    null,
   685	                    null,
   686	                    'mcp:reconsolidation_cleanup',
   687	                    conflictMemory.specFolder ?? null,
   688	                  );
   689	                } catch (_histErr: unknown) { /* best-effort */ }
   690	              }
   691	            } catch (_error: unknown) {
   692	              // Best-effort cleanup
   693	            }
   694	            console.warn('[reconsolidation] cleaned up orphan memory', conflictMemory.id, 'after executeConflict failure');
   695	          }
   696	          throw conflictErr;
   697	        }
   698	      }
   699	
   700	    case 'complement':
   701	      // Complement is a routing decision only; caller persists once.
   702	      return {
   703	        action: 'complement',
   704	        newMemoryId: newMemory.id ?? 0,
   705	        similarity: topMatch.similarity,
   706	      };
   707	
   708	    default:
   709	      // Exhaustive check
   710	      return {
   711	        action: 'complement',
   712	        newMemoryId: newMemory.id ?? 0,
   713	        similarity: topMatch.similarity,
   714	      };
   715	  }
   716	}
   717	
   718	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   719	// 10. HELPERS
   720	
   721	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   722	/**
   723	 * Convert an embedding array to a Buffer for SQLite storage.
   724	 *
   725	 * @param embedding - The embedding as Float32Array or number[]
   726	 * @returns Buffer representation of the embedding
   727	 */
   728	function embeddingToBuffer(embedding: Float32Array | number[]): Buffer {
   729	  if (embedding instanceof Float32Array) {
   730	    return Buffer.from(embedding.buffer, embedding.byteOffset, embedding.byteLength);
   731	  }
   732	  return Buffer.from(new Float32Array(embedding).buffer);
   733	}
   734	
   735	function repairBm25Document(args: {
   736	  previousMemoryId?: number;
   737	  memoryId: number;
   738	  documentText: string;
   739	}): { success: boolean; error?: string } {
   740	  if (!bm25Index.isBm25Enabled()) {
   741	    return { success: true };
   742	  }
   743	
   744	  try {
   745	    const bm25 = bm25Index.getIndex();
   746	    if (typeof args.previousMemoryId === 'number') {
   747	      bm25.removeDocument(String(args.previousMemoryId));
   748	    }
   749	    bm25.removeDocument(String(args.memoryId));
   750	    bm25.addDocument(String(args.memoryId), args.documentText);
   751	    return { success: true };
   752	  } catch (error: unknown) {
   753	    const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
   754	    console.warn('[reconsolidation] Immediate BM25 repair failed:', message);
   755	    return {
   756	      success: false,
   757	      error: message,
   758	    };
   759	  }
   760	}
   761	
   762	function getTableColumns(db: Database.Database, tableName: string): Set<string> {
   763	  const rows = db.prepare(`PRAGMA table_info(${tableName})`).all() as Array<{ name?: string }>;
   764	  return new Set(rows.map((row) => row.name).filter((name): name is string => typeof name === 'string'));
   765	}
   766	
   767	function ensureBm25RepairFlagColumn(db: Database.Database, tableColumns: Set<string>): void {
   768	  if (tableColumns.has('bm25_repair_needed')) {
   769	    return;
   770	  }
   771	
   772	  try {
   773	    db.exec('ALTER TABLE memory_index ADD COLUMN bm25_repair_needed INTEGER DEFAULT 0');
   774	    tableColumns.add('bm25_repair_needed');
   775	  } catch (error: unknown) {
   776	    const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
   777	    if (message.toLowerCase().includes('duplicate column')) {
   778	      tableColumns.add('bm25_repair_needed');
   779	      return;
   780	    }
   781	    console.warn('[reconsolidation] Failed to ensure bm25_repair_needed column:', message);
   782	  }
   783	}
   784	
   785	function setBm25RepairNeededFlag(
   786	  db: Database.Database,
   787	  tableColumns: Set<string>,
   788	  memoryId: number,
   789	  repairNeeded: boolean,
   790	): void {
   791	  if (!tableColumns.has('bm25_repair_needed')) {
   792	    return;
   793	  }
   794	
   795	  try {
   796	    db.prepare(`
   797	      UPDATE memory_index
   798	      SET bm25_repair_needed = ?,
   799	          updated_at = datetime('now')
   800	      WHERE id = ?
   801	    `).run(repairNeeded ? 1 : 0, memoryId);
   802	  } catch (error: unknown) {
   803	    const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
   804	    console.warn(`[reconsolidation] Failed to persist bm25_repair_needed=${repairNeeded ? 1 : 0} for memory ${memoryId}: ${message}`);
   805	  }
   806	}
   807	
   808	function getOptionalString(row: Record<string, unknown>, key: string): string | undefined {
   809	  const value = row[key];
   810	  return typeof value === 'string' && value.trim().length > 0 ? value : undefined;
   811	}
   812	
   813	function getOptionalNumber(row: Record<string, unknown>, key: string): number | undefined {
   814	  const value = row[key];
   815	  return typeof value === 'number' && Number.isFinite(value) ? value : undefined;
   816	}
   817	
   818	function capturePredecessorVersion(row: Record<string, unknown>): { contentHash: string | null; updatedAt: string | null } {
   819	  return {
   820	    contentHash: getOptionalString(row, 'content_hash') ?? null,
   821	    updatedAt: getOptionalString(row, 'updated_at') ?? null,
   822	  };
   823	}
   824	
   825	function hasPredecessorChanged(
   826	  snapshot: { contentHash: string | null; updatedAt: string | null },
   827	  currentRow: Record<string, unknown>,
   828	): boolean {
   829	  return snapshot.contentHash !== (getOptionalString(currentRow, 'content_hash') ?? null)
   830	    || snapshot.updatedAt !== (getOptionalString(currentRow, 'updated_at') ?? null);
   831	}
   832	
   833	function isArchivedRow(row: Record<string, unknown>): boolean {
   834	  const value = row.is_archived;
   835	  return value === 1 || value === true;
   836	}
   837	
   838	function buildMergeAbortResult(
   839	  existingMemory: SimilarMemory,
   840	  newMemory: NewMemoryData,
   841	  status: MergeAbortStatus,
   842	): MergeAbortedResult {
   843	  return {
   844	    action: 'complement',
   845	    status,
   846	    existingMemoryId: existingMemory.id,
   847	    newMemoryId: newMemory.id ?? 0,
   848	    similarity: existingMemory.similarity,
   849	  };
   850	}
   851	
   852	function buildMergedTriggerPhrases(
   853	  existingRow: Record<string, unknown>,
   854	  triggerPhrases?: string[],
   855	): string {
   856	  if (Array.isArray(triggerPhrases)) {
   857	    return JSON.stringify(triggerPhrases);
   858	  }
   859	  const existingValue = existingRow.trigger_phrases;
   860	  return typeof existingValue === 'string' ? existingValue : JSON.stringify([]);
===== /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/storage/mutation-ledger.ts : slice A =====
     1	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     2	// MODULE: Mutation Ledger
     3	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     4	// Feature catalog: Transaction wrappers on mutation handlers
     5	// Append-only audit trail for all memory mutations
     6	// SQLite triggers enforce immutability (no UPDATE/DELETE on ledger)
     7	import { createHash } from 'crypto';
     8	import type Database from 'better-sqlite3';
     9	import { runInTransaction } from './transaction-manager.js';
    10	
    11	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    12	   1. TYPES
    13	----------------------------------------------------------------*/
    14	
    15	type MutationType = 'create' | 'update' | 'delete' | 'merge' | 'archive' | 'restore' | 'reindex';
    16	
    17	interface MutationLedgerEntry {
    18	  id: number;
    19	  timestamp: string;
    20	  mutation_type: MutationType;
    21	  reason: string;
    22	  prior_hash: string | null;
    23	  new_hash: string;
    24	  linked_memory_ids: string; // JSON array
    25	  decision_meta: string;     // JSON object
    26	  actor: string;
    27	  session_id: string | null;
    28	}
    29	
    30	interface AppendEntryInput {
    31	  mutation_type: MutationType;
    32	  reason: string;
    33	  prior_hash: string | null;
    34	  new_hash: string;
    35	  linked_memory_ids: number[];
    36	  decision_meta: Record<string, unknown>;
    37	  actor: string;
    38	  session_id?: string | null;
    39	}
    40	
    41	interface GetEntriesOptions {
    42	  mutation_type?: MutationType;
    43	  actor?: string;
    44	  session_id?: string;
    45	  since?: string;    // ISO timestamp
    46	  until?: string;    // ISO timestamp
    47	  limit?: number;
    48	  offset?: number;
    49	}
    50	
    51	interface DivergenceReconcilePolicy {
    52	  normalizedPath: string;
    53	  attemptsSoFar: number;
    54	  nextAttempt: number;
    55	  maxRetries: number;
    56	  shouldRetry: boolean;
    57	  exhausted: boolean;
    58	}
    59	
    60	interface DivergenceEscalationPayload {
    61	  code: 'E_DIVERGENCE_RECONCILE_RETRY_EXHAUSTED';
    62	  normalizedPath: string;
    63	  attempts: number;
    64	  maxRetries: number;
    65	  recommendation: 'manual_triage_required';
    66	  reason: string;
    67	  variants: string[];
    68	}
    69	
    70	interface RecordDivergenceReconcileInput {
    71	  normalizedPath: string;
    72	  variants?: string[];
    73	  actor?: string;
    74	  session_id?: string | null;
    75	  maxRetries?: number;
    76	}
    77	
    78	interface RecordDivergenceReconcileResult {
    79	  policy: DivergenceReconcilePolicy;
    80	  retryEntryId: number | null;
    81	  escalationEntryId: number | null;
    82	  escalation: DivergenceEscalationPayload | null;
    83	}
    84	
    85	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    86	   2. SCHEMA SQL
    87	----------------------------------------------------------------*/
    88	
    89	const LEDGER_SCHEMA_SQL = `
    90	  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS mutation_ledger (
    91	    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    92	    timestamp TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (strftime('%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%fZ', 'now')),
    93	    mutation_type TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(mutation_type IN ('create','update','delete','merge','archive','restore','reindex')),
    94	    reason TEXT NOT NULL,
    95	    prior_hash TEXT,
    96	    new_hash TEXT NOT NULL,
    97	    linked_memory_ids TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]',
    98	    decision_meta TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}',
    99	    actor TEXT NOT NULL,
   100	    session_id TEXT
   101	  )
   102	`;
   103	
   104	const LEDGER_INDEX_SQL = `
   105	  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_ledger_type ON mutation_ledger(mutation_type);
   106	  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_ledger_actor ON mutation_ledger(actor);
   107	  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_ledger_timestamp ON mutation_ledger(timestamp);
   108	  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_ledger_session ON mutation_ledger(session_id);
   109	  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_ledger_memory_type_created_at ON mutation_ledger(
   110	    CAST(json_extract(linked_memory_ids, '$[0]') AS INTEGER),
   111	    mutation_type,
   112	    timestamp
   113	  )
   114	`;
   115	
   116	const LEDGER_TRIGGER_SQL = `
   117	  CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS prevent_ledger_update BEFORE UPDATE ON mutation_ledger
   118	  BEGIN SELECT RAISE(ABORT, 'mutation_ledger is append-only'); END;
   119	  CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS prevent_ledger_delete BEFORE DELETE ON mutation_ledger
   120	  BEGIN SELECT RAISE(ABORT, 'mutation_ledger is append-only'); END
   121	`;
   122	
   123	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   124	   3. INITIALIZATION
   125	----------------------------------------------------------------*/
   126	
   127	function initLedger(db: Database.Database): void {
   128	  db.exec(LEDGER_SCHEMA_SQL);
   129	  db.exec(LEDGER_INDEX_SQL);
   130	  db.exec(LEDGER_TRIGGER_SQL);
   131	}
   132	
   133	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   134	   4. HASH COMPUTATION
   135	----------------------------------------------------------------*/
   136	
   137	function computeHash(content: string): string {
   138	  return createHash('sha256').update(content, 'utf8').digest('hex');
   139	}
   140	
   141	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   142	   5. APPEND ENTRY
   143	----------------------------------------------------------------*/
   144	
   145	function appendEntry(db: Database.Database, entry: AppendEntryInput): MutationLedgerEntry {
   146	  const linkedJson = JSON.stringify(entry.linked_memory_ids);
   147	  const metaJson = JSON.stringify(entry.decision_meta);
   148	
   149	  const stmt = db.prepare(`
   150	    INSERT INTO mutation_ledger (mutation_type, reason, prior_hash, new_hash, linked_memory_ids, decision_meta, actor, session_id)
   151	    VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
   152	  `);
   153	
   154	  const result = stmt.run(
   155	    entry.mutation_type,
   156	    entry.reason,
   157	    entry.prior_hash,
   158	    entry.new_hash,
   159	    linkedJson,
   160	    metaJson,
   161	    entry.actor,
   162	    entry.session_id ?? null
   163	  );
   164	
   165	  const id = (result as { lastInsertRowid: number | bigint }).lastInsertRowid as number;
   166	
   167	  const row = db.prepare('SELECT * FROM mutation_ledger WHERE id = ?').get(id) as MutationLedgerEntry;
   168	  return row;
   169	}
   170	
   171	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   172	   6. QUERY ENTRIES
   173	----------------------------------------------------------------*/
   174	
   175	function getEntries(db: Database.Database, opts: GetEntriesOptions = {}): MutationLedgerEntry[] {
   176	  const conditions: string[] = [];
   177	  const params: Array<string | number> = [];
   178	
   179	  if (opts.mutation_type) {
   180	    conditions.push('mutation_type = ?');
   181	    params.push(opts.mutation_type);
   182	  }
   183	  if (opts.actor) {
   184	    conditions.push('actor = ?');
   185	    params.push(opts.actor);
   186	  }
   187	  if (opts.session_id) {
   188	    conditions.push('session_id = ?');
   189	    params.push(opts.session_id);
   190	  }
   191	  if (opts.since) {
   192	    conditions.push('timestamp >= ?');
   193	    params.push(opts.since);
   194	  }
   195	  if (opts.until) {
   196	    conditions.push('timestamp <= ?');
   197	    params.push(opts.until);
   198	  }
   199	
   200	  const where = conditions.length > 0 ? `WHERE ${conditions.join(' AND ')}` : '';
   201	  // String interpolation in LIMIT/OFFSET is safe here because both values
   202	  // Are coerced to non-negative integers via Math.floor + Math.max before use —
   203	  // Math.floor guarantees no decimal component, Math.max(1,…)/Math.max(0,…)
   204	  // Guarantees no negative value. No user-supplied string reaches the SQL directly.
   205	  const limit = opts.limit ? `LIMIT ${Math.max(1, Math.floor(opts.limit))}` : '';
   206	  // F-13 — OFFSET without LIMIT is invalid SQLite syntax.
   207	  // When offset is set but limit is not, emit LIMIT -1 (all rows) before OFFSET.
   208	  const offset = opts.offset ? `OFFSET ${Math.max(0, Math.floor(opts.offset))}` : '';
   209	  const effectiveLimit = !limit && offset ? 'LIMIT -1' : limit;
   210	
   211	  // String interpolation constructs IN(?,?,?) placeholder list only —
   212	  // All user values are parameterized. Accepted exception per audit H-08.
   213	  const sql = `SELECT * FROM mutation_ledger ${where} ORDER BY id ASC ${effectiveLimit} ${offset}`;
   214	  return db.prepare(sql).all(...params) as MutationLedgerEntry[];
   215	}
   216	
   217	function getEntryCount(db: Database.Database): number {
   218	  const row = db.prepare('SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM mutation_ledger').get() as { count: number };
   219	  return row.count;
   220	}
===== /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/storage/mutation-ledger.ts : slice B =====
   221	
   222	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   223	   7. LINKED ENTRIES LOOKUP
   224	----------------------------------------------------------------*/
   225	
   226	function getLinkedEntries(db: Database.Database, memoryId: number): MutationLedgerEntry[] {
   227	  // SQLite json_each can expand JSON arrays for matching
   228	  const sql = `
   229	    SELECT ml.* FROM mutation_ledger ml, json_each(ml.linked_memory_ids) je
   230	    WHERE je.value = ?
   231	    ORDER BY ml.id ASC
   232	  `;
   233	  return db.prepare(sql).all(memoryId) as MutationLedgerEntry[];
   234	}
   235	
   236	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   237	   8. APPEND-ONLY VERIFICATION
   238	----------------------------------------------------------------*/
   239	
   240	/**
   241	 * Verify that append-only triggers exist on the mutation_ledger table.
   242	 * Returns true if both UPDATE and DELETE triggers are present.
   243	 */
   244	function verifyAppendOnly(db: Database.Database): boolean {
   245	  const triggers = db.prepare(
   246	    "SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type = 'trigger' AND tbl_name = 'mutation_ledger'"
   247	  ).all() as Array<{ name: string }>;
   248	
   249	  const names = triggers.map(t => t.name);
   250	  return names.includes('prevent_ledger_update') && names.includes('prevent_ledger_delete');
   251	}
   252	
   253	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   254	   9. DIVERGENCE RETRY + ESCALATION HOOKS
   255	----------------------------------------------------------------*/
   256	
   257	const DEFAULT_DIVERGENCE_RECONCILE_MAX_RETRIES = 3;
   258	const DIVERGENCE_RECONCILE_REASON = 'alias_divergence_auto_reconcile';
   259	const DIVERGENCE_RECONCILE_ESCALATION_REASON = 'alias_divergence_auto_reconcile_escalated';
   260	const DIVERGENCE_RECONCILE_ACTOR = 'memory-index-scan';
   261	
   262	function normalizeMaxRetries(maxRetries?: number): number {
   263	  if (!Number.isFinite(maxRetries) || (maxRetries ?? 0) < 1) {
   264	    return DEFAULT_DIVERGENCE_RECONCILE_MAX_RETRIES;
   265	  }
   266	  return Math.max(1, Math.floor(maxRetries as number));
   267	}
   268	
   269	function normalizePath(value: string): string {
   270	  return value.trim().replace(/\\/g, '/');
   271	}
   272	
   273	function normalizeVariants(variants?: string[]): string[] {
   274	  if (!Array.isArray(variants)) {
   275	    return [];
   276	  }
   277	
   278	  const unique = new Set<string>();
   279	  for (const variant of variants) {
   280	    if (typeof variant !== 'string' || variant.trim().length === 0) {
   281	      continue;
   282	    }
   283	    unique.add(normalizePath(variant));
   284	  }
   285	  return Array.from(unique).sort((a, b) => a.localeCompare(b));
   286	}
   287	
   288	function getDivergenceReconcileAttemptCount(db: Database.Database, normalizedPath: string): number {
   289	  const targetPath = normalizePath(normalizedPath);
   290	  if (targetPath.length === 0) {
   291	    return 0;
   292	  }
   293	
   294	  // Use COUNT(*) with json_extract to filter by path in SQL rather than scanning
   295	  // All decision_meta rows in application code (O(1) vs O(n) full-table scan).
   296	  const row = db.prepare(`
   297	    SELECT COUNT(*) AS cnt
   298	    FROM mutation_ledger
   299	    WHERE mutation_type = 'reindex'
   300	      AND reason = ?
   301	      AND json_extract(decision_meta, '$.normalizedPath') = ?
   302	  `).get(DIVERGENCE_RECONCILE_REASON, targetPath) as { cnt: number } | undefined;
   303	
   304	  return row?.cnt ?? 0;
   305	}
   306	
   307	function hasDivergenceEscalationEntry(db: Database.Database, normalizedPath: string): boolean {
   308	  const targetPath = normalizePath(normalizedPath);
   309	  if (targetPath.length === 0) {
   310	    return false;
   311	  }
   312	
   313	  // Use COUNT(*) with json_extract to avoid O(n) full-table scan in application code.
   314	  const row = db.prepare(`
   315	    SELECT COUNT(*) AS cnt
   316	    FROM mutation_ledger
   317	    WHERE mutation_type = 'reindex'
   318	      AND reason = ?
   319	      AND json_extract(decision_meta, '$.normalizedPath') = ?
   320	  `).get(DIVERGENCE_RECONCILE_ESCALATION_REASON, targetPath) as { cnt: number } | undefined;
   321	
   322	  return (row?.cnt ?? 0) > 0;
   323	}
   324	
   325	function buildDivergenceReconcilePolicy(
   326	  normalizedPath: string,
   327	  attemptsSoFar: number,
   328	  maxRetries: number = DEFAULT_DIVERGENCE_RECONCILE_MAX_RETRIES
   329	): DivergenceReconcilePolicy {
   330	  const boundedMaxRetries = normalizeMaxRetries(maxRetries);
   331	  const safeAttempts = Math.max(0, Math.floor(attemptsSoFar));
   332	  const nextAttempt = safeAttempts + 1;
   333	  const shouldRetry = safeAttempts < boundedMaxRetries;
   334	
   335	  return {
   336	    normalizedPath: normalizePath(normalizedPath),
   337	    attemptsSoFar: safeAttempts,
   338	    nextAttempt,
   339	    maxRetries: boundedMaxRetries,
   340	    shouldRetry,
   341	    exhausted: !shouldRetry,
   342	  };
   343	}
   344	
   345	function buildDivergenceEscalationPayload(
   346	  policy: DivergenceReconcilePolicy,
   347	  variants: string[]
   348	): DivergenceEscalationPayload {
   349	  return {
   350	    code: 'E_DIVERGENCE_RECONCILE_RETRY_EXHAUSTED',
   351	    normalizedPath: policy.normalizedPath,
   352	    attempts: policy.attemptsSoFar,
   353	    maxRetries: policy.maxRetries,
   354	    recommendation: 'manual_triage_required',
   355	    reason: `Auto-reconcile exhausted after ${policy.maxRetries} attempt(s)`,
   356	    variants: normalizeVariants(variants),
   357	  };
   358	}
   359	
   360	function recordDivergenceReconcileHook(
   361	  db: Database.Database,
   362	  input: RecordDivergenceReconcileInput
   363	): RecordDivergenceReconcileResult {
   364	  initLedger(db);
   365	
   366	  const normalizedPath = normalizePath(input.normalizedPath);
   367	  if (normalizedPath.length === 0) {
   368	    throw new Error('normalizedPath is required for divergence reconciliation');
   369	  }
   370	
   371	  const maxRetries = normalizeMaxRetries(input.maxRetries);
   372	  const actor = input.actor ?? DIVERGENCE_RECONCILE_ACTOR;
   373	  const variants = normalizeVariants(input.variants);
   374	
   375	  return runInTransaction(db, () => {
   376	    const attemptsSoFar = getDivergenceReconcileAttemptCount(db, normalizedPath);
   377	    const policy = buildDivergenceReconcilePolicy(normalizedPath, attemptsSoFar, maxRetries);
   378	
   379	    if (policy.shouldRetry) {
   380	      const retryEntry = appendEntry(db, {
   381	        mutation_type: 'reindex',
   382	        reason: DIVERGENCE_RECONCILE_REASON,
   383	        prior_hash: null,
   384	        new_hash: computeHash(`${normalizedPath}|attempt:${policy.nextAttempt}|max:${policy.maxRetries}`),
   385	        linked_memory_ids: [],
   386	        decision_meta: {
   387	          normalizedPath,
   388	          attempt: policy.nextAttempt,
   389	          maxRetries: policy.maxRetries,
   390	          boundedRetry: true,
   391	          status: 'retry_scheduled',
   392	          variants,
   393	        },
   394	        actor,
   395	        session_id: input.session_id ?? null,
   396	      });
   397	
   398	      return {
   399	        policy,
   400	        retryEntryId: retryEntry.id,
   401	        escalationEntryId: null,
   402	        escalation: null,
   403	      };
   404	    }
   405	
   406	    const escalation = buildDivergenceEscalationPayload(policy, variants);
   407	    if (hasDivergenceEscalationEntry(db, normalizedPath)) {
   408	      return {
   409	        policy,
   410	        retryEntryId: null,
   411	        escalationEntryId: null,
   412	        escalation,
   413	      };
   414	    }
   415	
   416	    const escalationEntry = appendEntry(db, {
   417	      mutation_type: 'reindex',
   418	      reason: DIVERGENCE_RECONCILE_ESCALATION_REASON,
   419	      prior_hash: null,
   420	      new_hash: computeHash(`${normalizedPath}|escalated|attempts:${policy.attemptsSoFar}|max:${policy.maxRetries}`),
===== /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/storage/mutation-ledger.ts : slice C =====
   421	      linked_memory_ids: [],
   422	      decision_meta: {
   423	        normalizedPath,
   424	        attempts: policy.attemptsSoFar,
   425	        maxRetries: policy.maxRetries,
   426	        boundedRetry: true,
   427	        status: 'escalated',
   428	        escalation,
   429	      },
   430	      actor,
   431	      session_id: input.session_id ?? null,
   432	    });
   433	
   434	    return {
   435	      policy,
   436	      retryEntryId: null,
   437	      escalationEntryId: escalationEntry.id,
   438	      escalation,
   439	    };
   440	  });
   441	}

   444	   10. EXPORTS
   445	----------------------------------------------------------------*/
   446	
   447	export {
   448	  LEDGER_SCHEMA_SQL,
   449	  LEDGER_INDEX_SQL,
   450	  LEDGER_TRIGGER_SQL,
   451

   457	  getLinkedEntries,

   460	  DIVERGENCE_RECONCILE_REASON,
   461	  DIVERGENCE_RECONCILE_ESCALATION_REASON,
   462	  getDivergenceReconcileAttemptCount,
   463	  buildDivergenceReconcilePolicy,
   464	  buildDivergenceEscalationPayload,
   465	  recordDivergenceReconcileHook,
   466	};

   175	function getMemoryRow(database: Database.Database, memoryId: number): MemoryIndexRow {
   176	  const row = database.prepare('SELECT * FROM memory_index WHERE id = ?').get(memoryId) as MemoryIndexRow | undefined;
   177	  if (!row) {
