   179	  }
   180	  return row;
   181	}
   182	
   183	function normalizeTimestamp(value?: string | null): string {
   184	  if (typeof value === 'string' && value.trim().length > 0) {
   185	    return value;
   186	  }
   187	  return new Date().toISOString();
   188	}
   189	
   190	function getSafeHistoryEvents(database: Database.Database, memoryId: number): HistoryLineageEvent[] {
   191	  try {
   192	    return getHistoryEventsForLineage(memoryId, database);
   193	  } catch (error: unknown) {
   194	    logger.warn(`History events retrieval failed for memory ${memoryId}: ${error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error)}`);
   195	    return [];
   196	  }
   197	}
   198	
   199	function normalizeScopeValue(value: unknown): string | null {
   200	  if (typeof value !== 'string') {
   201	    return null;
   202	  }
   203	  const trimmed = value.trim();
   204	  return trimmed.length > 0 ? trimmed : null;
   205	}
   206	
   207	function buildScopePrefix(row: MemoryIndexRow): string | null {
   208	  const scopeTuple = [
   209	    ['tenant', normalizeScopeValue(row.tenant_id)],
   210	    ['user', normalizeScopeValue(row.user_id)],
   211	    ['agent', normalizeScopeValue(row.agent_id)],
   212	    ['session', normalizeScopeValue(row.session_id)],
   213	    ['shared_space', normalizeScopeValue(row.shared_space_id)],
   214	  ].filter((entry): entry is [string, string] => entry[1] != null);
   215	
   216	  if (scopeTuple.length === 0) {
   217	    return null;
   218	  }
   219	
   220	  const scopeHash = createHash('sha256')
===== /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/storage/lineage-state.ts : slice B =====
   221	    .update(JSON.stringify(scopeTuple), 'utf8')
   222	    .digest('hex')
   223	    .slice(0, 24);
   224	
   225	  return `scope-sha256:${scopeHash}`;
   226	}
   227	
   228	function hasLogicalKeySeparatorCollision(...components: string[]): boolean {
   229	  return components.some((component) => component.includes('::'));
   230	}
   231	
   232	function buildHashedLogicalKey(parts: {
   233	  specFolder: string;
   234	  scopePrefix: string | null;
   235	  canonicalPath: string;
   236	  anchorId: string;
   237	}): string {
   238	  const payload = JSON.stringify({
   239	    version: 2,
   240	    specFolder: parts.specFolder,
   241	    scopePrefix: parts.scopePrefix,
   242	    canonicalPath: parts.canonicalPath,
   243	    anchorId: parts.anchorId,
   244	  });
   245	  const digest = createHash('sha256')
   246	    .update(payload, 'utf8')
   247	    .digest('hex');
   248	  return `logical-sha256:${digest}`;
   249	}
   250	
   251	function buildLogicalKey(row: MemoryIndexRow): string {
   252	  const canonicalPath = typeof row.canonical_file_path === 'string' && row.canonical_file_path.trim().length > 0
   253	    ? row.canonical_file_path.trim()
   254	    : getCanonicalPathKey(row.file_path);
   255	  const anchorId = typeof row.anchor_id === 'string' && row.anchor_id.trim().length > 0
   256	    ? row.anchor_id.trim()
   257	    : '_';
   258	  const scopePrefix = buildScopePrefix(row);
   259	
   260	  if (hasLogicalKeySeparatorCollision(row.spec_folder, canonicalPath, anchorId)) {
   261	    logger.warn(
   262	      `[lineage-state] Logical key component contains '::'; using hashed structured key for spec_folder=${row.spec_folder}, path=${canonicalPath}, anchor=${anchorId}`,
   263	    );
   264	    return buildHashedLogicalKey({
   265	      specFolder: row.spec_folder,
   266	      scopePrefix,
   267	      canonicalPath,
   268	      anchorId,
   269	    });
   270	  }
   271	
   272	  if (!scopePrefix) {
   273	    return `${row.spec_folder}::${canonicalPath}::${anchorId}`;
   274	  }
   275	  return `${row.spec_folder}::${scopePrefix}::${canonicalPath}::${anchorId}`;
   276	}
   277	
   278	function getLineageRow(database: Database.Database, memoryId: number): MemoryLineageRow | null {
   279	  const row = database.prepare(`
   280	    SELECT *
   281	    FROM memory_lineage
   282	    WHERE memory_id = ?
   283	  `).get(memoryId) as MemoryLineageRow | undefined;
   284	  return row ?? null;
   285	}
   286	
   287	function getActiveProjection(database: Database.Database, logicalKey: string): ActiveProjectionRow | null {
   288	  const row = database.prepare(`
   289	    SELECT *
   290	    FROM active_memory_projection
   291	    WHERE logical_key = ?
   292	  `).get(logicalKey) as ActiveProjectionRow | undefined;
   293	  return row ?? null;
   294	}
   295	
   296	function getLatestLineageRowForLogicalKey(database: Database.Database, logicalKey: string): MemoryLineageRow | null {
   297	  const row = database.prepare(`
   298	    SELECT *
   299	    FROM memory_lineage
   300	    WHERE logical_key = ?
   301	    ORDER BY version_number DESC, created_at DESC
   302	    LIMIT 1
   303	  `).get(logicalKey) as MemoryLineageRow | undefined;
   304	  return row ?? null;
   305	}
   306	
   307	function loadLineageRowsForMemory(
   308	  database: Database.Database,
   309	  memoryId: number,
   310	): LoadedLineageRows | null {
   311	  const logicalKey = resolveLogicalKey(database, memoryId);
   312	  if (!logicalKey) {
   313	    return null;
   314	  }
   315	
   316	  const projection = getActiveProjection(database, logicalKey);
   317	  const rows = database.prepare(`
   318	    SELECT *
   319	    FROM memory_lineage
   320	    WHERE logical_key = ?
   321	    ORDER BY version_number ASC, created_at ASC
   322	  `).all(logicalKey) as MemoryLineageRow[];
   323	
   324	  return { logicalKey, projection, rows };
   325	}
   326	
   327	function isLogicalVersionConflict(error: unknown): boolean {
   328	  const message = error instanceof Error
   329	    ? error.message
   330	    : typeof error === 'object' && error !== null && typeof Reflect.get(error, 'message') === 'string'
   331	      ? String(Reflect.get(error, 'message'))
   332	      : String(error);
   333	  const code = typeof error === 'object' && error !== null && typeof Reflect.get(error, 'code') === 'string'
   334	    ? String(Reflect.get(error, 'code'))
   335	    : '';
   336	
   337	  return (
   338	    (code.includes('SQLITE_CONSTRAINT') || message.includes('SQLITE_CONSTRAINT') || message.includes('UNIQUE constraint failed'))
   339	    && message.includes('memory_lineage.logical_key')
   340	    && message.includes('memory_lineage.version_number')
   341	  );
   342	}
   343	
   344	function buildMetadata(
   345	  row: MemoryIndexRow,
   346	  actor: string,
   347	  historyEvents: HistoryLineageEvent[] = [],
   348	): string {
   349	  // D1: All fields are serialized for archival — only `.snapshot` is read back
   350	  // during lineage inspection, but the full metadata provides forensic context
   351	  // for post-mortem analysis and backfill validation.
   352	  const metadata: LineageMetadata = {
   353	    contentHash: typeof row.content_hash === 'string' ? row.content_hash : null,
   354	    filePath: row.file_path,
   355	    canonicalFilePath: typeof row.canonical_file_path === 'string' ? row.canonical_file_path : null,
   356	    anchorId: typeof row.anchor_id === 'string' ? row.anchor_id : null,
   357	    specFolder: row.spec_folder,
   358	    snapshot: row,
   359	    history: historyEvents,
   360	    actor,
   361	  };
   362	  return JSON.stringify(metadata);
   363	}
   364	
   365	function parseMetadata(row: MemoryLineageRow): LineageMetadata | null {
   366	  if (typeof row.metadata !== 'string' || row.metadata.length === 0) {
   367	    return null;
   368	  }
   369	
   370	  try {
   371	    const parsed: unknown = JSON.parse(row.metadata);
   372	    if (parsed && typeof parsed === 'object' && !Array.isArray(parsed)) {
   373	      return parsed as LineageMetadata;
   374	    }
   375	    logger.warn(`Invalid lineage metadata shape for memory ${row.memory_id}`);
   376	    return null;
   377	  } catch (error: unknown) {
   378	    logger.warn(`Failed to parse lineage metadata for memory ${row.memory_id}: ${error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error)}`);
   379	    return null;
   380	  }
   381	}
   382	
   383	function resolveSnapshotFromLineageRow(
   384	  database: Database.Database,
   385	  row: MemoryLineageRow,
   386	): ResolvedLineageSnapshot {
   387	  const metadata = parseMetadata(row);
   388	  return {
   389	    logicalKey: row.logical_key,
   390	    memoryId: row.memory_id,
   391	    versionNumber: row.version_number,
   392	    rootMemoryId: row.root_memory_id,
   393	    validFrom: row.valid_from,
   394	    validTo: row.valid_to,
   395	    transitionEvent: row.transition_event,
   396	    snapshot: metadata?.snapshot ?? getMemoryRow(database, row.memory_id),
   397	  };
   398	}
   399	
   400	function upsertActiveProjection(
   401	  database: Database.Database,
   402	  logicalKey: string,
   403	  rootMemoryId: number,
   404	  activeMemoryId: number,
   405	  updatedAt: string,
   406	): void {
   407	  // Evict any stale projection row that maps a *different* logical_key to the
   408	  // same active_memory_id — prevents UNIQUE constraint violation on re-index
   409	  // when the logical_key changes (e.g. anchor or path normalization drift).
   410	  database.prepare(
   411	    'DELETE FROM active_memory_projection WHERE active_memory_id = ? AND logical_key != ?',
   412	  ).run(activeMemoryId, logicalKey);
   413	  database.prepare(`
   414	    INSERT INTO active_memory_projection (logical_key, root_memory_id, active_memory_id, updated_at)
   415	    VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)
   416	    ON CONFLICT(logical_key) DO UPDATE SET
   417	      root_memory_id = excluded.root_memory_id,
   418	      active_memory_id = excluded.active_memory_id,
   419	      updated_at = excluded.updated_at
   420	  `).run(logicalKey, rootMemoryId, activeMemoryId, updatedAt);
===== /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/storage/lineage-state.ts : slice C =====
   421	}
   422	
   423	function bindHistory(database: Database.Database): void {
   424	  if (typeof (database as Database.Database & { exec?: unknown }).exec === 'function') {
   425	    initHistory(database);
   426	  }
   427	}
   428	
   429	function markHistoricalPredecessor(database: Database.Database, memoryId: number, updatedAt: string): void {
   430	  database.prepare(`
   431	    UPDATE memory_index
   432	    SET importance_tier = CASE
   433	          WHEN importance_tier = 'constitutional' THEN importance_tier
   434	          ELSE 'deprecated'
   435	        END,
   436	        updated_at = ?
   437	    WHERE id = ?
   438	  `).run(updatedAt, memoryId);
   439	}
   440	
   441	function insertAppendOnlyMemoryIndexRow(params: CreateAppendOnlyMemoryRecordParams): number {
   442	  const { database, parsed, filePath, embedding, embeddingFailureReason } = params;
   443	  const now = new Date().toISOString();
   444	  const canonicalFilePath = getCanonicalPathKey(filePath);
   445	  const importanceWeight = calculateDocumentWeight(filePath, parsed.documentType);
   446	  const specLevel = isSpecDocumentType(parsed.documentType)
   447	    ? detectSpecLevelFromParsed(filePath)
   448	    : null;
   449	  const encodingIntent = isEncodingIntentEnabled()
   450	    ? classifyEncodingIntent(parsed.content)
   451	    : undefined;
   452	  const embeddingStatus = embedding
   453	    ? (sqlite_vec_available() ? 'success' : 'pending')
   454	    : 'pending';
   455	
   456	  const result = database.prepare(`
   457	    INSERT INTO memory_index (
   458	      spec_folder,
   459	      file_path,
   460	      canonical_file_path,
   461	      title,
   462	      trigger_phrases,
   463	      importance_weight,
   464	      created_at,
   465	      updated_at,
   466	      embedding_model,
   467	      embedding_generated_at,
   468	      embedding_status,
   469	      failure_reason,
   470	      encoding_intent,
   471	      document_type,
   472	      spec_level,
   473	      content_text,
   474	      quality_score,
   475	      quality_flags
   476	    ) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
   477	  `).run(
   478	    parsed.specFolder,
   479	    filePath,
   480	    canonicalFilePath,
   481	    parsed.title,
   482	    JSON.stringify(parsed.triggerPhrases),
   483	    importanceWeight,
   484	    now,
   485	    now,
   486	    embeddingsProvider.getModelName(),
   487	    embedding ? now : null,
   488	    embeddingStatus,
   489	    embeddingFailureReason,
   490	    encodingIntent ?? 'document',
   491	    parsed.documentType || 'memory',
   492	    specLevel,
   493	    parsed.content,
   494	    parsed.qualityScore ?? 0,
   495	    JSON.stringify(parsed.qualityFlags ?? []),
   496	  );
   497	
   498	  const memoryId = Number(result.lastInsertRowid);
   499	  applyPostInsertMetadata(database, memoryId, {
   500	    content_hash: parsed.contentHash,
   501	    context_type: parsed.contextType,
   502	    importance_tier: parsed.importanceTier,
   503	    memory_type: parsed.memoryType,
   504	    type_inference_source: parsed.memoryTypeSource,
   505	    encoding_intent: encodingIntent,
   506	    document_type: parsed.documentType || 'memory',
   507	    spec_level: specLevel,
   508	    quality_score: parsed.qualityScore ?? 0,
   509	    quality_flags: JSON.stringify(parsed.qualityFlags ?? []),
   510	  });
   511	
   512	  if (embedding && sqlite_vec_available()) {
   513	    const expectedDim = get_embedding_dim();
   514	    if (embedding.length !== expectedDim) {
   515	      throw new Error(`Embedding must be ${expectedDim} dimensions, got ${embedding.length}`);
   516	    }
   517	
   518	    database.prepare(`
   519	      INSERT INTO vec_memories (rowid, embedding)
   520	      VALUES (?, ?)
   521	    `).run(BigInt(memoryId), to_embedding_buffer(embedding));
   522	  }
   523	
   524	  if (bm25Index.isBm25Enabled()) {
   525	    try {
   526	      bm25Index.getIndex().addDocument(String(memoryId), bm25Index.buildBm25DocumentText({
   527	        title: parsed.title,
   528	        content_text: parsed.content,
   529	        trigger_phrases: parsed.triggerPhrases,
   530	        file_path: filePath,
   531	      }));
   532	    } catch (error: unknown) {
   533	      logger.warn(`BM25 index update failed for memory ${memoryId}: ${error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error)}`);
   534	    }
   535	  }
   536	
   537	  try {
   538	    recordHistory(memoryId, 'ADD', null, parsed.title ?? filePath, params.actor ?? 'mcp:memory_save');
   539	  } catch (error: unknown) {
   540	    logger.warn(`History record failed for memory ${memoryId}: ${error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error)}`);
   541	  }
   542	
   543	  refresh_interference_scores_for_folder(database, parsed.specFolder);
   544	  return memoryId;
   545	}
   546	
   547	// R1: Shared early-return helper for lineage functions.
   548	function getExistingLineageTransition(
   549	  database: Database.Database,
   550	  memoryId: number,
   551	): RecordedLineageTransition | null {
   552	  const existing = getLineageRow(database, memoryId);
   553	  if (existing) {
   554	    return {
   555	      logicalKey: existing.logical_key,
   556	      versionNumber: existing.version_number,
   557	      rootMemoryId: existing.root_memory_id,
   558	      activeMemoryId: existing.memory_id,
   559	      predecessorMemoryId: existing.predecessor_memory_id,
   560	      transitionEvent: existing.transition_event,
   561	    };
   562	  }
   563	  return null;
   564	}
   565	
   566	/**
   567	 * Validates transition input combinations before persisting.
   568	 * Rejects invalid event/predecessor pairings and backwards timestamps.
   569	 */
   570	function validateTransitionInput(
   571	  transitionEvent: string,
   572	  predecessorMemoryId: number | null,
   573	  validFrom: string,
   574	  predecessor: LineageRow | null,
   575	): void {
   576	  if (transitionEvent === 'CREATE' && predecessorMemoryId != null) {
   577	    throw new Error(`E_LINEAGE: CREATE transition must not specify a predecessor (got ${predecessorMemoryId})`);
   578	  }
   579	  if (transitionEvent === 'SUPERSEDE' && predecessorMemoryId == null) {
   580	    throw new Error('E_LINEAGE: SUPERSEDE transition requires a predecessor');
   581	  }
   582	  // M6 FIX: Compare timestamps as numeric epoch values, not raw strings,
   583	  // to handle non-ISO strings and timezone-offset variants correctly.
   584	  if (predecessor && new Date(validFrom).getTime() < new Date(predecessor.valid_from).getTime()) {
   585	    throw new Error(
   586	      `E_LINEAGE: valid_from (${validFrom}) is earlier than predecessor valid_from (${predecessor.valid_from})`,
   587	    );
   588	  }
   589	}
   590	
   591	/**
   592	 * Seed lineage state from an existing memory row when no lineage entry exists yet.
   593	 *
   594	 * @param database - Database connection that stores lineage state.
   595	 * @param memoryId - Memory version to seed into lineage tables.
   596	 * @param options - Optional actor, timestamps, and transition metadata.
   597	 * @returns Seeded lineage state for the requested memory version.
   598	 */
   599	export function seedLineageFromCurrentState(
   600	  database: Database.Database,
   601	  memoryId: number,
   602	  options: RecordLineageTransitionOptions = {},
   603	): RecordedLineageTransition {
   604	  bindHistory(database);
   605	  ensureLineageTables(database);
   606	
   607	  const cached = getExistingLineageTransition(database, memoryId);
   608	  if (cached) return cached;
   609	
   610	  const row = getMemoryRow(database, memoryId);
   611	  const logicalKey = buildLogicalKey(row);
   612	  const actor = options.actor ?? 'system';
   613	  const historyEvents = options.historyEvents ?? getSafeHistoryEvents(database, memoryId);
   614	  const validFrom = options.validFrom
   615	    ?? historyEvents[0]?.timestamp
   616	    ?? normalizeTimestamp(row.created_at ?? row.updated_at);
   617	
   618	  const seedTx = database.transaction(() => {
   619	    database.prepare(`
   620	      INSERT INTO memory_lineage (
   621	        memory_id,
   622	        logical_key,
   623	        version_number,
   624	        root_memory_id,
   625	        predecessor_memory_id,
   626	        superseded_by_memory_id,
   627	        valid_from,
   628	        valid_to,
   629	        transition_event,
   630	        actor,
   631	        metadata
   632	      ) VALUES (?, ?, 1, ?, NULL, NULL, ?, NULL, ?, ?, ?)
   633	    `).run(
   634	      memoryId,
   635	      logicalKey,
   636	      memoryId,
   637	      validFrom,
   638	      options.transitionEvent ?? 'BACKFILL',
   639	      actor,
   640	      buildMetadata(row, actor, historyEvents),
   641	    );
   642	
   643	    upsertActiveProjection(database, logicalKey, memoryId, memoryId, normalizeTimestamp(row.updated_at ?? validFrom));
   644	  });
   645	
   646	  seedTx();
   647	
   648	  return {
   649	    logicalKey,
   650	    versionNumber: 1,
   651	    rootMemoryId: memoryId,
   652	    activeMemoryId: memoryId,
   653	    predecessorMemoryId: null,
   654	    transitionEvent: options.transitionEvent ?? 'BACKFILL',
   655	  };
   656	}
   657	
   658	/**
   659	 * Seed or append a lineage transition for a memory version.
   660	 *
   661	 * @param database - Database connection that stores lineage state.
   662	 * @param memoryId - Memory version being recorded.
   663	 * @param options - Transition details such as predecessor and actor.
   664	 * @returns Recorded lineage state for the requested memory version.
   665	 */
   666	export function recordLineageTransition(
   667	  database: Database.Database,
   668	  memoryId: number,
   669	  options: RecordLineageTransitionOptions = {},
   670	): RecordedLineageTransition {
   671	  bindHistory(database);
   672	  ensureLineageTables(database);
   673	
   674	  const cached = getExistingLineageTransition(database, memoryId);
   675	  if (cached) return cached;
   676	
   677	  for (let attempt = 0; attempt <= MAX_LINEAGE_VERSION_RETRIES; attempt += 1) {
   678	    // A1/B14: Wrap predecessor UPDATE + lineage INSERT + projection UPSERT in a transaction.
   679	    const recordTransitionTx = database.transaction(() => {
   680	      const row = getMemoryRow(database, memoryId);
   681	      const rowLogicalKey = buildLogicalKey(row);
   682	      const actor = options.actor ?? 'system';
   683	      const transitionEvent = options.transitionEvent ?? 'CREATE';
   684	      const historyEvents = options.historyEvents ?? getSafeHistoryEvents(database, memoryId);
   685	      const predecessorMemoryId = options.predecessorMemoryId ?? null;
   686	      const validFrom = options.validFrom ?? normalizeTimestamp(row.updated_at ?? row.created_at);
   687	
   688	      let logicalKey = rowLogicalKey;
   689	      let rootMemoryId = memoryId;
   690	      let versionNumber = 1;
   691	      let predecessor: LineageRow | null = null;
   692	
   693	      if (predecessorMemoryId != null) {
   694	        predecessor = getLineageRow(database, predecessorMemoryId);
   695	        if (predecessor) {
   696	          if (predecessor.logical_key !== rowLogicalKey) {
   697	            throw new Error(
   698	              `E_LINEAGE: predecessor ${predecessorMemoryId} logical identity ${predecessor.logical_key} ` +
   699	              `does not match memory ${memoryId} logical identity ${rowLogicalKey}`,
   700	            );
   701	          }
   702	          logicalKey = predecessor.logical_key;
   703	          rootMemoryId = predecessor.root_memory_id;
   704	          versionNumber = predecessor.version_number + 1;
   705	        } else {
   706	          const seeded = seedLineageFromCurrentState(database, predecessorMemoryId, {
   707	            actor,
   708	            transitionEvent: 'BACKFILL',
   709	          });
   710	          if (seeded.logicalKey !== rowLogicalKey) {
   711	            throw new Error(
   712	              `E_LINEAGE: predecessor ${predecessorMemoryId} logical identity ${seeded.logicalKey} ` +
   713	              `does not match memory ${memoryId} logical identity ${rowLogicalKey}`,
   714	            );
   715	          }
   716	          logicalKey = seeded.logicalKey;
   717	          rootMemoryId = seeded.rootMemoryId;
   718	          versionNumber = seeded.versionNumber + 1;
   719	          predecessor = getLineageRow(database, predecessorMemoryId);
   720	        }
   721	      }
   722	
   723	      if (attempt > 0) {
   724	        const latest = getLatestLineageRowForLogicalKey(database, logicalKey);
   725	        if (latest) {
   726	          rootMemoryId = latest.root_memory_id;
   727	          versionNumber = latest.version_number + 1;
   728	        }
   729	      }
   730	
   731	      validateTransitionInput(transitionEvent, predecessorMemoryId, validFrom, predecessor);
   732	
   733	      if (predecessorMemoryId != null) {
   734	        if (predecessor && predecessor.valid_to) {
   735	          logger.warn(
   736	            `Predecessor ${predecessorMemoryId} already superseded (valid_to: ${predecessor.valid_to}). ` +
   737	            'COALESCE will preserve the existing valid_to.',
   738	          );
   739	        }
   740	        database.prepare(`
   741	          UPDATE memory_lineage
   742	          SET valid_to = COALESCE(valid_to, ?),
   743	              superseded_by_memory_id = COALESCE(superseded_by_memory_id, ?)
   744	          WHERE memory_id = ?
   745	        `).run(validFrom, memoryId, predecessorMemoryId);
   746	        markHistoricalPredecessor(database, predecessorMemoryId, validFrom);
   747	      }
   748	
   749	      database.prepare(`
   750	        INSERT INTO memory_lineage (
   751	          memory_id,
   752	          logical_key,
   753	          version_number,
   754	          root_memory_id,
   755	          predecessor_memory_id,
   756	          superseded_by_memory_id,
   757	          valid_from,
   758	          valid_to,
   759	          transition_event,
   760	          actor,
   761	          metadata
   762	        ) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, NULL, ?, NULL, ?, ?, ?)
   763	      `).run(
   764	        memoryId,
   765	        logicalKey,
   766	        versionNumber,
   767	        rootMemoryId,
   768	        predecessorMemoryId,
   769	        validFrom,
   770	        transitionEvent,
   771	        actor,
   772	        buildMetadata(row, actor, historyEvents),
   773	      );
   774	
   775	      upsertActiveProjection(database, logicalKey, rootMemoryId, memoryId, validFrom);
   776	
   777	      return {
   778	        logicalKey,
   779	        versionNumber,
   780	        rootMemoryId,
   781	        activeMemoryId: memoryId,
   782	        predecessorMemoryId,
   783	        transitionEvent,
   784	      } as RecordedLineageTransition;
   785	    });
   786	
   787	    try {
   788	      return recordTransitionTx();
   789	    } catch (error: unknown) {
   790	      const shouldRetry = isLogicalVersionConflict(error) && attempt < MAX_LINEAGE_VERSION_RETRIES;
   791	      if (!shouldRetry) {
   792	        throw error;
   793	      }
   794	
   795	      logger.warn(
   796	        `Retrying lineage insert for memory ${memoryId} after logical_key/version_number conflict ` +
   797	        `(attempt ${attempt + 1}/${MAX_LINEAGE_VERSION_RETRIES})`,
   798	      );
   799	    }
   800	  }
   801	
   802	  throw new Error(`E_LINEAGE: exhausted retries while recording lineage for memory ${memoryId}`);
   803	}
   804	
   805	/**
   806	 * Create a new append-only memory row and wire it into lineage state.
   807	 *
   808	 * @param params - Parsed memory payload and append-only lineage metadata.
   809	 * @returns Identifier of the newly inserted memory row.
   810	 */
   811	export function createAppendOnlyMemoryRecord(params: CreateAppendOnlyMemoryRecordParams): number {
   812	  bindHistory(params.database);
   813	  const appendTx = params.database.transaction(() => {
   814	    const memoryId = insertAppendOnlyMemoryIndexRow(params);
   815	    recordLineageTransition(params.database, memoryId, {
   816	      actor: params.actor ?? 'mcp:memory_save',
   817	      predecessorMemoryId: params.predecessorMemoryId,
   818	      transitionEvent: 'SUPERSEDE',
   819	    });
   820	    return memoryId;
   821	  });
   822	
   823	  return appendTx();
   824	}
   825	
   826	function resolveLogicalKey(database: Database.Database, memoryId: number): string | null {
   827	  const row = getLineageRow(database, memoryId);
   828	  if (row) {
   829	    return row.logical_key;
   830	  }
   831	
   832	  const memoryRow = getMemoryRow(database, memoryId);
   833	  const projection = getActiveProjection(database, buildLogicalKey(memoryRow));
   834	  return projection?.logical_key ?? buildLogicalKey(memoryRow);
   835	}
   836	
   837	/**
   838	 * Return the full ordered lineage chain for the logical key behind a memory.
   839	 *
   840	 * @param database - Database connection that stores lineage state.
   841	 * @param memoryId - Memory identifier used to resolve the logical key.
   842	 * @returns Ordered lineage snapshots from oldest to newest version.
   843	 */
   844	export function inspectLineageChain(database: Database.Database, memoryId: number): ResolvedLineageSnapshot[] {
   845	  bindHistory(database);
   846	  ensureLineageTables(database);
   847	  const lineage = loadLineageRowsForMemory(database, memoryId);
   848	  if (!lineage) {
   849	    return [];
   850	  }
   851	
   852	  return lineage.rows.map((row) => resolveSnapshotFromLineageRow(database, row));
   853	}
   854	
   855	/**
   856	 * Build a compact operator-facing summary for the lineage behind a memory.
   857	 *
   858	 * @param database - Database connection that stores lineage state.
   859	 * @param memoryId - Memory identifier used to resolve the logical key.
   860	 * @returns Aggregated lineage summary when one exists.

codex
I have enough to state one high-confidence theme already: Mex enforces continuity mostly through filenames, frontmatter, and prompts, whereas our system encodes continuity in DB state and lineage. I’m grabbing just the exact line ranges for those DB guarantees so the final comparison can stay precise and not overclaim.

 succeeded in 0ms:

     2	// MODULE: Mutation Ledger
     3	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     4	// Feature catalog: Transaction wrappers on mutation handlers
     5	// Append-only audit trail for all memory mutations
     6	// SQLite triggers enforce immutability (no UPDATE/DELETE on ledger)
     7	import { createHash } from 'crypto';
     8	import type Database from 'better-sqlite3';
     9	import { runInTransaction } from './transaction-manager.js';
    10	
    11	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    12	   1. TYPES
    13	----------------------------------------------------------------*/
    14	
    15	type MutationType = 'create' | 'update' | 'delete' | 'merge' | 'archive' | 'restore' | 'reindex';
    16	
    17	interface MutationLedgerEntry {
    18	  id: number;
    19	  timestamp: string;
    20	  mutation_type: MutationType;
    21	  reason: string;
    22	  prior_hash: string | null;
    23	  new_hash: string;
    24	  linked_memory_ids: string; // JSON array
    25	  decision_meta: string;     // JSON object
    26	  actor: string;
    27	  session_id: string | null;
    28	}
    29	
    30	interface AppendEntryInput {
    31	  mutation_type: MutationType;
    32	  reason: string;
    33	  prior_hash: string | null;
    34	  new_hash: string;
    35	  linked_memory_ids: number[];
    36	  decision_meta: Record<string, unknown>;
    37	  actor: string;
    38	  session_id?: string | null;
    39	}
    40	
    41	interface GetEntriesOptions {
    42	  mutation_type?: MutationType;
    43	  actor?: string;
    44	  session_id?: string;
    45	  since?: string;    // ISO timestamp
    46	  until?: string;    // ISO timestamp
    47	  limit?: number;
    48	  offset?: number;
    49	}
    50	
    51	interface DivergenceReconcilePolicy {
    52	  normalizedPath: string;
    53	  attemptsSoFar: number;
    54	  nextAttempt: number;
    55	  maxRetries: number;
    56	  shouldRetry: boolean;
    57	  exhausted: boolean;
    58	}
    59	
    60	interface DivergenceEscalationPayload {
    61	  code: 'E_DIVERGENCE_RECONCILE_RETRY_EXHAUSTED';
    62	  normalizedPath: string;
    63	  attempts: number;
    64	  maxRetries: number;
    65	  recommendation: 'manual_triage_required';
    66	  reason: string;
    67	  variants: string[];
    68	}
    69	
    70	interface RecordDivergenceReconcileInput {
    71	  normalizedPath: string;
    72	  variants?: string[];
    73	  actor?: string;
    74	  session_id?: string | null;
    75	  maxRetries?: number;
    76	}
    77	
    78	interface RecordDivergenceReconcileResult {
    79	  policy: DivergenceReconcilePolicy;
    80	  retryEntryId: number | null;
    81	  escalationEntryId: number | null;
    82	  escalation: DivergenceEscalationPayload | null;
    83	}
    84	
    85	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    86	   2. SCHEMA SQL
    87	----------------------------------------------------------------*/
    88	
    89	const LEDGER_SCHEMA_SQL = `
    90	  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS mutation_ledger (
    91	    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    92	    timestamp TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (strftime('%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%fZ', 'now')),
    93	    mutation_type TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(mutation_type IN ('create','update','delete','merge','archive','restore','reindex')),
    94	    reason TEXT NOT NULL,
    95	    prior_hash TEXT,
    96	    new_hash TEXT NOT NULL,
    97	    linked_memory_ids TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]',
    98	    decision_meta TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}',
    99	    actor TEXT NOT NULL,
   100	    session_id TEXT
   101	  )
   102	`;
   103	
   104	const LEDGER_INDEX_SQL = `
   105	  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_ledger_type ON mutation_ledger(mutation_type);
   106	  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_ledger_actor ON mutation_ledger(actor);
   107	  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_ledger_timestamp ON mutation_ledger(timestamp);
   108	  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_ledger_session ON mutation_ledger(session_id);
   109	  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_ledger_memory_type_created_at ON mutation_ledger(
   110	    CAST(json_extract(linked_memory_ids, '$[0]') AS INTEGER),
   111	    mutation_type,
   112	    timestamp
   113	  )
   114	`;
   115	
   116	const LEDGER_TRIGGER_SQL = `
   117	  CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS prevent_ledger_update BEFORE UPDATE ON mutation_ledger
   118	  BEGIN SELECT RAISE(ABORT, 'mutation_ledger is append-only'); END;
   119	  CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS prevent_ledger_delete BEFORE DELETE ON mutation_ledger
   120	  BEGIN SELECT RAISE(ABORT, 'mutation_ledger is append-only'); END
   121	`;
   122	
   123	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   124	   3. INITIALIZATION
   125	----------------------------------------------------------------*/
   126	
   127	function initLedger(db: Database.Database): void {
   128	  db.exec(LEDGER_SCHEMA_SQL);
   129	  db.exec(LEDGER_INDEX_SQL);
   130	  db.exec(LEDGER_TRIGGER_SQL);
   131	}
   132	
   133	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   134	   4. HASH COMPUTATION
   135	----------------------------------------------------------------*/
   136	
   137	function computeHash(content: string): string {
   138	  return createHash('sha256').update(content, 'utf8').digest('hex');
   139	}
   140	
   141	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   142	   5. APPEND ENTRY
   143	----------------------------------------------------------------*/
   144	
   145	function appendEntry(db: Database.Database, entry: AppendEntryInput): MutationLedgerEntry {
   146	  const linkedJson = JSON.stringify(entry.linked_memory_ids);
   147	  const metaJson = JSON.stringify(entry.decision_meta);
   148	
   149	  const stmt = db.prepare(`
   150	    INSERT INTO mutation_ledger (mutation_type, reason, prior_hash, new_hash, linked_memory_ids, decision_meta, actor, session_id)
   151	    VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
   152	  `);
   153	
   154	  const result = stmt.run(
   155	    entry.mutation_type,
   156	    entry.reason,
   157	    entry.prior_hash,
   158	    entry.new_hash,
   159	    linkedJson,
   160	    metaJson,
   161	    entry.actor,
   162	    entry.session_id ?? null
   163	  );
   164	
   165	  const id = (result as { lastInsertRowid: number | bigint }).lastInsertRowid as number;
   166	
   167	  const row = db.prepare('SELECT * FROM mutation_ledger WHERE id = ?').get(id) as MutationLedgerEntry;
   168	  return row;
   169	}
   170	
   171	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   172	   6. QUERY ENTRIES
   173	----------------------------------------------------------------*/
   174	
   175	function getEntries(db: Database.Database, opts: GetEntriesOptions = {}): MutationLedgerEntry[] {
   176	  const conditions: string[] = [];
   177	  const params: Array<string | number> = [];
   178	
   179	  if (opts.mutation_type) {
   180	    conditions.push('mutation_type = ?');
   181	    params.push(opts.mutation_type);
   182	  }
   183	  if (opts.actor) {
   184	    conditions.push('actor = ?');
   185	    params.push(opts.actor);
   186	  }
   187	  if (opts.session_id) {
   188	    conditions.push('session_id = ?');
   189	    params.push(opts.session_id);
   190	  }
   191	  if (opts.since) {
   192	    conditions.push('timestamp >= ?');
   193	    params.push(opts.since);
   194	  }
   195	  if (opts.until) {
   196	    conditions.push('timestamp <= ?');
   197	    params.push(opts.until);
   198	  }
   199	
   200	  const where = conditions.length > 0 ? `WHERE ${conditions.join(' AND ')}` : '';
   201	  // String interpolation in LIMIT/OFFSET is safe here because both values
   202	  // Are coerced to non-negative integers via Math.floor + Math.max before use —
   203	  // Math.floor guarantees no decimal component, Math.max(1,…)/Math.max(0,…)
   204	  // Guarantees no negative value. No user-supplied string reaches the SQL directly.
   205	  const limit = opts.limit ? `LIMIT ${Math.max(1, Math.floor(opts.limit))}` : '';
   206	  // F-13 — OFFSET without LIMIT is invalid SQLite syntax.
   207	  // When offset is set but limit is not, emit LIMIT -1 (all rows) before OFFSET.
   208	  const offset = opts.offset ? `OFFSET ${Math.max(0, Math.floor(opts.offset))}` : '';
   209	  const effectiveLimit = !limit && offset ? 'LIMIT -1' : limit;
   210	
   211	  // String interpolation constructs IN(?,?,?) placeholder list only —
   212	  // All user values are parameterized. Accepted exception per audit H-08.
   213	  const sql = `SELECT * FROM mutation_ledger ${where} ORDER BY id ASC ${effectiveLimit} ${offset}`;
   214	  return db.prepare(sql).all(...params) as MutationLedgerEntry[];
   215	}
   216	
   217	function getEntryCount(db: Database.Database): number {
   218	  const row = db.prepare('SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM mutation_ledger').get() as { count: number };
   219	  return row.count;
   220	}
   221	
   222	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   223	   7. LINKED ENTRIES LOOKUP
   224	----------------------------------------------------------------*/
   225	
   226	function getLinkedEntries(db: Database.Database, memoryId: number): MutationLedgerEntry[] {
   227	  // SQLite json_each can expand JSON arrays for matching
   228	  const sql = `
   229	    SELECT ml.* FROM mutation_ledger ml, json_each(ml.linked_memory_ids) je
   230	    WHERE je.value = ?
   231	    ORDER BY ml.id ASC
   232	  `;
   233	  return db.prepare(sql).all(memoryId) as MutationLedgerEntry[];
   234	}
   235	
   236	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   237	   8. APPEND-ONLY VERIFICATION
   238	----------------------------------------------------------------*/
   239	
   240	/**
   241	 * Verify that append-only triggers exist on the mutation_ledger table.
   242	 * Returns true if both UPDATE and DELETE triggers are present.
   243	 */
   244	function verifyAppendOnly(db: Database.Database): boolean {
   245	  const triggers = db.prepare(
   246	    "SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type = 'trigger' AND tbl_name = 'mutation_ledger'"
   247	  ).all() as Array<{ name: string }>;
   248	
   249	  const names = triggers.map(t => t.name);
   250	  return names.includes('prevent_ledger_update') && names.includes('prevent_ledger_delete');
   251	}
   252	
   253	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   254	   9. DIVERGENCE RETRY + ESCALATION HOOKS
   255	----------------------------------------------------------------*/
   256	
   257	const DEFAULT_DIVERGENCE_RECONCILE_MAX_RETRIES = 3;
   258	const DIVERGENCE_RECONCILE_REASON = 'alias_divergence_auto_reconcile';
   259	const DIVERGENCE_RECONCILE_ESCALATION_REASON = 'alias_divergence_auto_reconcile_escalated';
   260	const DIVERGENCE_RECONCILE_ACTOR = 'memory-index-scan';
===== B =====
   261	
   262	function normalizeMaxRetries(maxRetries?: number): number {
   263	  if (!Number.isFinite(maxRetries) || (maxRetries ?? 0) < 1) {
   264	    return DEFAULT_DIVERGENCE_RECONCILE_MAX_RETRIES;
   265	  }
   266	  return Math.max(1, Math.floor(maxRetries as number));
   267	}
   268	
   269	function normalizePath(value: string): string {
   270	  return value.trim().replace(/\\/g, '/');
   271	}
   272	
   273	function normalizeVariants(variants?: string[]): string[] {
   274	  if (!Array.isArray(variants)) {
   275	    return [];
   276	  }
   277	
   278	  const unique = new Set<string>();
   279	  for (const variant of variants) {
   280	    if (typeof variant !== 'string' || variant.trim().length === 0) {
   281	      continue;
   282	    }
   283	    unique.add(normalizePath(variant));
   284	  }
   285	  return Array.from(unique).sort((a, b) => a.localeCompare(b));
   286	}
   287	
   288	function getDivergenceReconcileAttemptCount(db: Database.Database, normalizedPath: string): number {
   289	  const targetPath = normalizePath(normalizedPath);
   290	  if (targetPath.length === 0) {
   291	    return 0;
   292	  }
   293	
   294	  // Use COUNT(*) with json_extract to filter by path in SQL rather than scanning
   295	  // All decision_meta rows in application code (O(1) vs O(n) full-table scan).
   296	  const row = db.prepare(`
   297	    SELECT COUNT(*) AS cnt
   298	    FROM mutation_ledger
   299	    WHERE mutation_type = 'reindex'
   300	      AND reason = ?
   301	      AND json_extract(decision_meta, '$.normalizedPath') = ?
   302	  `).get(DIVERGENCE_RECONCILE_REASON, targetPath) as { cnt: number } | undefined;
   303	
   304	  return row?.cnt ?? 0;
   305	}
   306	
   307	function hasDivergenceEscalationEntry(db: Database.Database, normalizedPath: string): boolean {
   308	  const targetPath = normalizePath(normalizedPath);
   309	  if (targetPath.length === 0) {
   310	    return false;
   311	  }
   312	
   313	  // Use COUNT(*) with json_extract to avoid O(n) full-table scan in application code.
   314	  const row = db.prepare(`
   315	    SELECT COUNT(*) AS cnt
   316	    FROM mutation_ledger
   317	    WHERE mutation_type = 'reindex'
   318	      AND reason = ?
   319	      AND json_extract(decision_meta, '$.normalizedPath') = ?
   320	  `).get(DIVERGENCE_RECONCILE_ESCALATION_REASON, targetPath) as { cnt: number } | undefined;
   321	
   322	  return (row?.cnt ?? 0) > 0;
   323	}
   324	
   325	function buildDivergenceReconcilePolicy(
   326	  normalizedPath: string,
   327	  attemptsSoFar: number,
   328	  maxRetries: number = DEFAULT_DIVERGENCE_RECONCILE_MAX_RETRIES
   329	): DivergenceReconcilePolicy {
   330	  const boundedMaxRetries = normalizeMaxRetries(maxRetries);
   331	  const safeAttempts = Math.max(0, Math.floor(attemptsSoFar));
   332	  const nextAttempt = safeAttempts + 1;
   333	  const shouldRetry = safeAttempts < boundedMaxRetries;
   334	
   335	  return {
   336	    normalizedPath: normalizePath(normalizedPath),
   337	    attemptsSoFar: safeAttempts,
   338	    nextAttempt,
   339	    maxRetries: boundedMaxRetries,
   340	    shouldRetry,
   341	    exhausted: !shouldRetry,
   342	  };
   343	}
   344	
   345	function buildDivergenceEscalationPayload(
   346	  policy: DivergenceReconcilePolicy,
   347	  variants: string[]
   348	): DivergenceEscalationPayload {
   349	  return {
   350	    code: 'E_DIVERGENCE_RECONCILE_RETRY_EXHAUSTED',
   351	    normalizedPath: policy.normalizedPath,
   352	    attempts: policy.attemptsSoFar,
   353	    maxRetries: policy.maxRetries,
   354	    recommendation: 'manual_triage_required',
   355	    reason: `Auto-reconcile exhausted after ${policy.maxRetries} attempt(s)`,
   356	    variants: normalizeVariants(variants),
   357	  };
   358	}
   359	
   360	function recordDivergenceReconcileHook(
   361	  db: Database.Database,
   362	  input: RecordDivergenceReconcileInput
   363	): RecordDivergenceReconcileResult {
   364	  initLedger(db);
   365	
   366	  const normalizedPath = normalizePath(input.normalizedPath);
   367	  if (normalizedPath.length === 0) {
   368	    throw new Error('normalizedPath is required for divergence reconciliation');
   369	  }
   370	
   371	  const maxRetries = normalizeMaxRetries(input.maxRetries);
   372	  const actor = input.actor ?? DIVERGENCE_RECONCILE_ACTOR;
   373	  const variants = normalizeVariants(input.variants);
   374	
   375	  return runInTransaction(db, () => {
   376	    const attemptsSoFar = getDivergenceReconcileAttemptCount(db, normalizedPath);
   377	    const policy = buildDivergenceReconcilePolicy(normalizedPath, attemptsSoFar, maxRetries);
   378	
   379	    if (policy.shouldRetry) {
   380	      const retryEntry = appendEntry(db, {
   381	        mutation_type: 'reindex',
   382	        reason: DIVERGENCE_RECONCILE_REASON,
   383	        prior_hash: null,
   384	        new_hash: computeHash(`${normalizedPath}|attempt:${policy.nextAttempt}|max:${policy.maxRetries}`),
   385	        linked_memory_ids: [],
   386	        decision_meta: {
   387	          normalizedPath,
   388	          attempt: policy.nextAttempt,
   389	          maxRetries: policy.maxRetries,
   390	          boundedRetry: true,
   391	          status: 'retry_scheduled',
   392	          variants,
   393	        },
   394	        actor,
   395	        session_id: input.session_id ?? null,
   396	      });
   397	
   398	      return {
   399	        policy,
   400	        retryEntryId: retryEntry.id,
   401	        escalationEntryId: null,
   402	        escalation: null,
   403	      };
   404	    }
   405	
   406	    const escalation = buildDivergenceEscalationPayload(policy, variants);
   407	    if (hasDivergenceEscalationEntry(db, normalizedPath)) {
   408	      return {
   409	        policy,
   410	        retryEntryId: null,
   411	        escalationEntryId: null,
   412	        escalation,
   413	      };
   414	    }
   415	
   416	    const escalationEntry = appendEntry(db, {
   417	      mutation_type: 'reindex',
   418	      reason: DIVERGENCE_RECONCILE_ESCALATION_REASON,
   419	      prior_hash: null,
   420	      new_hash: computeHash(`${normalizedPath}|escalated|attempts:${policy.attemptsSoFar}|max:${policy.maxRetries}`),

   161	/**
   162	 * Runs reconsolidation when enabled and returns either an early tool response
   163	 * or a signal to continue the standard create-record path.
   164	 */
   165	export async function runReconsolidationIfEnabled(
   166	  database: BetterSqlite3.Database,
   167	  parsed: ReturnType<typeof memoryParser.parseMemoryFile>,
   168	  filePath: string,
   169	  force: boolean,
   170	  embedding: Float32Array | null,
   171	  scope?: { tenantId?: string | null; userId?: string | null; agentId?: string | null; sessionId?: string | null; sharedSpaceId?: string | null },
   172	): Promise<ReconsolidationBridgeResult> {
   173	  // BUG-2 fix: Track reconsolidation warnings for structured MCP response (not just console.warn)
   174	  const reconWarnings = [] as ReconWarningList;
   175	
   176	  // T-04: search-flags.ts is the canonical caller-visible opt-in gate.
   177	  // Reconsolidation.ts keeps an internal guard as a defensive fallback for
   178	  // Direct callers and future entry points.
   179	  if (!force && isReconsolidationFlagEnabled() && embedding) {
   180	    try {
   181	      const hasCheckpoint = hasReconsolidationCheckpoint(database, parsed.specFolder);
   182	      if (!hasCheckpoint) {
   183	        const reconMsg = 'TM-06: reconsolidation skipped — create checkpoint "pre-reconsolidation" first';
   184	        console.warn(`[memory-save] ${reconMsg}`);
   185	        reconWarnings.push(reconMsg);
   186	        // Continue normal create path without reconsolidation.
   187	      } else {
   188	        const reconResult: ReconsolidationResult | null = await reconsolidate(
   189	          {
   190	            title: parsed.title,
   191	            content: parsed.content,
   192	            specFolder: parsed.specFolder,
   193	            filePath,
   194	            embedding,
   195	            triggerPhrases: parsed.triggerPhrases,
   196	            importanceTier: parsed.importanceTier,
   197	          },
   198	          database,
   199	          {
   200	            findSimilar: (emb, opts) => {
   201	              const searchEmb = emb instanceof Float32Array ? emb : new Float32Array(emb as number[]);
   202	              const results = vectorIndex.vectorSearch(searchEmb, {
   203	                limit: (opts.limit ?? 3) * 3, // Over-fetch for post-scope-filter
   204	                specFolder: opts.specFolder,
   205	                minSimilarity: 50,
   206	                includeConstitutional: false,
   207	              });
   208	              // Post-filter by governance scope to prevent cross-tenant reconsolidation
   209	              const scopeFiltered = results.filter((r: Record<string, unknown>) => {
   210	                if (scope?.tenantId && r.tenant_id && r.tenant_id !== scope.tenantId) return false;
   211	                if (scope?.userId && r.user_id && r.user_id !== scope.userId) return false;
   212	                if (scope?.agentId && r.agent_id && r.agent_id !== scope.agentId) return false;
   213	                if (scope?.sharedSpaceId && r.shared_space_id && r.shared_space_id !== scope.sharedSpaceId) return false;
   214	                return true;
   215	              }).slice(0, opts.limit ?? 3);
   216	              return scopeFiltered.map((r: Record<string, unknown>) => ({
   217	                id: typeof r.id === 'number' ? r.id : 0,
   218	                file_path: typeof r.file_path === 'string' ? r.file_path : '',
   219	                title: typeof r.title === 'string' ? r.title : null,
   220	                content_text: typeof r.content_text === 'string' ? r.content_text : (typeof r.content === 'string' ? r.content : null),
   221	                similarity: (typeof r.similarity === 'number' ? r.similarity : 0) / 100,
   222	                spec_folder: parsed.specFolder,
   223	                importance_weight: typeof r.importance_weight === 'number'
   224	                  ? r.importance_weight
   225	                  : 0.5,
   226	              }));
   227	            },
   228	            storeMemory: (memory) => {
   229	              const importanceWeight = calculateDocumentWeight(filePath, parsed.documentType);
   230	              const callbackSpecLevel = isSpecDocumentType(parsed.documentType)
   231	                ? detectSpecLevelFromParsed(memory.filePath)
   232	                : null;
   233	              const memoryEncodingIntent = isEncodingIntentEnabled()
   234	                ? classifyEncodingIntent(memory.content)
   235	                : undefined;
   236	
   237	              // P1-01 fix — wrap all DB writes (index, metadata, BM25, history) in a
   238	              // Single transaction for atomicity. better-sqlite3 supports nested transactions
   239	              // Via savepoints, so this is safe even if indexMemory uses its own transaction.
   240	              const fileMetadata = incrementalIndex.getFileMetadata(memory.filePath);
   241	              const fileMtimeMs = fileMetadata ? fileMetadata.mtime : null;
   242	
   243	              const memoryId = database.transaction(() => {
   244	                const id = vectorIndex.indexMemory({
   245	                  specFolder: memory.specFolder,
   246	                  filePath: memory.filePath,
   247	                  title: memory.title,
   248	                  triggerPhrases: memory.triggerPhrases ?? [],
   249	                  importanceWeight,
   250	                  embedding: memory.embedding as Float32Array,
   251	                  encodingIntent: memoryEncodingIntent,
   252	                  documentType: parsed.documentType || 'memory',
   253	                  specLevel: callbackSpecLevel,
   254	                  contentText: memory.content,
   255	                  qualityScore: parsed.qualityScore,
   256	                  qualityFlags: parsed.qualityFlags,
   257	                });
   258	
   259	                applyPostInsertMetadata(database, id, {
   260	                  content_hash: parsed.contentHash,
   261	                  context_type: parsed.contextType,
   262	                  importance_tier: parsed.importanceTier,
   263	                  memory_type: parsed.memoryType,
   264	                  type_inference_source: parsed.memoryTypeSource,
   265	                  stability: fsrsScheduler.DEFAULT_INITIAL_STABILITY,
   266	                  difficulty: fsrsScheduler.DEFAULT_INITIAL_DIFFICULTY,
   267	                  file_mtime_ms: fileMtimeMs,
   268	                  encoding_intent: memoryEncodingIntent,
   269	                  document_type: parsed.documentType || 'memory',
   270	                  spec_level: callbackSpecLevel,
   271	                  quality_score: parsed.qualityScore ?? 0,
   272	                  quality_flags: JSON.stringify(parsed.qualityFlags ?? []),
   273	                });
   274	
   275	                if (bm25Index.isBm25Enabled()) {
   276	                  try {
   277	                    const bm25 = bm25Index.getIndex();
   278	                    bm25.addDocument(String(id), bm25Index.buildBm25DocumentText({
   279	                      title: memory.title,
   280	                      content_text: memory.content,
   281	                      trigger_phrases: memory.triggerPhrases ?? [],
   282	                      file_path: memory.filePath,
   283	                    }));
   284	                  } catch (bm25Err: unknown) {
   285	                    const message = toErrorMessage(bm25Err);
   286	                    console.warn(`[memory-save] BM25 indexing failed (recon conflict store): ${message}`);
   287	                    const bm25Warning = repairBm25Document({
   288	                      memoryId: id,
   289	                      title: memory.title,
   290	                      contentText: memory.content,
   291	                      triggerPhrases: memory.triggerPhrases ?? [],
   292	                      filePath: memory.filePath,
   293	                      contextLabel: 'recon conflict store',
   294	                    });
   295	                    if (bm25Warning) {
   296	                      reconWarnings.push(bm25Warning);
   297	                    }
   298	                  }
   299	                }
   300	
   301	                recordHistory(id, 'ADD', null, memory.title ?? memory.filePath ?? null, 'mcp:memory_save');
   302	
   303	                return id;
   304	              })();
   305	
   306	              return memoryId;
   307	            },
   308	            generateEmbedding: async (content: string) => {
   309	              return embeddings.generateDocumentEmbedding(content);
   310	            },
   311	          }
   312	        );
   313	
   314	        if (reconResult && reconResult.action !== 'complement') {
   315	          // Reconsolidation handled the memory (merge or conflict) — skip normal CREATE path
   316	          console.error(`[memory-save] TM-06: Reconsolidation ${reconResult.action} for ${path.basename(filePath)}`);
   317	
   318	          const reconId = reconResult.newMemoryId;
   319	
   320	          const ledgerRecorded = appendMutationLedgerSafe(database, {
   321	            mutationType: 'update',
   322	            reason: `memory_save: reconsolidation ${reconResult.action}`,
   323	            priorHash: null,
   324	            newHash: parsed.contentHash,
   325	            linkedMemoryIds: reconResult.action === 'conflict'
   326	              ? [reconResult.newMemoryId, reconResult.existingMemoryId]
   327	              : [reconId],
   328	            decisionMeta: {
   329	              tool: 'memory_save',
   330	              action: `reconsolidation_${reconResult.action}`,
   331	              similarity: reconResult.similarity,
   332	              specFolder: parsed.specFolder,
   333	              filePath,
   334	            },
   335	            actor: 'mcp:memory_save',
   336	          });
   337	          const earlyReturnWarnings = [
   338	            ...reconWarnings,
   339	            ...(reconResult.warnings ?? []),
   340	            ...(!ledgerRecorded ? ['Mutation ledger append failed; audit trail may be incomplete.'] : []),
   341	          ];
   342	
   343	          return {
   344	            earlyReturn: {
   345	              status: reconResult.action === 'merge' ? 'merged' : 'superseded',
   346	              id: reconId,
   347	              specFolder: parsed.specFolder,
   348	              title: parsed.title ?? '',
   349	              reconsolidation: reconResult,
   350	              message: `Reconsolidation: ${reconResult.action} (similarity: ${reconResult.similarity?.toFixed(3) ?? 'N/A'})`,
   351	              warnings: earlyReturnWarnings.length > 0 ? earlyReturnWarnings : undefined,
   352	            },
   353	            warnings: reconWarnings,
   354	          };
   355	        }
   356	        // ReconResult is null or complement — fall through to normal CREATE path
   357	      }
   358	    } catch (reconErr: unknown) {
   359	      const message = toErrorMessage(reconErr);
   360	      console.warn(`[memory-save] TM-06: Reconsolidation error (proceeding with normal save): ${message}`);
   361	      // Reconsolidation errors must not block saves
   362	    }
   363	  }
   364	
   365	  // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   366	  // ASSISTIVE RECONSOLIDATION (REQ-D4-005)
   367	  // Runs independently of the TM-06 flag.  Requires embedding.
   368	  // Does NOT block normal save — all actions are advisory or
   369	  // shadow-only (auto-merge at >= 0.96 only archives old record).
   370	  // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   371	  let assistiveRecommendation: AssistiveRecommendation | null = null;
   372	
   373	  if (!force && isAssistiveReconsolidationEnabled() && embedding) {
   374	    try {
   375	      // Find the top similar memory using the existing vector search
   376	      const searchEmb = embedding instanceof Float32Array ? embedding : new Float32Array(embedding as number[]);
   377	      const candidateResults = vectorIndex.vectorSearch(searchEmb, {
   378	        limit: 3,
   379	        specFolder: parsed.specFolder,
   380	        minSimilarity: Math.round(ASSISTIVE_REVIEW_THRESHOLD * 100), // convert to 0-100 scale
   381	        includeConstitutional: false,
   382	      });
   383	
   384	      if (candidateResults.length > 0) {
   385	        const top = candidateResults[0] as Record<string, unknown>;
   386	        // vectorSearch returns similarity in [0, 100], normalise to [0, 1]
   387	        const rawSimilarity = typeof top.similarity === 'number' ? top.similarity : 0;
   388	        const similarity = rawSimilarity > 1 ? rawSimilarity / 100 : rawSimilarity;
   389	        const topId = typeof top.id === 'number' ? top.id : 0;
   390	        const topContent = typeof top.content_text === 'string' ? top.content_text :
   391	                          (typeof top.content === 'string' ? top.content : '');
   392	
   393	        const tier = classifyAssistiveSimilarity(similarity);
   394	
   395	        if (tier === 'auto_merge') {
   396	          // Auto-merge: archive the older memory record (shadow operation —
   397	          // we mark is_archived so it is excluded from future search results
   398	          // but NOT physically deleted).
   399	          try {
   400	            database.prepare(`
   401	              UPDATE memory_index
   402	              SET is_archived = 1,
   403	                  updated_at = datetime('now')
   404	              WHERE id = ?
   405	            `).run(topId);
   406	            if (bm25Index.isBm25Enabled()) {
   407	              bm25Index.getIndex().removeDocument(String(topId));
   408	            }
   409	            console.warn(
   410	              `[reconsolidation-bridge] assistive auto-merge: archived older=${topId} ` +
   411	              `(similarity=${similarity.toFixed(3)}) — newer memory continues normal save`
   412	            );
   413	          } catch (mergeErr: unknown) {
   414	            const msg = mergeErr instanceof Error ? mergeErr.message : String(mergeErr);
   415	            console.warn(`[reconsolidation-bridge] assistive auto-merge archive failed: ${msg}`);
   416	          }
   417	        } else if (tier === 'review') {
   418	          // Review tier: classify and surface as recommendation (no mutations)
   419	          const classification = classifySupersededOrComplement(topContent, parsed.content);
   420	          assistiveRecommendation = {
   421	            action: 'review',
   422	            candidateMemoryIds: [topId],
   423	            description:
   424	              `Review borderline similarity ${similarity.toFixed(3)} between the pending save ` +
   425	              `and existing memory #${topId}; heuristic suggests ${classification}.`,
   426	            olderMemoryId: topId,
   427	            newerMemoryId: null,
   428	            similarity,
   429	            classification,
   430	            recommendedAt: Date.now(),
   431	          };
   432	          // Preserve the advisory payload on the warning carrier so the normal save
   433	          // path can forward it without widening the handler signature.
   434	          reconWarnings.assistiveRecommendation = assistiveRecommendation;
   435	          logAssistiveRecommendation(assistiveRecommendation);
   436	        }
   437	        // 'keep_separate' → no action, fall through to normal save
   438	      }
   439	    } catch (assistiveErr: unknown) {
   440	      const message = toErrorMessage(assistiveErr);
   441	      console.warn(
   442	        `[reconsolidation-bridge] assistive reconsolidation error (proceeding with normal save): ${message}`
   443	      );
   444	      // Errors must not block saves
   445	    }
   446	  }
   447	
   448	  return {
   449	    earlyReturn: null,
   450	    warnings: reconWarnings,
   451	    assistiveRecommendation,
   452	  };
   453	}

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     2	// MODULE: Reconsolidation
     3	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     4	// TM-06: Reconsolidation-on-Save
     5	//
     6	// After embedding generation, check top-3 most similar memories
     7	// In the spec folder:
     8	// - similarity in [0.88, 1.0]: MERGE (duplicate — merge content, boost importance_weight)
     9	// - similarity in [0.75, 0.88): CONFLICT (supersede prior memory via causal 'supersedes' edge)
    10	// - similarity in [0, 0.75): COMPLEMENT (store new memory unchanged)
    11	// Note: cosine similarity, normalized [0,1]. Thresholds defined by MERGE_THRESHOLD and CONFLICT_THRESHOLD constants.
    12	//
    13	// Behind SPECKIT_RECONSOLIDATION opt-in flag (default OFF)
    14	// REQUIRES: checkpoint created before first enable
    15	import { createHash } from 'crypto';
    16	import type Database from 'better-sqlite3';
    17	import { recordHistory } from './history.js';
    18	import * as causalEdges from './causal-edges.js';
    19	import * as bm25Index from '../search/bm25-index.js';

    22	import { getCanonicalPathKey } from '../utils/canonical-path.js';
    23	import { delete_memory_from_database } from '../search/vector-index-mutations.js';
