    25	import {
    26	  applyPostInsertMetadata,
    27	  type PostInsertMetadataFields,
    28	} from './post-insert-metadata.js';
    29	
    30	// Feature catalog: Reconsolidation-on-save
    31	
    32	
    33	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    34	// 1. TYPES
    35	
    36	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    37	/** Action determined by similarity threshold comparison */
    38	export type ReconsolidationAction = 'merge' | 'conflict' | 'complement';
    39	
    40	/** An existing memory found during similarity search */
    41	export interface SimilarMemory {
    42	  id: number;
    43	  file_path: string;
    44	  title: string | null;
    45	  content_text: string | null;
    46	  similarity: number;
    47	  spec_folder: string;
    48	  importance_weight?: number;
    49	  [key: string]: unknown;
    50	}
    51	
    52	/** A new memory to be reconsolidated */
    53	export interface NewMemoryData {
    54	  id?: number;
    55	  title: string | null;
    56	  content: string;
    57	  specFolder: string;
    58	  filePath: string;
    59	  embedding: Float32Array | number[];
    60	  triggerPhrases?: string[];
    61	  importanceTier?: string;
    62	  importanceWeight?: number;
    63	}
    64	
    65	/** Result of a merge operation */
    66	export interface MergeResult {
    67	  action: 'merge';
    68	  existingMemoryId: number;
    69	  newMemoryId: number;
    70	  importanceWeight: number;
    71	  mergedContentLength: number;
    72	  similarity: number;
    73	  warnings?: string[];
    74	}
    75	
    76	export type MergeAbortStatus = 'predecessor_changed' | 'predecessor_gone';
    77	
    78	/** Result when a merge candidate becomes stale before commit */
    79	export interface MergeAbortedResult {
    80	  action: 'complement';
    81	  status: MergeAbortStatus;
    82	  existingMemoryId: number;
    83	  newMemoryId: number;
    84	  similarity: number;
    85	  warnings?: string[];
    86	}
    87	
    88	/** Result of a conflict (supersede) operation */
    89	export interface ConflictResult {
    90	  action: 'conflict';
    91	  existingMemoryId: number;
    92	  newMemoryId: number;
    93	  causalEdgeId: number | null;
    94	  similarity: number;
    95	  warnings?: string[];
    96	}
    97	
    98	/** Result of a complement (new) operation */
    99	export interface ComplementResult {
   100	  action: 'complement';
   101	  /**
   102	   * Memory ID when already known by caller; 0 means "not persisted yet" and
   103	   * caller should execute normal create flow.
   104	   */
   105	  newMemoryId: number;
   106	  similarity: number | null;
   107	  warnings?: string[];
   108	}
   109	
   110	/** Combined reconsolidation result */
   111	export type ReconsolidationResult = MergeResult | MergeAbortedResult | ConflictResult | ComplementResult;
   112	
   113	/** Callback for finding similar memories by embedding */
   114	type FindSimilarFn = (
   115	  embedding: Float32Array | number[],
   116	  options: { limit: number; specFolder?: string }
   117	) => SimilarMemory[];
   118	
   119	/** Callback for storing a new memory (complement path) */
   120	type StoreMemoryFn = (memory: NewMemoryData) => number;
   121	
   122	/** Callback for generating an embedding from content */
   123	type GenerateEmbeddingFn = (content: string) => Promise<Float32Array | number[] | null>;
   124	
   125	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   126	// 2. CONFIGURATION
   127	
   128	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   129	/** Threshold above which memories are merged (near-duplicates) */
   130	const MERGE_THRESHOLD = 0.88;
   131	
   132	/** Threshold above which memories are in conflict (supersede) */
   133	const CONFLICT_THRESHOLD = 0.75;
   134	
   135	/** Maximum number of similar memories to check */
   136	const SIMILAR_MEMORY_LIMIT = 3;
   137	
   138	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   139	// 3. FEATURE FLAG
   140	
   141	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   142	// Reconsolidation gate — canonical implementation in search-flags.ts.
   143	// Default: ON (graduated). Set SPECKIT_RECONSOLIDATION=false to disable.
   144	import { isReconsolidationEnabled } from '../search/search-flags.js';
   145	export { isReconsolidationEnabled };
   146	
   147	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   148	// 4. SIMILARITY SEARCH
   149	
   150	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   151	/**
   152	 * Find the top-N most similar memories in a spec folder.
   153	 *
   154	 * @param embedding - The embedding vector to compare against
   155	 * @param specFolder - The spec folder to search within
   156	 * @param findSimilar - Callback to find similar memories by embedding
   157	 * @param limit - Maximum number of results (default: 3)
   158	 * @returns Array of similar memories sorted by similarity DESC
   159	 */
   160	export function findSimilarMemories(
   161	  embedding: Float32Array | number[],
   162	  specFolder: string,
   163	  findSimilar: FindSimilarFn,
   164	  limit: number = SIMILAR_MEMORY_LIMIT
   165	): SimilarMemory[] {
   166	  try {
   167	    return findSimilar(embedding, { limit, specFolder });
   168	  } catch (err: unknown) {
   169	    const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);
   170	    console.warn('[reconsolidation] findSimilarMemories error:', message);
   171	    return [];
   172	  }
   173	}
   174	
   175	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   176	// 5. ACTION DETERMINATION
   177	
   178	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   179	/**
   180	 * Determine the reconsolidation action based on similarity score.
   181	 *
   182	 * @param similarity - Cosine similarity between new and existing memory
   183	 * @returns The action to take: 'merge', 'conflict', or 'complement'
   184	 */
   185	export function determineAction(similarity: number): ReconsolidationAction {
   186	  if (similarity >= MERGE_THRESHOLD) {
   187	    return 'merge';
   188	  }
   189	  if (similarity >= CONFLICT_THRESHOLD) {
   190	    return 'conflict';
   191	  }
   192	  return 'complement';
   193	}
   194	
   195	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   196	// 6. MERGE OPERATION
   197	
   198	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   199	/**
   200	 * Merge a new memory into an existing one (similarity >= 0.88).
   201	 *
   202	 * Combines content by appending new unique sections to the existing
   203	 * memory, boosts the importance_weight, and updates the embedding
   204	 * to reflect the merged content.
   205	 *
   206	 * @param existingMemory - The existing memory to merge into
   207	 * @param newMemory - The new memory being saved
   208	 * @param db - The database instance
   209	 * @param generateEmbedding - Optional callback to regenerate embedding for merged content
   210	 * @returns MergeResult when merged, or a complement-style abort result when the predecessor changed
   211	 */
   212	export async function executeMerge(
   213	  existingMemory: SimilarMemory,
   214	  newMemory: NewMemoryData,
   215	  db: Database.Database,
   216	  generateEmbedding?: GenerateEmbeddingFn | null
   217	): Promise<MergeResult | MergeAbortedResult> {
   218	  const newContent = newMemory.content;
   219	  let newId = 0;
   220	  let bm25RepairWarning: string | null = null;
   221	
   222	  try {
   223	    const memoryIndexColumns = getTableColumns(db, 'memory_index');
   224	    ensureBm25RepairFlagColumn(db, memoryIndexColumns);
   225	    const existingRow = db.prepare('SELECT * FROM memory_index WHERE id = ?').get(existingMemory.id) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;
   226	
   227	    if (!existingRow || isArchivedRow(existingRow)) {
   228	      return buildMergeAbortResult(existingMemory, newMemory, 'predecessor_gone');
   229	    }
   230	
   231	    const predecessorVersion = capturePredecessorVersion(existingRow);
   232	    const existingContent = getOptionalString(existingRow, 'content_text') ?? '';
   233	    const mergedContent = mergeContent(existingContent, newContent);
   234	    const mergedHash = createHash('sha256').update(mergedContent, 'utf-8').digest('hex');
   235	    const currentWeight = getOptionalNumber(existingRow, 'importance_weight') ?? existingMemory.importance_weight ?? 0.5;
   236	    const boostedWeight = Math.min(1.0, currentWeight + 0.1);
   237	    const now = new Date().toISOString();
   238	
   239	    // Generate embedding BEFORE transaction (async I/O cannot run inside
   240	    // Better-sqlite3's synchronous transaction callback).
===== B =====
   240	    // Better-sqlite3's synchronous transaction callback).
   241	    let newEmbedding: Float32Array | number[] | null = null;
   242	    if (generateEmbedding) {
   243	      try {
   244	        newEmbedding = await generateEmbedding(mergedContent);
   245	      } catch (embErr: unknown) {
   246	        const msg = embErr instanceof Error ? embErr.message : String(embErr);
   247	        console.warn('[reconsolidation] Failed to regenerate embedding for merge:', msg);
   248	        // Non-fatal: merged content is stored even without updated embedding
   249	      }
   250	    }
   251	
   252	    let bm25RepairNeeded = false;
   253	    let mergedBm25DocumentText = '';
   254	
   255	    // F04-001: Append-only merge — mark old as superseded, create new record
   256	    const txResult = db.transaction((): { status: 'merged' } | { status: MergeAbortStatus } => {
   257	      const currentRow = db.prepare('SELECT * FROM memory_index WHERE id = ?').get(existingMemory.id) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;
   258	
   259	      if (!currentRow || isArchivedRow(currentRow)) {
   260	        return { status: 'predecessor_gone' };
   261	      }
   262	
   263	      if (hasPredecessorChanged(predecessorVersion, currentRow)) {
   264	        return { status: 'predecessor_changed' };
   265	      }
   266	
   267	      const currentFilePath = getOptionalString(currentRow, 'file_path') ?? existingMemory.file_path;
   268	      const reusedEmbeddingRow = db.prepare(`
   269	        SELECT embedding
   270	        FROM vec_memories
   271	        WHERE rowid = ?
   272	      `).get(existingMemory.id) as { embedding?: Buffer } | undefined;
   273	      const mergedEmbeddingBuffer = newEmbedding
   274	        ? embeddingToBuffer(newEmbedding)
   275	        : (reusedEmbeddingRow?.embedding ?? null);
   276	      const mergedEmbeddingStatus = mergedEmbeddingBuffer ? 'success' : getOptionalString(currentRow, 'embedding_status') ?? 'pending';
   277	      const mergedCanonicalPath = getOptionalString(currentRow, 'canonical_file_path') ?? getCanonicalPathKey(currentFilePath);
   278	      const mergedTriggerPhrases = buildMergedTriggerPhrases(currentRow, newMemory.triggerPhrases);
   279	      const mergedTitle = newMemory.title ?? getOptionalString(currentRow, 'title') ?? existingMemory.title ?? '';
   280	      const mergedImportanceTier = newMemory.importanceTier ?? getOptionalString(currentRow, 'importance_tier');
   281	
   282	      mergedBm25DocumentText = bm25Index.buildBm25DocumentText({
   283	        title: mergedTitle,
   284	        content_text: mergedContent,
   285	        trigger_phrases: mergedTriggerPhrases,
   286	        file_path: currentFilePath,
   287	      });
   288	
   289	      // Mark existing memory as archived (superseded)
   290	      db.prepare(`
   291	        UPDATE memory_index
   292	        SET is_archived = 1,
   293	            updated_at = datetime('now')
   294	        WHERE id = ?
   295	      `).run(existingMemory.id);
   296	
   297	      const insertValues = buildMergedMemoryInsertValues(currentRow, {
   298	        spec_folder: existingMemory.spec_folder,
   299	        file_path: currentFilePath,
   300	        canonical_file_path: mergedCanonicalPath,
   301	        anchor_id: getOptionalString(currentRow, 'anchor_id'),
   302	        title: mergedTitle,
   303	        trigger_phrases: mergedTriggerPhrases,
   304	        importance_weight: boostedWeight,
   305	        importance_tier: mergedImportanceTier,
   306	        content_text: mergedContent,
   307	        content_hash: mergedHash,
   308	        embedding_status: mergedEmbeddingStatus,
   309	        embedding_model: getOptionalString(currentRow, 'embedding_model'),
   310	        embedding_generated_at: mergedEmbeddingBuffer ? now : getOptionalString(currentRow, 'embedding_generated_at'),
   311	        encoding_intent: getOptionalString(currentRow, 'encoding_intent'),
   312	        document_type: getOptionalString(currentRow, 'document_type'),
   313	        spec_level: getOptionalNumber(currentRow, 'spec_level'),
   314	        bm25_repair_needed: 0,
   315	        created_at: now,
   316	        updated_at: now,
   317	      }, memoryIndexColumns);
   318	      const insertColumns = Object.keys(insertValues);
   319	      const insertSql = `
   320	        INSERT INTO memory_index (${insertColumns.join(', ')})
   321	        VALUES (${insertColumns.map(() => '?').join(', ')})
   322	      `;
   323	      const insertResult = db.prepare(insertSql).run(...insertColumns.map((column) => insertValues[column]));
   324	
   325	      newId = Number(insertResult.lastInsertRowid);
   326	
   327	      if (mergedEmbeddingBuffer) {
   328	        db.prepare(`
   329	          INSERT INTO vec_memories (rowid, embedding)
   330	          VALUES (?, ?)
   331	        `).run(newId, mergedEmbeddingBuffer);
   332	      }
   333	
   334	      const postInsertMetadata = buildMergePostInsertMetadata(currentRow, {
   335	        content_hash: mergedHash,
   336	        importance_tier: mergedImportanceTier,
   337	        embedding_status: mergedEmbeddingStatus,
   338	        document_type: getOptionalString(currentRow, 'document_type'),
   339	        spec_level: getOptionalNumber(currentRow, 'spec_level'),
   340	      }, memoryIndexColumns);
   341	
   342	      if (Object.keys(postInsertMetadata).length > 0) {
   343	        applyPostInsertMetadata(db, newId, postInsertMetadata);
   344	      }
   345	
   346	      // Create supersedes causal edge
   347	      db.prepare(`
   348	        INSERT OR IGNORE INTO causal_edges (source_id, target_id, relation, strength, extracted_at)
   349	        VALUES (?, ?, 'supersedes', 1.0, datetime('now'))
   350	      `).run(newId, existingMemory.id);
   351	
   352	      recordLineageTransition(db, newId, {
   353	        actor: 'mcp:reconsolidation',
   354	        predecessorMemoryId: existingMemory.id,
   355	        transitionEvent: 'SUPERSEDE',
   356	      });
   357	
   358	      if (bm25Index.isBm25Enabled()) {
   359	        try {
   360	          const bm25 = bm25Index.getIndex();
   361	          bm25.removeDocument(String(existingMemory.id));
   362	          bm25.addDocument(String(newId), mergedBm25DocumentText);
   363	        } catch (bm25Err: unknown) {
   364	          const message = bm25Err instanceof Error ? bm25Err.message : String(bm25Err);
   365	          console.warn('[reconsolidation] Failed to update BM25 index for merge:', message);
   366	          bm25RepairNeeded = true;
   367	        }
   368	      }
   369	
   370	      refresh_interference_scores_for_folder(db, existingMemory.spec_folder);
   371	      clear_search_cache();
   372	
   373	      try {
   374	        recordHistory(newId, 'ADD', null, mergedTitle || existingMemory.file_path, 'mcp:reconsolidation');
   375	        recordHistory(
   376	          existingMemory.id,
   377	          'UPDATE',
   378	          existingMemory.title ?? existingMemory.file_path,
   379	          mergedTitle || existingMemory.file_path,
   380	          'mcp:reconsolidation',
   381	        );
   382	      } catch (_historyErr: unknown) {
   383	        // Best-effort history tracking during reconsolidation merge
   384	      }
   385	      return { status: 'merged' };
   386	    })();
   387	
   388	    if (txResult.status !== 'merged') {
   389	      return buildMergeAbortResult(existingMemory, newMemory, txResult.status);
   390	    }
   391	
   392	    if (bm25RepairNeeded) {
   393	      const repairResult = repairBm25Document({
   394	        previousMemoryId: existingMemory.id,
   395	        memoryId: newId,
   396	        documentText: mergedBm25DocumentText,
   397	      });
   398	      if (!repairResult.success) {
   399	        setBm25RepairNeededFlag(db, memoryIndexColumns, newId, true);
   400	        bm25RepairWarning =
   401	          `BM25 repair failed after reconsolidation merge for memory ${newId}: ${repairResult.error}`;
   402	      } else {
   403	        setBm25RepairNeededFlag(db, memoryIndexColumns, newId, false);
   404	      }
   405	    }
   406	
   407	    return {
   408	      action: 'merge',
   409	      existingMemoryId: existingMemory.id,
   410	      newMemoryId: newId,
   411	      importanceWeight: boostedWeight,
   412	      mergedContentLength: mergedContent.length,
   413	      similarity: existingMemory.similarity,
   414	      warnings: bm25RepairWarning ? [bm25RepairWarning] : undefined,
   415	    };
   416	  } catch (err: unknown) {
   417	    const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);
   418	    throw new Error(`Merge operation failed: ${message}`);
   419	  }
   420	}
===== C =====
   420	}
   421	
   422	/**
   423	 * Merge two content strings by appending unique new lines.
   424	 *
   425	 * Splits both contents into lines, then appends lines from the new
   426	 * content that are not present in the existing content.
   427	 *
   428	 * @param existing - The existing memory content
   429	 * @param incoming - The new memory content
   430	 * @returns The merged content string
   431	 */
   432	export function mergeContent(existing: string, incoming: string): string {
   433	  if (!existing || existing.trim().length === 0) {
   434	    return incoming;
   435	  }
   436	  if (!incoming || incoming.trim().length === 0) {
   437	    return existing;
   438	  }
   439	
   440	  const existingLines = new Set(
   441	    existing.split('\n').map(line => line.trim()).filter(line => line.length > 0)
   442	  );
   443	
   444	  const newLines = incoming
   445	    .split('\n')
   446	    .map(line => line.trim())
   447	    .filter(line => line.length > 0 && !existingLines.has(line));
   448	
   449	  if (newLines.length === 0) {
   450	    return existing;
   451	  }
   452	
   453	  return existing + '\n\n<!-- Merged content -->\n' + newLines.join('\n');
   454	}
   455	
   456	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   457	// 7. CONFLICT OPERATION
   458	
   459	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   460	/**
   461	 * Resolve a conflict between highly similar memories (similarity 0.75-0.88).
   462	 *
   463	 * Preferred path (when caller provides a distinct new memory ID):
   464	 * - Mark existing memory as deprecated (superseded)
   465	 * - Create a 'supersedes' causal edge from new -> existing
   466	 *
   467	 * Legacy fallback (when no new ID is available):
   468	 * - Update existing memory content/title in-place
   469	 * - Skip edge creation (avoids self-referential edges)
   470	 *
   471	 * @param existingMemory - The existing memory being superseded
   472	 * @param newMemory - The new memory replacing it
   473	 * @param db - The database instance
   474	 * @returns ConflictResult with supersede details
   475	 */
   476	export function executeConflict(
   477	  existingMemory: SimilarMemory,
   478	  newMemory: NewMemoryData,
   479	  db: Database.Database
   480	): ConflictResult {
   481	  try {
   482	    // Add causal 'supersedes' edge only when caller provides a distinct new ID.
   483	    // Prevent self-referential supersedes edges (source == target).
   484	    let edgeId: number | null = null;
   485	    const hasDistinctNewId =
   486	      typeof newMemory.id === 'number' &&
   487	      Number.isFinite(newMemory.id) &&
   488	      newMemory.id !== existingMemory.id;
   489	
   490	    if (hasDistinctNewId) {
   491	      // Atomic transaction: deprecate + edge must succeed or fail together.
   492	      // Without this, a failed insertEdge leaves an orphaned deprecation.
   493	      db.transaction(() => {
   494	        const updateResult = db.prepare(`
   495	          UPDATE memory_index
   496	          SET importance_tier = 'deprecated',
   497	              updated_at = datetime('now')
   498	          WHERE id = ?
   499	        `).run(existingMemory.id) as { changes: number };
   500	
   501	        if (updateResult.changes === 0) {
   502	          console.warn('[reconsolidation] Deprecate target not found, skipping edge insert');
   503	          return;
   504	        }
   505	
   506	        const sourceId = String(newMemory.id);
   507	        const targetId = String(existingMemory.id);
   508	        edgeId = causalEdges.insertEdge(
   509	          sourceId,
   510	          targetId,
   511	          'supersedes',
   512	          1.0,
   513	          `TM-06 reconsolidation conflict: similarity ${(existingMemory.similarity * 100).toFixed(1)}%`
   514	        );
   515	        if (edgeId == null) {
   516	          throw new Error(
   517	            `Failed to insert supersedes edge (${sourceId} -> ${targetId}) — aborting reconsolidation`
   518	          );
   519	        }
   520	      })();
   521	    } else {
   522	      // Atomic transaction: content + embedding + hash update together.
   523	      const updatedHash = createHash('sha256').update(newMemory.content, 'utf-8').digest('hex');
   524	      db.transaction(() => {
   525	        db.prepare(`
   526	          UPDATE memory_index
   527	          SET content_text = ?,
   528	              title = ?,
   529	              content_hash = ?,
   530	              updated_at = datetime('now')
   531	          WHERE id = ?
   532	        `).run(newMemory.content, newMemory.title, updatedHash, existingMemory.id);
   533	
   534	        if (newMemory.embedding) {
   535	          const buffer = embeddingToBuffer(newMemory.embedding);
   536	          db.prepare(
   537	            'UPDATE vec_memories SET embedding = ? WHERE rowid = ?'
   538	          ).run(buffer, existingMemory.id);
   539	        }
   540	      })();
   541	    }
   542	
   543	    return {
   544	      action: 'conflict',
   545	      existingMemoryId: existingMemory.id,
   546	      newMemoryId: newMemory.id ?? existingMemory.id,
   547	      causalEdgeId: edgeId,
   548	      similarity: existingMemory.similarity,
   549	    };
   550	  } catch (err: unknown) {
   551	    const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);
   552	    throw new Error(`Conflict operation failed: ${message}`);
   553	  }
   554	}
   555	
   556	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   557	// 8. COMPLEMENT OPERATION
   558	
   559	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   560	/**

     1	import chalk from "chalk";

     8	function askUser(question: string): Promise<string> {
     9	  const rl = createInterface({ input: process.stdin, output: process.stdout });
    10	  return new Promise((resolve) => {
    11	    rl.question(question, (answer) => {
    12	      rl.close();
    13	      resolve(answer.trim());
    14	    });

    18	function runClaudeInteractive(brief: string, cwd: string): boolean {
    19	  const result = spawnSync("claude", [brief], {
    20	    cwd,
    21	    stdio: "inherit",
    22	    timeout: 300_000,
    23	  });
    24	  return result.status === 0 || result.status === null;
    25	}

    27	type SyncMode = "interactive" | "prompts";
    28	
    29	/** Run targeted sync: detect → brief → AI → verify → ask → loop */
    30	export async function runSync(
    31	  config: MexConfig,
    32	  opts: { dryRun?: boolean; includeWarnings?: boolean }
    33	): Promise<void> {
    34	  let cycle = 0;
    35	  let mode: SyncMode | null = null;
    36	
    37	  while (true) {
    38	    cycle++;
    39	
    40	    // Step 1: Run drift check
    41	    if (cycle === 1) {
    42	      console.log(chalk.bold("Running drift check..."));
    43	    } else {
    44	      console.log(chalk.bold("\nRe-checking for remaining drift..."));
    45	    }
    46	
    47	    const report = await runDriftCheck(config);
    48	
    49	    if (report.issues.length === 0) {
    50	      console.log(chalk.green("✓ No drift detected. Everything is in sync."));
    51	      return;
    52	    }
    53	
    54	    console.log(
    55	      chalk.yellow(
    56	        `Found ${report.issues.length} issues (score: ${report.score}/100)`
    57	      )
    58	    );
    59	
    60	    // Step 2: Group issues by file
    61	    const relevantIssues = opts.includeWarnings
    62	      ? report.issues
    63	      : report.issues.filter((i) => {
    64	          const fileHasError = report.issues.some(
    65	            (other) => other.file === i.file && other.severity === "error"
    66	          );
    67	          return fileHasError;
    68	        });
    69	
    70	    if (relevantIssues.length === 0) {
    71	      console.log(
    72	        chalk.green(
    73	          "No errors found. Only warnings remain (use --warnings to include them)."
    74	        )
    75	      );
    76	      return;
    77	    }
    78	
    79	    const targets = groupIntoTargets(relevantIssues);
    80	
    81	    console.log(
    82	      chalk.bold(`\n${targets.length} file(s) need attention:\n`)
    83	    );
    84	
    85	    for (const target of targets) {
    86	      const errors = target.issues.filter(
    87	        (i) => i.severity === "error"
    88	      ).length;
    89	      const warnings = target.issues.filter(
    90	        (i) => i.severity === "warning"
    91	      ).length;
    92	      console.log(
    93	        `  ${target.file} — ${errors} errors, ${warnings} warnings`
    94	      );
    95	    }
    96	
    97	    // Dry run — show combined prompt and exit
    98	    if (opts.dryRun) {
    99	      console.log(
   100	        chalk.dim("\n--dry-run: showing prompt without executing\n")
   101	      );
   102	      const brief = await buildCombinedBrief(targets, config.projectRoot);
   103	      console.log(brief);
   104	      console.log();
   105	      return;
   106	    }
   107	
   108	    // Ask user for mode (only on first cycle)
   109	    if (mode === null) {
   110	      console.log(chalk.bold("\nHow should we fix these?"));
   111	      console.log();
   112	      console.log("  1) Interactive — Claude fixes with you watching (default)");
   113	      console.log("  2) Show prompts — I'll paste manually");
   114	      console.log("  3) Exit");
   115	      console.log();
   116	
   117	      const choice = await askUser("Choice [1-3] (default: 1): ");
   118	      const picked = choice || "1";
   119	
   120	      switch (picked) {
   121	        case "1":
   122	          mode = "interactive";
   123	          break;
   124	        case "2":
   125	          mode = "prompts";
   126	          break;
   127	        case "3":
   128	          console.log(chalk.dim("Exiting. Run mex sync again anytime."));
   129	          return;
   130	        default:
   131	          console.log(chalk.dim("Exiting."));
   132	          return;
   133	      }
   134	    }
   135	
   136	    // Show prompts mode — print combined prompt and exit
   137	    if (mode === "prompts") {
   138	      const brief = await buildCombinedBrief(targets, config.projectRoot);
   139	      console.log(brief);
   140	      console.log();
   141	      return;
   142	    }
   143	
   144	    // Step 3: Fix all files in one interactive session
   145	    console.log();
   146	    console.log(chalk.bold(`\nSending all ${targets.length} file(s) to Claude in one session...\n`));
   147	
   148	    const brief = await buildCombinedBrief(targets, config.projectRoot);
   149	    const ok = runClaudeInteractive(brief, config.projectRoot);
   150	
   151	    if (!ok) {
   152	      console.log(chalk.red("  ✗ Claude session failed"));
   153	    }
   154	
   155	    // Step 4: Verify
   156	    const postReport = await runDriftCheck(config);
   157	    const scoreDelta = postReport.score - report.score;
   158	    const deltaStr =
   159	      scoreDelta > 0
   160	        ? chalk.green(`+${scoreDelta}`)
   161	        : scoreDelta === 0
   162	          ? chalk.yellow("+0")
   163	          : chalk.red(`${scoreDelta}`);
   164	
   165	    console.log(
   166	      chalk.bold(
   167	        `\nDrift score: ${report.score} → ${postReport.score}/100 (${deltaStr})`
   168	      )
   169	    );
   170	
   171	    // Step 5: Check if we should continue
   172	    const remainingErrors = postReport.issues.filter(
   173	      (i) => i.severity === "error"
   174	    ).length;
   175	    const remainingWarnings = postReport.issues.filter(
   176	      (i) => i.severity === "warning"
   177	    ).length;
   178	
   179	    if (remainingErrors === 0 && !opts.includeWarnings) {
   180	      if (remainingWarnings > 0) {
   181	        console.log(
   182	          chalk.dim(
   183	            `${remainingWarnings} warning(s) remain (use --warnings to include them).`
   184	          )
   185	        );
   186	      } else {
   187	        console.log(chalk.green("✓ All issues resolved."));
   188	      }
   189	      return;
   190	    }
   191	
   192	    if (postReport.score === 100) {
   193	      console.log(chalk.green("✓ Perfect score. All issues resolved."));
   194	      return;
   195	    }
   196	
   197	    // Ask user whether to continue
   198	    const remaining = opts.includeWarnings
   199	      ? remainingErrors + remainingWarnings
   200	      : remainingErrors;
   201	
   202	    const answer = await askUser(
   203	      `\n${remaining} issue(s) remain. Run another cycle? [Y/n] `
   204	    );
   205	
   206	    if (answer.toLowerCase() === "n") {
   207	      console.log(chalk.dim("Stopped. Run mex sync again anytime."));
   208	      return;
   209	    }
   210	  }
   211	}
   212	
   213	function groupIntoTargets(issues: DriftIssue[]): SyncTarget[] {
   214	  const byFile = new Map<string, DriftIssue[]>();
   215	  for (const issue of issues) {
   216	    if (!byFile.has(issue.file)) byFile.set(issue.file, []);
   217	    byFile.get(issue.file)!.push(issue);
   218	  }
   219	
   220	  return Array.from(byFile.entries()).map(([file, issues]) => ({
   221	    file,
   222	    issues,
   223	    gitDiff: null,
   224	  }));
   225	}

   248	export function buildSaveResponse({ result, filePath, asyncEmbedding, requestId }: BuildSaveResponseParams): MCPResponse {
   249	  if (result.status === 'unchanged') {
   250	    return createMCPSuccessResponse({
   251	      tool: 'memory_save',
   252	      summary: 'Memory already indexed with same content',
   253	      data: {
   254	        status: 'unchanged',
   255	        id: result.id,
   256	        specFolder: result.specFolder,
   257	        title: result.title,
   258	      },
   259	      hints: ['Use force: true to re-index anyway'],
   260	    });
   261	  }
   262	
   263	  if (result.status === 'error') {
   264	    const errorMessage = typeof result.error === 'string' && result.error.length > 0
   265	      ? result.error
   266	      : (typeof result.message === 'string' && result.message.length > 0 ? result.message : 'Memory save failed');
   267	
   268	    return createMCPErrorResponse({
   269	      tool: 'memory_save',
   270	      error: errorMessage,
   271	      code: 'E081',
   272	      details: {
   273	        status: result.status,
   274	        id: result.id,
   275	        specFolder: result.specFolder,
   276	        title: result.title,
   277	        ...(typeof result.superseded === 'boolean' ? { superseded: result.superseded } : {}),
   278	      },
   279	    });
   280	  }
   281	
   282	  if (result.status === 'rejected') {
   283	    const isInsufficientContext = result.rejectionCode === MEMORY_SUFFICIENCY_REJECTION_CODE;
   284	    return createMCPSuccessResponse({
   285	      tool: 'memory_save',
   286	      summary: result.message ?? result.rejectionReason ?? 'Memory save rejected',
   287	      data: {
   288	        status: 'rejected',
   289	        id: result.id,
   290	        specFolder: result.specFolder,
   291	        title: result.title,
   292	        qualityScore: result.qualityScore,
   293	        qualityFlags: result.qualityFlags,
   294	        ...(result.rejectionCode ? { rejectionCode: result.rejectionCode } : {}),
   295	        rejectionReason: result.rejectionReason ?? result.message,
   296	        ...(result.sufficiency ? { sufficiency: result.sufficiency } : {}),
   297	        ...(result.qualityGate ? { qualityGate: result.qualityGate } : {}),
   298	        ...(result.warnings ? { warnings: result.warnings } : {}),
   299	        message: result.message ?? result.rejectionReason ?? 'Memory save rejected',
   300	      },
   301	      hints: isInsufficientContext
   302	        ? [
   303	            'Rejected saves do not mutate the memory index',
   304	            'Not enough context was available to save a durable memory',
   305	            'Add at least one concrete file, tool result, decision, blocker, next action, or outcome and retry',

   307	          ]
   308	        : ['Rejected saves do not mutate the memory index', 'Review quality issues and retry the save'],
   309	    });
   310	  }
   311	
   312	  const shouldEmitPostMutationFeedback = result.status !== 'duplicate' && result.status !== 'unchanged';
   313	  let postMutationFeedback: ReturnType<typeof buildMutationHookFeedback> | null = null;
   314	  if (shouldEmitPostMutationFeedback) {
   315	    let postMutationHooks: import('../mutation-hooks.js').MutationHookResult;
   316	    try {
   317	      postMutationHooks = runPostMutationHooks('save', { specFolder: result.specFolder, filePath });
   318	    } catch (hookError: unknown) {
   319	      const msg = hookError instanceof Error ? hookError.message : String(hookError);
   320	      postMutationHooks = {
   321	        latencyMs: 0, triggerCacheCleared: false,
   322	        constitutionalCacheCleared: false, toolCacheInvalidated: 0,

   326	    }
   327	    postMutationFeedback = buildMutationHookFeedback('save', postMutationHooks);
   328	  }
   329	
   330	  const response: Record<string, unknown> = {
   331	    status: result.status,
   332	    id: result.id,
   333	    specFolder: result.specFolder,
   334	    title: result.title,
   335	    triggerPhrases: result.triggerPhrases,
   336	    contextType: result.contextType,
   337	    importanceTier: result.importanceTier,
   338	    qualityScore: result.qualityScore,

# Iteration 006: MEMORY HYGIENE

## Focus
MEMORY HYGIENE: Deduplication, upserts, revision tracking, garbage collection, soft deletes, topic key stability.

## Findings
### Finding 1: Mex has no stable topic key beyond file paths and pattern filenames
- **Source**: [external/src/types.ts:119](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/types.ts#L119), [external/src/config.ts:27](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/config.ts#L27), [external/src/pattern/index.ts:11](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/pattern/index.ts#L11), [external/src/sync/index.ts:60](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/sync/index.ts#L60)
- **What it does**: Mex targets scaffold files by relative file path and pattern filename, groups sync work by `issue.file`, and rejects duplicate pattern names if the target file already exists. There is no source-level logical topic ID, alias table, or revision key in the runtime model.
- **Why it matters for us**: this is fine for a markdown scaffold, but weak for memory topics that survive renames, symlink aliases, file moves, or multiple observations of the same concept.
- **Recommendation**: reject
- **Impact**: high

### Finding 2: Mex revision tracking is policy text for the agent, not enforced state
- **Source**: [external/SYNC.md:44](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/SYNC.md#L44), [external/SYNC.md:51](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/SYNC.md#L51), [external/ROUTER.md:64](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/ROUTER.md#L64), [external/README.md:212](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/README.md#L212)
- **What it does**: Mex tells the agent to do surgical edits, preserve frontmatter, never delete old decisions, and mark changed decisions as “Superseded by …”. But that behavior lives in prompts/docs, not in a mutation log, version table, or append-only constraint.
- **Why it matters for us**: the idea is cheap and understandable for docs, but it cannot guarantee revision integrity under automation. It gives guidance, not durable provenance.
- **Recommendation**: prototype later
- **Impact**: high

### Finding 3: Mex does not implement soft delete or garbage collection; it only surfaces staleness and manual cleanup points
- **Source**: [external/src/drift/checkers/staleness.ts:4](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/checkers/staleness.ts#L4), [external/src/watch.ts:17](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/watch.ts#L17), [external/patterns/INDEX.md:21](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/patterns/INDEX.md#L21), [external/SYNC.md:39](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/SYNC.md#L39)
- **What it does**: Mex flags stale scaffold files by days and commit counts, prints drift after commits, and expects humans/agents to update or remove obsolete file/index entries manually. There is no tombstone field, archive flag, retention clock, or cleanup worker in the scaffold runtime.
- **Why it matters for us**: Mex helps you notice stale knowledge, but not manage its lifecycle safely. For memory hygiene, “warn and manually edit markdown” is much weaker than archival or retention policies.
- **Recommendation**: reject
- **Impact**: high

### Finding 4: Mex deduplication is structural and file-oriented, not content-oriented
- **Source**: [external/src/drift/index.ts:21](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/index.ts#L21), [external/src/drift/index.ts:108](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/index.ts#L108), [external/src/drift/checkers/index-sync.ts:41](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/checkers/index-sync.ts#L41), [external/src/pattern/index.ts:15](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/pattern/index.ts#L15)
- **What it does**: Mex deduplicates scaffold file discovery with `new Set(files)`, checks whether pattern files are missing from or orphaned in `INDEX.md`, and blocks duplicate pattern filenames. It does not compare memory content hashes, skip unchanged saves, or reconcile semantically duplicate observations.
- **Why it matters for us**: this is useful for keeping a doc scaffold tidy, but it is not a memory dedup pipeline. It won’t stop repeated facts from accumulating as new markdown edits.
- **Recommendation**: reject
- **Impact**: high

### Finding 5: Mex’s contradiction hygiene is useful, but bounded to extracted markdown claims
- **Source**: [external/src/drift/claims.ts:104](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/claims.ts#L104), [external/src/drift/checkers/dependency.ts:47](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/checkers/dependency.ts#L47), [external/src/drift/checkers/cross-file.ts:7](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/checkers/cross-file.ts#L7)
- **What it does**: Mex extracts dependency/version claims from markdown and then flags mismatches against manifests plus cross-file conflicts between claimed versions or package-manager styles.
- **Why it matters for us**: this is the strongest hygiene idea in Mex for this iteration. It is not revision tracking, but it is a cheap static contradiction lane we could add around docs/memory claims without touching the retrieval core.
- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
- **Impact**: medium

### Finding 6: Spec Kit Memory already implements the lifecycle primitives Mex is missing
- **Source**: [memory-save dedup.ts:229](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/save/dedup.ts#L229), [response-builder.ts:248](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/save/response-builder.ts#L248), [canonical-path.ts:18](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/utils/canonical-path.ts#L18), [mutation-ledger.ts:5](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/storage/mutation-ledger.ts#L5), [lineage-state.ts:407](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/storage/lineage-state.ts#L407), [lineage-state.ts:666](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/storage/lineage-state.ts#L666), [reconsolidation-bridge.ts:395](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/save/reconsolidation-bridge.ts#L395), [archival-manager.ts:192](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/cognitive/archival-manager.ts#L192), [tool-input-schemas.ts:179](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/schemas/tool-input-schemas.ts#L179)
- **What it does**: our system already has canonical path keys, unchanged-save short-circuits, duplicate content-hash rejection, append-only mutation ledger with DB triggers, lineage versions with logical keys and active projection upserts, soft archive via `is_archived`, and retention metadata like `retentionPolicy` and `deleteAfter`.
- **Why it matters for us**: Mex is not a hygiene upgrade for the memory store itself. The reusable slice is a cheap static markdown-claim checker, not the mutation model.
- **Recommendation**: reject
- **Impact**: high

## Sources Consulted

- [system-spec-kit/tool-input-schemas.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/schemas/tool-input-schemas.ts)

## Assessment
- New information ratio: 0.84
- Questions addressed: whether Mex has real dedup or upsert logic, whether it tracks revisions durably, whether it supports soft delete or garbage collection, how stable its topic identity is under path changes, and which hygiene ideas are actually reusable for Spec Kit Memory
- Questions answered: Mex is file-scaffold hygiene rather than memory-store hygiene; its revision behavior is mostly prompt-enforced; its dedup is structural rather than content-based; it has no soft-delete/retention lifecycle; the reusable piece is a cheap static claim-contradiction lane, not the lifecycle model

## Reflection
- What worked: reading `SYNC.md`, `pattern/index.ts`, `types.ts`, `sync/index.ts`, and the drift checkers side by side with our save/lineage/archival code made the boundary between scaffold maintenance and actual memory lifecycle very clear
- What did not work: semantic search was not usable in this run, so hidden cross-cutting discovery had to be done with direct file tracing instead of CocoIndex

## Recommended Next Focus
Investigate a narrowly scoped static “claim hygiene” lane for Spec Kit Memory: extract dependency/version/command/path claims from markdown memory/spec docs, run cheap contradiction and drift checks against manifests/filesystem, and surface that as advisory hygiene without replacing our canonical-path, dedup, lineage, archive, or retention machinery.

- **Impact**: high

### Finding 4: Mex deduplication is structural and file-oriented, not content-oriented
- **Source**: [external/src/drift/index.ts:21](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/index.ts#L21), [external/src/drift/index.ts:108](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/index.ts#L108), [external/src/drift/checkers/index-sync.ts:41](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/checkers/index-sync.ts#L41), [external/src/pattern/index.ts:15](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/pattern/index.ts#L15)
- **What it does**: Mex deduplicates scaffold file discovery with `new Set(files)`, checks whether pattern files are missing from or orphaned in `INDEX.md`, and blocks duplicate pattern filenames. It does not compare memory content hashes, skip unchanged saves, or reconcile semantically duplicate observations.
- **Why it matters for us**: this is useful for keeping a doc scaffold tidy, but it is not a memory dedup pipeline. It won’t stop repeated facts from accumulating as new markdown edits.
- **Recommendation**: reject
- **Impact**: high

### Finding 5: Mex’s contradiction hygiene is useful, but bounded to extracted markdown claims
- **Source**: [external/src/drift/claims.ts:104](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/claims.ts#L104), [external/src/drift/checkers/dependency.ts:47](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/checkers/dependency.ts#L47), [external/src/drift/checkers/cross-file.ts:7](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/checkers/cross-file.ts#L7)
- **What it does**: Mex extracts dependency/version claims from markdown and then flags mismatches against manifests plus cross-file conflicts between claimed versions or package-manager styles.
- **Why it matters for us**: this is the strongest hygiene idea in Mex for this iteration. It is not revision tracking, but it is a cheap static contradiction lane we could add around docs/memory claims without touching the retrieval core.
- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
- **Impact**: medium

### Finding 6: Spec Kit Memory already implements the lifecycle primitives Mex is missing
- **Source**: [memory-save dedup.ts:229](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/save/dedup.ts#L229), [response-builder.ts:248](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/save/response-builder.ts#L248), [canonical-path.ts:18](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/utils/canonical-path.ts#L18), [mutation-ledger.ts:5](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/storage/mutation-ledger.ts#L5), [lineage-state.ts:407](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/storage/lineage-state.ts#L407), [lineage-state.ts:666](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/storage/lineage-state.ts#L666), [reconsolidation-bridge.ts:395](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/save/reconsolidation-bridge.ts#L395), [archival-manager.ts:192](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/cognitive/archival-manager.ts#L192), [tool-input-schemas.ts:179](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/schemas/tool-input-schemas.ts#L179)
- **What it does**: our system already has canonical path keys, unchanged-save short-circuits, duplicate content-hash rejection, append-only mutation ledger with DB triggers, lineage versions with logical keys and active projection upserts, soft archive via `is_archived`, and retention metadata like `retentionPolicy` and `deleteAfter`.
- **Why it matters for us**: Mex is not a hygiene upgrade for the memory store itself. The reusable slice is a cheap static markdown-claim checker, not the mutation model.
- **Recommendation**: reject
- **Impact**: high

## Assessment
- New information ratio: 0.84
- Questions addressed: whether Mex has real dedup or upsert logic, whether it tracks revisions durably, whether it supports soft delete or garbage collection, how stable its topic identity is under path changes, and which hygiene ideas are actually reusable for Spec Kit Memory
- Questions answered: Mex is file-scaffold hygiene rather than memory-store hygiene; its revision behavior is mostly prompt-enforced; its dedup is structural rather than content-based; it has no soft-delete/retention lifecycle; the reusable piece is a cheap static claim-contradiction lane, not the lifecycle model

## Reflection
- What worked: reading `SYNC.md`, `pattern/index.ts`, `types.ts`, `sync/index.ts`, and the drift checkers side by side with our save/lineage/archival code made the boundary between scaffold maintenance and actual memory lifecycle very clear
- What did not work: semantic search was not usable in this run, so hidden cross-cutting discovery had to be done with direct file tracing instead of CocoIndex

## Recommended Next Focus
Investigate a narrowly scoped static “claim hygiene” lane for Spec Kit Memory: extract dependency/version/command/path claims from markdown memory/spec docs, run cheap contradiction and drift checks against manifests/filesystem, and surface that as advisory hygiene without replacing our canonical-path, dedup, lineage, archive, or retention machinery.
